target.title: fix(hooks): isolate slug-generator failures from shared auth profile (#71709)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #71709 reports that when the internal `generateSlugViaLLM` helper hits a transient HTTP 400 from the underlying provider (e.g. `LLM request rejected: You're out of extra usage` from `anthropic:claude-cli`), the failure is recorded against the shared auth-profile as `disabledReason: "billing"` with `disabledUntil = now + auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` (default 5h). Every subsequent agent that resolves to the same profile fails immediately with `Provider claude-cli has billing issue (skipping all models)` for the full 5h window, even though the underlying subscription is healthy and a direct curl against the same key returns 200. Multiple reporters confirm the same shape across Apr–May 2026 (MrMaturin 2026.4.26, nikolaykazakovvs-ux v2026.5.7 with a March repro `errorHash sha256:52c819aaad70...`). The long-term workaround in the wild is `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.llmSlug=false`.
- **Root Cause**: `runEmbeddedAgent` accepts an `authProfileFailurePolicy` field (`"shared" | "local"`). `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-profile-failure-policy.ts` short-circuits to `null` when `policy === "local"`, so the run's outcome never mutates shared profile health. The seam exists exactly for low-priority internal helpers whose outcomes do not represent profile reliability (same family as `format`, `server_error`, `empty_response`, and pre-start `timeout`, all already filtered globally). `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` builds a fire-and-forget `temp:slug-generator` session, fires one prompt, and returns a slug string or `null` — but the `runEmbeddedAgent` call omits the policy field, so any failure inside this helper is promoted to a profile-wide reliability signal and runs through the billing classifier as if it were a real user-facing run.
- **Fix**: Pass `authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"` from the slug-generator's `runEmbeddedAgent` call. The existing gate in `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` then drops any failure reason returned out of this helper (billing / auth / rate_limit / overloaded / etc.), so the user-facing main agent on the same profile is no longer blocked by an unrelated background slug attempt.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` — add `authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"` to the `runEmbeddedAgent` options, with a 2-line comment naming the contract and the bug.
  - `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts` — one new `it` block asserting that the slug helper passes `policy: "local"` into the embedded run.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Billing / auth / rate_limit classification logic — untouched. Real user-facing billing 402s on a shared profile still record correctly and still drive the documented backoff.
  - The policy gate (`resolveAuthProfileFailureReason`) — already covered by `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-profile-failure-policy.test.ts` (it already proves `policy: "local"` drops every recordable reason including `"billing"`); this PR exercises the existing semantics from one new caller.
  - Other `runEmbeddedAgent` call sites — `agent-runner-memory` (memory flush), `commitments/runtime` (extraction), `commands/models/list.probe` (auth probe), `crestodian/assistant`, `cron/isolated-agent` and the main user-facing runners in `auto-reply/reply/` still default to `policy: "shared"`. The probe deliberately wants to record health; the user-facing runners legitimately represent profile health. Each is a separate decision and not part of this PR.
  - Cooldown duration / backoff (`auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours`) — unchanged.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config` edits).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts`, registry, or loader edits).
  - Dependency surface unchanged (no `package.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml` / `npm-shrinkwrap.json` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml` edits; Dependency Guard not applicable).

### Reproduction

1. Enable `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.llmSlug: true` (default on).
2. Configure `anthropic:claude-cli` as the primary auth profile (Max subscription, healthy).
3. Trigger any session that ends up in the slug-generator path; let the underlying `claude` invocation return a 400 (reporters observed it as `LLM request rejected: You're out of extra usage`).
4. **Before this PR**: `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-state.json` shows `usageStats["anthropic:claude-cli"]` gaining `disabledReason: "billing"` and `disabledUntil` set ~5h forward. Any subsequent prompt to any agent resolving to the same profile fails instantly with `Provider claude-cli has billing issue (skipping all models)`, despite a direct curl to the same key returning 200.
5. **After this PR**: the slug helper's run reaches `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason({ policy: "local", failoverReason: "billing" })` which returns `null`. No write to `usageStats["anthropic:claude-cli"].disabledReason`. The next user-facing agent dispatches normally; if it then hits a real billing 402 itself, it records the failure correctly through the default `"shared"` policy.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#71709)**: Slug-generator's internal HTTP 400 (e.g. `LLM request rejected: You're out of extra usage` from `anthropic:claude-cli`) was promoted to a profile-wide `disabledReason: "billing"` cooldown lasting `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` (default 5h), blocking every user-facing agent that resolved to the same auth profile.

**Real environment tested (Linux x64, Node 22.19, pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7; unit test against the production `generateSlugViaLLM` + existing `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` policy gate)**: vitest mock of `runEmbeddedAgent` captures the production call options; the new assertion verifies `options.authProfileFailurePolicy === "local"`. The policy gate itself is covered end-to-end by the existing `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-profile-failure-policy.test.ts`, which proves `policy: "local"` returns `null` for `"billing"` and `"auth"` reasons (no shared-state write).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts` (4/4 pass)
2. Bite check — temporarily revert the single prod line, re-run the same vitest command, observe the new test fail with `AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'local'` (1 failed | 3 passed); restore prod line; confirm 4/4 green.
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts` (clean)
4. `pnpm exec oxlint src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts` (exit 0)

**Evidence after fix (verbatim vitest output)**:

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/main/openclaw/.worktrees/pr-71709

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Start at  11:00:40
   Duration  4.90s (transform 1.97s, setup 464ms, import 3.72s, tests 489ms, environment 0ms)
```

Bite check (after reverting the single prod line, same command):

```text
 FAIL  |hooks| ../../src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts > generateSlugViaLLM > marks the run lane-local so internal-helper failures do not poison shared profile health (#71709)
AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'local' // Object.is equality

- Expected:
"local"

+ Received:
undefined

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 3 passed (4)
```

**Observed result after fix**: The slug-generator opts into the lane-local failure policy. Any failure routed through `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` from this helper returns `null`, so a transient 400 inside slug generation cannot register `disabledReason: "billing"` against the shared profile or trigger the 5-hour user-facing cooldown. The other `runEmbeddedAgent` callers (user-facing runners, auth probe, memory flush, commitments, consult, cron) keep their existing default `"shared"` semantics unchanged.

**What was not tested**: A live Anthropic 7-day-cap / 402 reproduction (no live provider key available in this environment). The policy gate is covered end-to-end by its existing dedicated test, and `markAuthProfileFailure` semantics for the user-facing `"shared"` path are unchanged by this PR. Sibling helpers (`agent-runner-memory`, `commitments/runtime`, `commands/models/list.probe`, `crestodian/assistant`) were intentionally not touched and are out of scope.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: A real, persistent billing exhaustion on the user's provider key is no longer detected from the slug-helper lane. **Mitigation**: the next user-facing main-agent call still runs with `policy: "shared"` (default) and still classifies and records the same condition correctly. The pre-fix behavior merely moved that detection forward by one fire-and-forget background event, at the cost of a 5-hour false-positive whenever that event was unrelated. Detection-at-point-of-use is the documented contract for the other already-lane-local paths (`format`, `server_error`, `empty_response`, pre-start `timeout`).
- **Risk**: The change touches the auth-provider surface (whether a particular caller writes to shared profile health). **Mitigation**: zero new branches in the auth/cooldown layer itself; the seam (`authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"`) is shipped behavior already covered by `auth-profile-failure-policy.test.ts`. The classifier (`isBillingErrorMessage` and related) and the cooldown duration logic (`calculateAuthProfileCooldownMs`) are not touched.
- **Risk**: Sibling internal-helper callers (`agent-runner-memory`, `commitments/runtime`, `crestodian/assistant`) may have the same anti-pattern. **Mitigation**: explicitly named in the scope-boundary above and intentionally left for a follow-up if and when an owner decides. The probe (`commands/models/list.probe`) deliberately wants to record health and must stay `shared`.

### Change Type

`fix(hooks)` — internal-helper failure isolation for the slug-generator lane.

### Scope

`src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` and its colocated test only. 2 files, +13 / −0.

### Linked Issue

Fixes #71709.

target.body:
## Summary

- Adds deterministic autoscrubbing for package lockfile-only PR residue when the PR branch is in the main OpenClaw repository and the workflow token can push the cleanup commit.
- Restores only changed package lockfiles from the PR base tree using the Git data API, then creates one narrow commit: `Remove dependency lockfile change`.
- Skips autoscrub for fork PRs instead of attempting an uncertain push, and posts the blocking comment with the exact manual next step.
- Skips autoscrub when any changed `package.json` modifies `dependencies` or `devDependencies`, because restoring lockfiles could break frozen installs or dependency semantics.

## User-Facing Comments

This stacked PR adds one new success comment and expands the blocked comment:

- `Dependency lockfile changes were removed`: posted after a successful autoscrub commit, listing restored lockfiles, target branch, and cleanup commit SHA.
- `Dependency graph changes are blocked`: now explains whether autoscrub was not attempted for a fork branch, skipped because dependency fields changed, or attempted but rejected by GitHub.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/dependency-change-awareness.yml scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`

- [x] `pnpm vitest run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`
- [ ] `pnpm test:extension imessage` currently hits a pre-existing unrelated failure on `extensions/imessage/src/monitor.media-policy.test.ts` on clean `origin/main` as well

## Notes
- AI-assisted: yes
- This PR is intentionally scoped to the iMessage media handoff fix only.
- Verified the unrelated `monitor.media-policy` failure on a clean `origin/main` worktree before noting it here.

Closes #87597

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. Made `sourceDelivery` optional in both `createCronPromptExecutor` and `executeCronRun`
2. When `sourceDelivery` is undefined, reconstruct a `SourceDeliveryPlan` that **preserves legacy caller fields**:
   - Reads `toolPolicy` from stale caller params (`disableMessageTool`, `forceMessageTool`, `requireExplicitMessageTarget`)
   - Reads `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` from stale caller params — selects `owner: "message_tool"` when mode is `"message_tool_only"`, preserving delivery semantics
   - Reads `messageChannel` from stale caller params
   - Falls back to `resolvedDelivery` for channel/to/accountId/threadId
   - Passes `requireExplicitMessageTarget` through to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` (previously omitted, defaulting to `false`)
3. Added `logWarn` diagnostic to flag the fallback

This ensures webhook callers that disabled the message tool still have it disabled, announce callers that set `message_tool_only` keep that mode, `requireExplicitMessageTarget` is honored, and no security boundary is widened.

## Tests

11 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (6 tests):**
1. `sourceDelivery: undefined` (no legacy fields) → reconstructs with safe defaults ✅
2. `sourceDelivery: undefined` → falls back to `resolvedDelivery` channel/to/accountId/threadId ✅
3. Stale announce caller → preserves `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` and tool policy ✅
4. Stale webhook caller → message tool stays disabled ✅
5. `requireExplicitMessageTarget: true` → passed through to plan ✅
6. Valid `sourceDelivery` → works normally (regression) ✅

**`executeCronRun` level (5 tests):**
7. Stale announce caller → `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` preserved through full execute path ✅
8. No legacy mode → `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` defaults to `undefined` ✅
9. `requireExplicitMessageTarget: true` → passed through executeCronRun fallback ✅
10. Missing toolPolicy → `requireExplicitMessageTarget` defaults to `false` ✅
11. Stale webhook caller → message tool disabled preserved through executeCronRun ✅

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: All `sessionTarget: "isolated"` cron jobs crash with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`. Additionally, stale announce callers would silently lose `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` and `requireExplicitMessageTarget` through the `executeCronRun` → `createCronPromptExecutor` boundary.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, source tree at commit `65c7e3812e`

### Failing proof (upstream/main, before fix)

On upstream/main (`13deea2a`), `sourceDelivery` is **required** — no guard exists, no fallback:

```
$ git show 13deea2a9d:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | grep -n "sourceDelivery"
112:  sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan;       # <-- NOT optional
156:  const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;  # <-- direct deref, crashes when undefined
327:  sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan;       # <-- NOT optional in executeCronRun either
```

The test file does not exist on main:
```
$ git show 13deea2a9d:src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts' does not exist in '13deea2a9d'
```

### Passing proof (fix branch, after fix)

**Delivery mode preservation proof** (demonstrates the owner-selection fix):

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/cron-execute-proof.mjs

=== PR #85249 executeCronRun Delivery Mode Proof ===
Environment: darwin arm64 Node v24.10.0

Test 1: Stale announce caller (sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only)
  owner resolved to: "message_tool"
  plan.sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"
  plan.messageTool.requireExplicitTarget: true
  plan.messageTool.enabled: true
  plan.messageTool.force: true
  ✅ PASSED

Test 2: Stale webhook caller (sourceReplyDeliveryMode: undefined, message tool disabled)
  owner resolved to: "none"
  plan.sourceReplyDeliveryMode: undefined
  plan.messageTool.enabled: false
  ✅ PASSED

✅ All delivery mode proofs passed
```

### Vitest proof (all 11 tests including executeCronRun-level coverage)

```
$ npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reconstructs a safe delivery plan when sourceDelivery is undefined 53ms
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > uses resolvedDelivery channel/to for legacy callers without sourceDelivery
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy/sourceReplyDeliveryMode from stale announce caller
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy from stale webhook caller with message tool disabled
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget from legacy toolPolicy in createCronPromptExecutor
 ✓ cron > createCronPromptExecutor sourceDelivery guard > still works with a valid sourceDelivery
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > preserves legacy sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only through executeCronRun
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > defaults to sourceReplyDeliveryMode undefined when legacy mode is absent
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget through executeCronRun fallback
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > passes requireExplicitMessageTarget=false by default when legacy toolPolicy omits it
 ✓ cron > executeCronRun sourceDelivery guard > reads legacy toolPolicy with message tool disabled through executeCronRun

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  837ms
```

### Regression test (existing message-tool-policy tests still pass)

```
$ npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)
   Duration  8.12s
```

### What was not tested
- Live Telegram cron delivery (would require a running Gateway with configured Telegram bot)
- Actual version-skew scenario with stale compiled dist/ artifacts (tested via TypeScript-level params simulation)

Fixes #85051

target.title: fix: prevent MEDIA: false-positive extraction from indented code blocks
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #69311

`splitMediaFromOutput()` incorrectly extracts `MEDIA:` directives from 4-space/tab indented code blocks because `parseFenceSpans()` only recognizes fenced (`` ``` ``/`~~~`) code blocks. This causes code lines to be silently deleted and spurious media attachment requests to be generated.

## Changes

### Fix A: Indented code block heuristic (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Added `isIndentedCodeLine()` function that detects lines starting with 4 spaces or tab, preceded by a blank line or another indented code line (CommonMark heuristic)
- Tracks `prevLineBlankOrIndentedCode` state through the line loop
- Skips MEDIA extraction for lines identified as indented code

### Fix A+: Whitespace folding protection (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Changed `cleanedText` construction to apply whitespace folding only to non-code lines
- Tracks `keptLineIsCode[]` parallel to `keptLines[]`
- Changed `.trim()` to `.replace(/^\\n+/, "").trimEnd()` to preserve leading indentation on first content line

### Fix C: Fence closing line validation (`src/markdown/fences.ts`)
- Added `/^\\s*$/.test(match[3])` check when validating closing fence lines
- Rejects closing fences with trailing non-whitespace text per CommonMark spec
- Affects 5 downstream consumers (all become more correct — strictly more conservative fence spans)

### Fix D: MEDIA_TOKEN_RE flag cleanup (`src/media/parse.ts`)
- Removed dead `i` flag from `MEDIA_TOKEN_RE` regex
- The upstream gate `startsWith("MEDIA:")` is already case-sensitive, so lowercase never reaches the regex

## Real behavior proof (live runtime)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `MEDIA:` directive is incorrectly extracted from indented code blocks (4-space/tab indented) and fenced code blocks, causing spurious attachment attempts.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout (fix branch), macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Main branch regex `/^MEDIA:(.+)$/gm` matches ALL lines starting with `MEDIA:` regardless of indentation context. An LLM reply like:

```
Here's how to use the directive:

    MEDIA:/path/to/example.jpg
    # This is just example code
```

Would incorrectly trigger file attachment for `/path/to/example.jpg`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/parse.test.ts

--- Fix: isIndentedCodeLine heuristic (parse.ts L490-492) ---
function isIndentedCodeLine(line: string, prevLineBlankOrCode: boolean): boolean {
  return prevLineBlankOrCode && /^(?: {4}|\t)/.test(line);
}

--- Fix: indented code block skip in splitMediaFromOutput (L562-569) ---
if (isIndented) {
  keptLines.push(line);
  pushTextSegment(line);
  prevLineBlankOrIndentedCode = true;
  continue;  // Skip MEDIA extraction for this line
}

--- Test results ---
✓ does not extract MEDIA: from 4-space indented code block
✓ does not extract MEDIA: from tab-indented code block
✓ does not extract MEDIA: from multi-line indented code block
✓ does not extract MEDIA: from indented code block with blank lines within
✓ does not extract MEDIA: from indented code block immediately after fenced block
✓ preserves indented code block formatting when real MEDIA: is also present
✓ preserves fenced code block content when real MEDIA: is extracted
✓ handles mixed: real MEDIA + fenced code + indented code in one output

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  80 passed (80)
  Duration  136ms
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 80 tests pass including 5 new indented code block protection tests. The fix correctly skips `MEDIA:` extraction for lines inside 4-space/tab indented code blocks while still extracting valid directives from non-indented positions.
- **Observed result after fix**: The parser now tracks `prevLineBlankOrIndentedCode` state and uses `isIndentedCodeLine()` to detect markdown code blocks, skipping MEDIA extraction for those lines. Both fenced (```) and indented (4-space/tab) code blocks are protected.
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram/Discord message delivery was not tested (no bot environment configured). The fix is validated at the parser level with comprehensive test cases covering all indentation patterns, mixed scenarios, and edge cases.


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `fcfd88dd4f`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/parse.test.ts src/markdown/fences.test.ts

 ✓ unit-fast  ../../src/media/parse.test.ts (80 tests) 10ms
 ✓ unit-fast  ../../src/markdown/fences.test.ts (8 tests) 1ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  88 passed (88)
   Start at  20:20:57
   Duration  152ms (transform 48ms, setup 0ms, import 63ms, tests 10ms, environment 0ms)
```

target.title: fix #80213: [Feature]: Skill author-defined setup hook (run skill-supplied script on install/update)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80213

### Changes

- Add `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` frontmatter hook that runs a skill-supplied script after install/update
- Parse `setup.script` and optional `timeoutMs` from SKILL.md frontmatter
- Wire setup hook into archive installs via `afterInstall` with `allowSetupHooks` trust gate
- Security: realpath confinement for symlink escape prevention
- Security: blocklisted env forwarding (PATH, HOME, NODE_OPTIONS, LD_PRELOAD, etc.)
- Security: gate archive install hooks behind explicit `allowSetupHooks` parameter (default false)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill authors can define a `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` in SKILL.md frontmatter that executes after install/update. The setup script receives only `SKILL_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_HOOK_KIND`, and allowlisted `requires.env` — dangerous vars like PATH and NODE_OPTIONS are blocked.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw worktree at abdfaf2a.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  mkdir /tmp/test-skill
  cat > /tmp/test-skill/SKILL.md <<'MD'
  ---
  metadata:
    openclaw:
      setup:
        script: setup.sh
  ---
  # Test
  MD
  echo -e '#!/bin/sh\necho "setup ok"' > /tmp/test-skill/setup.sh
  chmod +x /tmp/test-skill/setup.sh

  # Run setup hook via the real code path
  node --import tsx -e '
  import { runSkillSetupHook } from "./src/agents/skills-setup.js";
  console.log(JSON.stringify(await runSkillSetupHook({ targetDir: "/tmp/test-skill", mode: "install" })));
  '
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  === Setup hook success ===
  {"ok":true}

  === Setup hook: missing script ===
  {"ok":false,"error":"Setup script not found: missing.sh","failureKind":"setup-failed"}
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** A script declared in `metadata.openclaw.setup.script` executes after install and returns `ok:true`. Missing scripts return `ok:false` with clear error. Path traversal (`../etc/passwd`) is blocked by realpath containment. Env vars like PATH and NODE_OPTIONS in `requires.env` are silently dropped.
- **What was not tested:** Live `openclaw skills install` with a published skill (requires ClawHub-deployed skill with setup metadata).

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/agents/skills-setup.test.ts`, `src/agents/skills/frontmatter.test.ts`, `src/agents/skills-archive-install.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Setup hook success, missing script, symlink escapes blocked, env isolation, timeout handling
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: All hook execution flows through `runSkillSetupHook`; the three P1 security fixes (realpath containment, env allowlist, trust gate) are each exercised by tests covering the exact failure modes ClawSweeper identified.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Skill install had no post-install hook mechanism; the PR adds frontmatter-based setup declaration with security-hardened execution.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Prior code lacked realpath containment, env sanitization, and a trust gate for automatic code execution during package installs.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose spooled handler timeout config
target.body:
## Summary
- expose `channels.telegram.spooledUpdateHandlerTimeoutMs` with top-level and per-account config support
- pass the configured value into Telegram isolated polling sessions
- document the timeout and regenerate bundled channel config metadata

## Why
Telegram isolated ingress already fails stale claimed spool updates after a handler timeout, but operators could not tune that timeout from `channels.telegram`. A wedged `.processing` file can therefore keep a same-chat lane blocked until the default 25 minute timeout.

## Runtime Proof
I ran a redacted local runtime harness against the PR head using the real `TelegramPollingSession`, real spool files, real reply-fence timeout recovery path, and a fake Telegram Bot runtime to avoid live network calls. The harness configured `isolatedIngress.spooledUpdateHandlerTimeoutMs = 200`, wrote two same-lane spooled updates, wedged update `42`, and verified update `43` drained only after timeout recovery restarted isolated ingress.

```json
{
  "configuredTimeoutMs": 200,
  "configuredAbortGraceMs": 200,
  "elapsedMs": 475,
  "workerCreateCount": 2,
  "botConstructCount": 2,
  "events": [
    "first:42",
    "second:43"
  ],
  "failedUpdateIds": [
    42
  ],
  "pendingUpdateIds": [],
  "timeoutLog": "[telegram] Telegram isolated polling spool handler timed out behind update 42 on lane telegram:-100:topic:10 after 205ms; marking the update failed, aborting active reply work, and restarting isolated ingress so later updates can drain."
}
```

This shows the configured timeout was honored by the isolated-ingress runtime path: update `42` was failed after the configured timeout budget and the later same-lane update `43` drained after the restart.

## Validation
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` passed locally while preparing the patch. A later rerun in this workspace timed out in Vitest/Rolldown pre-test bundling with repeated plugin timing warnings before any assertion output.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Session titles were added as default-on background summarization, but review found upgrade/privacy risk, missing auth-profile propagation, and generated titles overriding existing subject labels.
- Solution: Make AI session titles explicit opt-in, carry the resolved session auth profile into title generation, and keep existing manual/subject naming precedence ahead of generated titles.
- What changed: Updated the session title generator, conversation label auth lookup, runtime model-auth wrapper, session title precedence, config help text, and focused regression coverage.
- What did NOT change: No dependency changes. Manual session renaming still wins, existing subject labels still win, and title generation still uses the selected session model only when sessionTitle.enabled is true.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** model-generated session titles must be opt-in, use the session-scoped auth profile for the background model request, persist autoTitle, and surface the generated title through the session-list/sidebar data path without hiding manual names or subject labels.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node 24.16.0, local OpenClaw PR worktree for the submitted PR diff; the final rebase only changed commit SHA and the PR diff was verified unchanged before push. Gateway startup used `pnpm gateway:dev` through the dev-gateway script with DeepSeek configured. Title persistence/sidebar proof used the patched OpenClaw session-title generator, session store, runtime model-auth lookup, and `sessions.list` row builder against a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 
```bash
OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/dev-gateway-current-head.log \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643 \
  OPENCLAW_MODEL=openai/deepseek-chat \
  bash /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh

TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/session-title-runtime-proof.mts

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 \
  pnpm exec vitest run \
    src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts \
    src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts \
    src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts \
    src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts \
    src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts \
    ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts

env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm check:test-types
env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" pnpm lint --threads=8
```
- **Evidence after fix:** 
```text
2026-05-29T12:37:25.224+08:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/deepseek-chat (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-29T12:37:25.227+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice; 6.3s)
2026-05-29T12:37:25.234+08:00 [gateway] ready
OpenClaw session title runtime proof passed
local OpenAI-compatible endpoint requests: 1
auth profile used for title request: openai:default (user locked)
autoTitle persisted: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
sessions.list sidebar derivedTitle: Invoice Export Duplicate Tax Rows
subject precedence row: Existing Subject Label
manual displayName precedence row: Manual Sidebar Name
Test Files  8 passed (8)
Tests  357 passed (357)
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] starting
[oxlint:core] still running after 30s
[oxlint:core] still running after 60s
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] starting
[oxlint:extensions] still running after 30s
[oxlint:extensions] still running after 60s
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] starting
[oxlint:scripts] finished
```
- **Observed result after fix:** On current HEAD, `sessionTitle.enabled` remains opt-in. With `sessionTitle.enabled: true`, the title generator used the session model and user-locked auth profile, persisted `autoTitle`, and `sessions.list` exposed the generated `derivedTitle` for sidebar display. Existing `subject` and manual `displayName` still take precedence over generated titles. The PR-focused tests, `pnpm check:test-types`, and `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed after the rebase/conflict fix.
- **What was not tested:** No live browser screenshot was captured; the sidebar behavior was verified through the same `sessions.list` row data consumed by Control UI. The title proof used a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint for deterministic model output; gateway startup was verified separately with the DeepSeek model configuration.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit and local runtime proof
- Target test file: src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts; src/auto-reply/reply/conversation-label-generator.test.ts; src/plugins/runtime/runtime-model-auth.runtime.test.ts; src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts
- Scenario locked in: sessionTitle is opt-in, title generation carries the resolved auth profile, runtime model auth receives profile parameters, autoTitle persists into the session store, and subject/displayName remain stronger than autoTitle.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the exact review blockers at the narrow functions that schedule title generation, resolve auth, and compute sidebar title precedence.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The first implementation treated missing `sessionTitle` config as enabled, forwarded provider/model without the auth profile used by the reply, and inserted `autoTitle` before `subject` in the title precedence chain.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered generated title display and model override routing, but not opt-in upgrade behavior, auth-profile propagation, or subject-over-autoTitle precedence.

target.title: fix(agents): model-scope cooldown for transport timeout (#87462)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #87462 reports that on v2026.5.26, a single `timeout` failure on one Google model marks the entire Google auth profile as cooldowned, exhausting the configured fallback chain (gemini-3-flash-preview → gemini-3.1-flash-lite → gemini-2.5-flash → gemini-3.1-pro-preview) within ~186ms. Google AI Studio confirms zero API errors at the same timestamp, proving the cooldown is triggered internally by OpenClaw rather than by a real provider failure. The user sees `FailoverError: No available auth profile for google (all in cooldown or unavailable)`; the next message succeeds, confirming the issue is transient and self-recovering.
- **Root Cause**: In `computeNextProfileUsageStats` (`src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`), the cooldown scope is decided by hardcoding `params.reason === "rate_limit"` for model-scoping vs. profile-wide. Specifically, the initial-cooldown branch assigns `cooldownModel = params.reason === "rate_limit" ? params.modelId : undefined`, so a `timeout` failure clears `cooldownModel` (= profile-wide). The bypass predicate `shouldBypassModelScopedCooldown` in `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts` mirrors the same rate_limit-only guard, so even setting `cooldownModel` for timeout would not let sibling models pass. Reporter's scenario: one Google auth profile shared by 4 fallback models → first model's post-start timeout sets a profile-wide cooldown → subsequent attempts of the other 3 models all see the profile as unavailable → 186ms cascade exhaustion. `failover-policy.ts` already treats `"timeout"` as a transient probe-able failure (same list as `"rate_limit"`); the cooldown layer was the only place still treating timeout as profile-wide.
- **Fix**: Introduce `isModelScopedCooldownReason(reason)` in `usage-state.ts` covering `rate_limit` and `timeout`. Wire it through:
  - `shouldBypassModelScopedCooldown` so siblings of a timeout-failed model can bypass the cooldown for a different `forModel`.
  - `getSoonestCooldownExpiry` so display/exhausted-reason resolution honors model-scoped timeout cooldowns.
  - `computeNextProfileUsageStats` (all three rate_limit-only branches): initial cooldown sets `cooldownModel = modelId` for timeout, active-cooldown widening keeps the scope-clear logic for profile-wide reasons only, and the "no originating model" widening guard now triggers for any model-scoped reason.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts` — new `isModelScopedCooldownReason` predicate; two existing `cooldownReason === "rate_limit"` checks routed through it.
  - `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts` — three `params.reason === "rate_limit"` checks routed through the predicate; behavior unchanged for non-timeout reasons.
  - `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts` — two new tests under `describe("isProfileInCooldown")` covering timeout model-scope and the legacy widened-scope path.
  - `src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts` — one new test verifying `markAuthProfileFailure` with `reason: "timeout"` and a `modelId` persists `cooldownModel`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `markAuthProfileFailure` gate (`auth-profile-failure-policy.ts`) — still returns null for transport/`server_error`/`empty_response`/`format`, and for `timeout` when `providerStarted !== true`. Only post-start timeouts that already reach `markAuthProfileFailure` change scope, not the set of failures that get recorded.
  - Cooldown duration / backoff (`calculateAuthProfileCooldownMs`) — unchanged. Timeout cooldowns still escalate per the existing stepped backoff.
  - Profile-wide failures (`auth`, `billing`, `format`, `server_error`, `auth_permanent`, `overloaded`, …) — `cooldownModel` continues to be cleared so all models stay blocked.
  - `rate_limit` semantics — unchanged.
  - PR #87122's overload handling (`cooldownUntil` computation for `reason === "overloaded"`) — non-overlapping; this PR touches the `cooldownModel` scope branches and the `shouldBypassModelScopedCooldown` predicate.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config` edits).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/api.ts` / `runtime-api.ts`, registry, or loader edits).
  - Session store unchanged (relevant given the June SQLite migration).

### Reproduction

1. Configure a single Google auth profile with multiple fallback models (the reporter's chain: `gemini-3-flash-preview` → `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` → `gemini-2.5-flash` → `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`).
2. Send a request that triggers a post-start streaming timeout on the primary model (provider has begun emitting, then stalls past the idle watchdog).
3. **Before this PR**: `markAuthProfileFailure({ reason: "timeout", modelId: "gemini-3-flash-preview" })` records `cooldownReason="timeout"` and `cooldownModel=undefined`. Subsequent fallback attempts of `flash-lite` / `2.5-flash` / `pro-preview` all see the profile as cooldowned and fail within ~50ms each, surfacing as `FailoverError: No available auth profile for google (all in cooldown or unavailable)`.
4. **After this PR**: the same failure records `cooldownModel="gemini-3-flash-preview"`. The 3 sibling models pass `shouldBypassModelScopedCooldown` and dispatch normally; only the failed model is blocked for the backoff window.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#87462)**: A transport-class `timeout` failure on one model no longer takes down sibling fallback models that share the same auth profile. Sibling models bypass the cooldown via `shouldBypassModelScopedCooldown` (now broadened from rate_limit-only to "model-scoped reasons"), while the failed model itself stays cooldowned per the existing stepped backoff.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22, tsx real-component harness driving production `isProfileInCooldown` + `computeNextProfileUsageStats` semantics via a hand-constructed `AuthProfileStore` matching reporter's 4-model Google config)**: tsx-resolved imports of production `isProfileInCooldown` from `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts`. The harness simulates the post-failure usageStats state (matching what `computeNextProfileUsageStats` produces both before and after this fix) and probes the per-model bypass behavior.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/qmd87462/repro.mts` (real-component cooldown-scope sweep)
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.getsoonestcooldownexpiry.test.ts` (158/158 pass, 5 test files)
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix (verbatim real-component harness output)**:

```text
================ #87462 cooldown-scope contract sweep ================

--- CURRENT MAIN: timeout, cooldownModel=undefined (the bug — all 4 blocked) ---
  state: cooldownReason=timeout, cooldownModel=<undefined>
  gemini-3-flash-preview       → blocked=true ✓ expected
  gemini-3.1-flash-lite        → blocked=true
  gemini-2.5-flash             → blocked=true
  gemini-3.1-pro-preview       → blocked=true

--- AFTER FIX: timeout, cooldownModel=gemini-3-flash-preview (only failed model blocked) ---
  state: cooldownReason=timeout, cooldownModel=gemini-3-flash-preview
  gemini-3-flash-preview       → blocked=true ✓ expected
  gemini-3.1-flash-lite        → blocked=false
  gemini-2.5-flash             → blocked=false
  gemini-3.1-pro-preview       → blocked=false

--- BASELINE (rate_limit, already model-scoped — proves the bypass mechanism) ---
  state: cooldownReason=rate_limit, cooldownModel=gemini-3-flash-preview
  gemini-3-flash-preview       → blocked=true ✓ expected
  gemini-3.1-flash-lite        → blocked=false
  gemini-2.5-flash             → blocked=false
  gemini-3.1-pro-preview       → blocked=false

--- CONTROL: auth failure (profile-wide, all blocked — must NOT change after fix) ---
  state: cooldownReason=auth, cooldownModel=<undefined>
  gemini-3-flash-preview       → blocked=true ✓ expected
  gemini-3.1-flash-lite        → blocked=true
  gemini-2.5-flash             → blocked=true
  gemini-3.1-pro-preview       → blocked=true
```

**Observed result after fix**: post-start timeout on model X cooldowns only model X on the shared profile. Sibling fallback models bypass and can dispatch. Auth/billing/format/server_error failures continue to block all models on the profile, matching their profile-wide reliability semantics. `rate_limit` semantics unchanged.

**What was not tested**: a real live Google API request producing a streaming timeout. The harness exercises the cooldown-scope contract that `markAuthProfileFailure` produces; the upstream `auth-profile-failure-policy.ts` gate that routes only `providerStarted=true` timeouts into this code path is unchanged and covered by its existing tests.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The fix sits in `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts` / `usage-state.ts`, a hot path consulted by every model dispatch and failover decision. **Mitigation**: change is a single-predicate generalization (`rate_limit` → `rate_limit || timeout`) wired through one new helper, with five existing-call-site rewrites that all read the same boolean. No new state, no new persisted fields, no schema changes. The active-cooldown-widening path (different model fails during cooldown → widen to profile-wide) is preserved.
- **Risk**: Overlap with PR #87122 ("Avoid profile cooldowns for provider overloads", cbcampos, open). **Mitigation**: #87122 changes `cooldownUntil` computation for `reason === "overloaded"`; this PR changes `cooldownModel` scope for `rate_limit || timeout`. Different conditionals, non-overlapping logic, no merge conflict expected.
- **Risk**: Could mask a real provider outage where the timeout is genuinely Google-wide rather than per-model. **Mitigation**: when a second model fails during the active cooldown window, the existing widening logic clears `cooldownModel` and the cooldown reverts to profile-wide — exactly as it does today for rate_limit cascades.

### Change Type

`fix(agents)` — cooldown scope correctness for transport timeouts.

### Scope

`src/agents/auth-profiles/` only. 4 files, +76 / −8.

### Linked Issue

Fixes #87462.

target.title: fix(codex): move skills list and memory pointer to collaboration instructions
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reduces repeated per-turn Codex prompt payload by moving two stable OpenClaw context blocks out of native Codex user input and into turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions:

- the compact OpenClaw skills list
- the tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer

Full `MEMORY.md` fallback injection is intentionally unchanged. When memory tools are unavailable, tools are disabled, or the active workspace differs from the configured memory workspace, `MEMORY.md` still uses the existing bounded turn-context path.

## Why

Native Codex threads preserve prior user turns in their transcript. OpenClaw was adding stable runtime/reference blocks to the user prompt every turn, so those blocks accumulated in the native Codex thread history, and bloated the context with duplicate content.

That is especially wasteful for the compact OpenClaw skills list:

- it is usually stable across normal turns
- it can be large
- it is metadata/instructions for how to load skills, not the user's actual request
- repeating it in user input makes prompt bloat worse after every turn

The `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` block has the same problem when `MEMORY.md` is routed through memory tools. It is a pointer telling Codex that durable memory exists and which tools to use. It does not need to live inside the repeated user prompt.

This matters because the user prompt should stay focused on the current user request and truly turn-specific reference context. Stable routing and reference context belongs in developer-instruction surfaces where Codex already handles collaboration-mode overlays.

## How

The change splits Codex prompt assembly more cleanly by lifetime and role:

- `OpenClaw Skills` is rendered as turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions for native Codex turns.
- The tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer is rendered as turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.
- `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` no longer includes skills or the memory pointer in the per-turn user prompt wrapper.
- Full `MEMORY.md` fallback content is still included through the existing bounded workspace prompt context when the tool-routed memory path is not valid.
- Docs now describe the new native Codex routing behavior.

This keeps stable context available to Codex while reducing repeated user-input payload in normal native Codex sessions.

## Behavior addressed

Native Codex turns should stop repeating the compact OpenClaw skills list and tool-routed memory pointer in the per-turn user input. Those blocks should instead arrive through turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions. Full `MEMORY.md` fallback injection should continue to behave exactly as before.

## Real environment tested

Local OpenClaw source checkout, using the Codex app-server test harness. 

`bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts --run
git diff --check
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts docs/concepts/system-prompt.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/token-use.md`

## Evidence after fix

The Codex app-server tests now assert that:

- the OpenClaw skills block is present in `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`
- the skills block is absent from `turn/start` user input
- the tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer is present in collaboration developer instructions
- the memory pointer is absent from `turn/start` user input
- full `MEMORY.md` fallback content remains in the existing prompt path when memory tools cannot route it
- extra non-root `MEMORY.md` bootstrap files still use the normal workspace context behavior

The targeted test run passed:
text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       124 passed (124)

## Observed result after fix

For normal native Codex turns with memory tools available, stable skills metadata and the memory-tool pointer are no longer repeated inside the user prompt. Codex still receives them as turn-scoped developer instructions, while the current user request remains clean.

When the full `MEMORY.md` fallback path is required, `MEMORY.md` remains in the bounded turn-context path as before.

## Real behavior proof guidance

Before the fix I ran the gateway, reset the session with `/new`, and prompted it first: `reply only "1"`. This step is needed as we want to check what happens to the prompt on the second turn. After the agent's reply, I entered the next prompt:
`check your current system prompt. report, do you have any duplicated blocks in there, like files being included multiple times`

Agent's reply:
```text
Yes. Current visible prompt has duplicated blocks now.

Core MD files:
- `TOOLS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md` are still injected once each in developer context.
- `AGENTS.md` full content appears once, from the earlier user-provided `AGENTS.md instructions...` block.
- `MEMORY.md` and `HEARTBEAT.md` are not pasted.

Duplicated / repeated context:
- The `OpenClaw Skills` inventory is included again, effectively duplicated across the previous and current user runtime-context blocks.
- The `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` note is repeated.
- Conversation metadata blocks are repeated with new message ids/timestamps.
- The prior user question and my prior answer are present twice in the total visible context: once as actual conversation turns, and once quoted inside `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn`.

So: no full core MD file is duplicated, but yes, OpenClaw is now duplicating runtime/reference material and conversation history. The quoted conversation-context duplication is especially obvious.
```

After the fix, I did the same: reset the session with `/new`, and prompted it first: `reply only "1"`. After the agent's reply, I entered the same next prompt:
`check your current system prompt. report, do you have any duplicated blocks in there, like files being included multiple times`

Agent's reply now changed to reflect the expected behavior:
```text
Yes.

Visible duplicated/overlapping blocks in this turn:

- Previous turn transcript is duplicated: it exists in native conversation history and is also re-quoted inside `OpenClaw assembled context`.
- `skill-creator` appears twice as an available skill:
  - OpenClaw workspace skill: `~/src/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md`
  - Codex system skill: `/home/beru/.openclaw/agents/home/agent/codex-home/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md`
- There are two separate skill systems injected: OpenClaw workspace skills and Codex skills instructions. That is overlap, not exact file duplication.
- `AGENTS.md` is visible once from the earlier user-provided block in conversation history. I do not see it separately injected as a developer block.
- `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and `TOOLS.md` each appear once in the loaded workspace context.

So the real current bloat is transcript duplication via `OpenClaw assembled context`, plus overlapping skill frameworks.
```

As expected, the agents does not see the addressed repetition anymore. It still identifies other duplications, but they are outside of the scope of this PR: skill overlap between Codex and OpenClaw, and conversation history bloat.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `model: "inherit"` support for native sub-agent spawns so child runs can follow the requester session's active model.
- Threads requester model context from tool creation through `sessions_spawn` into direct sub-agent planning, with a persisted-session fallback when needed.
- Preserves compatibility for an existing model alias named `inherit`: aliases resolve before the inheritance sentinel is applied.
- Documents the new sub-agent model inheritance behavior, alias precedence, and config precedence.
- Refreshes generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.
- Simplifies a dynamic live model test assertion path that timed out a PR CI shard.
- Out of scope: ACP runtime model inheritance; ACP spawns keep their existing model handling.

Review focus: model-selection precedence, alias compatibility around the `inherit` sentinel, provider-qualified requester model refs, the missing-requester-model error path, and whether docs describe the new behavior clearly.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No formal maintainer request; this addresses a configuration gap where sessions can use different active models but derived sub-agents could not intentionally follow the requester model.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior or issue addressed: native `sessions_spawn` can now use `model: "inherit"` or sub-agent model config set to `"inherit"` to resolve the child model from the requester session, while preserving any configured model alias named `"inherit"`.

Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw checkout at current PR head `a4afd578ae` for current-head local validation; local loopback Gateway real provider proof was run at `7e88ecd0ba` before the latest rebases/test-only CI stabilization commits. The final commits only rebase onto latest `origin/main` and simplify a slow test assertion; they do not change the runtime model-inheritance path. Live proof used OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and a real `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` OAuth auth profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
GOMAXPROCS=1 node scripts/check-changed.mjs
node scripts/run-node.mjs models auth order set --provider openai-codex openai-codex:default
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs

Focused regression tests also verify that a configured alias named `"inherit"` remains a concrete model selection instead of triggering inheritance, and that provider-qualified requester model refs are not duplicated.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof used a local loopback Gateway and a real model provider run, with channel connectors skipped to avoid external delivery. Local `check:changed` was rerun with `GOMAXPROCS=1` after an intermittent `tsgolint` parser SIGSEGV seen in the local environment; the rerun passed before the final latest-main rebases. The final PR head differs from the live-proof head by rebases plus test-only assertion simplification.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing tests covered concrete model overrides and config defaults but had no requester-model inheritance path or alias-compatibility coverage for an alias named `"inherit"`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/config-agents.md docs/tools/subagents.md src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/tool-description-presets.ts`

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts`
- `GOMAXPROCS=1 node scripts/check-changed.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --compact --cli-backend-logs`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --agent main --session-key agent:main:pr-inherit-proof-20260528-7e88ecd-006 --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --timeout 900 --json --message '<sessions_spawn model="inherit" proof prompt>'`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs sessions --agent main --active 30 --json`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model-selection tests for explicit `inherit`, configured `inherit`, alias preservation, missing requester model behavior, provider-qualified requester model refs, and a model alias named `"inherit"` winning over the sentinel.
- `sessions_spawn` tool tests to verify active model provider/id is passed into native sub-agent context.
- Direct sub-agent spawn tests to verify requester model context is persisted on the child session.
- Generated Codex prompt snapshots for the updated `sessions_spawn` description.
- Dynamic live model candidate test assertion simplification to avoid a slow Vitest mock/deep-matcher path that timed out the PR CI shard.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Sub-agent spawns could select explicit or configured concrete models, but there was no supported way to make a child follow the requester session's active model. The first implementation also treated bare `"inherit"` as a sentinel before alias resolution; this follow-up preserves existing aliases named `"inherit"`. After rebasing onto latest `origin/main`, the PR CI also exposed a slow `live-model-dynamic-candidates` test path; the final test-only commit keeps the same assertions with cheaper explicit call capture.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now accept `"inherit"` as a special value when no model alias named `"inherit"` exists.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model resolution precedence for native sub-agent spawns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps existing concrete model and alias precedence, resolves `"inherit"` only after alias resolution, preserves provider-qualified requester model refs, errors when inheritance is requested without a requester model, and adds focused tests at the model-selection, tool, direct-spawn, prompt-snapshot, and live Gateway seams.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper to re-run on `a4afd578ae`, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

The PR body includes live Gateway proof plus current-head local validation. Waiting on automated checks and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's live Gateway proof request, the provider-qualified inherited model ref finding, the rebase/dirty-branch finding, the prompt snapshot CI drift, the CI timeout in `checks-node-agentic-agents`, the transient `check-test-types` bonjour test type failure by rebasing onto latest main, and the compatibility finding about a model alias named `"inherit"`.

target.title: fix(logging): resolve hostname lazily to avoid caching empty string on macOS
target.body:
## Summary
- Replace module-level `HOSTNAME` constant with lazy `getHostname()` function
- macOS intermittently returns empty hostname during ESM module load phase
- Lazy resolution ensures hostname is captured correctly after gateway startup

## Reproduction
```bash
grep '"hostname"' /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log | head -5
```
Before fix:
```
_meta.hostname: "unknown"   ← wrong
hostname: "hopembpm5deMBP"  ← correct (from different source)
```

## Root Cause
`os.hostname()` returns empty string on macOS during module load, cached as "unknown" for entire gateway lifetime.

Fixes #87258

target.title: feat(ui): add KaTeX math rendering support
target.body:
## Summary

Add KaTeX-based mathematical formula rendering to OpenClaw chat interface. When `ui.mathRendering` is set to `"katex"`, LaTeX math expressions (both inline `$...$` and display `$$...$$`) are rendered as beautifully formatted math in chat messages.

## Changes

### Configuration
- Add `mathRendering?: "off" | "katex"` to `types.openclaw.ts` UI config
- Add corresponding Zod schema in `zod-schema.ts` (inside `.strict()` ui object)
- Add `ui.mathRendering` label in `schema.labels.ts`, help text in `schema.help.ts`, documentation in `configuration-reference.md`

### Core Rendering
- New `ui/src/ui/katex-renderer.ts` module with math extraction, pre-processing, and synchronous rendering
- Placeholder-based architecture: extract math → sanitize HTML → restore rendered math
- Handles `\$` escaped delimiters, nested braces, display/inline modes
- Post-processes `katex-error` class → falls back to raw LaTeX text
- CJK boundary support: `isCJKBoundary()` covers fullwidth punctuation, CJK punctuation, CJK ideographs, Hangul, Kana

### DOMPurify Integration
- New `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex()` function (original `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` unchanged)
- `uponSanitizeAttribute` DOMPurify hook using `node.closest(".katex")` for attribute preservation
- Extended allowed tags: 29 MathML + SVG tags (msqrt, mroot, msubsup, menclose, etc.)
- Extended allowed attributes: 22 MathML + SVG attributes (mathvariant, displaystyle, etc.)

### Rendering Pipeline
- `grouped-render.ts`: Thread `mathRendering` through `renderMarkdownContent` selector and `renderStreamingGroup`
- Streaming: `mathRendering` passed through `renderStreamingGroup()` → `renderGroupedMessage()`
- `chat.ts`: Preload KaTeX module + CSS when `mathRendering === "katex"` (one-shot guard to prevent render loops)
- 7-file bootstrap chain: `control-ui-contract.ts` → `control-ui.ts` → `control-ui-bootstrap.ts` → `app-view-state.ts` → `app.ts` → `app-render.ts` → `chat.ts`

### Tests
- 56 test cases in `katex-renderer.test.ts` covering extraction, rendering, escaping, error handling, CJK boundaries, maxSize cap
- All 1599 UI tests pass, 3 pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

### Dependencies
- Pinned `katex@0.16.47` (exact version, zero behavioral impact)

## Design Decisions

- **Full synchronous architecture**: No async changes to `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml`; new sync `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex` function
- **Streaming safety**: KaTeX rendering now flows through streaming path via `mathRendering` parameter
- **CJK boundary support**: Closing `$` before fullwidth punctuation, CJK characters, Hangul, and Kana is recognized as valid boundary
- **Error resilience**: KaTeX parse errors gracefully fall back to displaying raw LaTeX; `maxSize: 100` prevents layout-breaking output
- **Render loop guard**: `katexPreloadTriggered` one-shot boolean prevents infinite update cycles
- **Zero breakage**: All existing callers of `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` continue working unchanged

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: KaTeX math rendering in OpenClaw Control UI chat. Inline and display LaTeX formulas render correctly. CJK boundary formulas like `$x^2$的值` and `$E=mc^2$，` also render correctly. Streaming path now passes `mathRendering` through to the renderer.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw Control UI dev server (`pnpm dev:ui:mock`) with `mathRendering: "katex"` enabled, tested in Chrome browser on Linux.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
1. Set `mathRendering: "katex"` in mock dev config
2. Run `pnpm dev:ui:mock` to start OpenClaw UI dev server
3. Open Chrome, navigate to `http://localhost:3010`
4. Send chat messages containing LaTeX formulas: `$E=mc^2$`, `$$\int_0^1 f(x)dx$$`, `$x^2$的值`, `$E=mc^2$，以及`
5. Observe rendered output in chat window

**Evidence after fix**: Screenshots from real OpenClaw UI showing correct rendering:

Inline and display math rendering:
![Inline and display math](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85a4a034-b275-4510-8d19-f66b9cf4b03e)

Complex formulas (matrices, integrals, nested):
![Complex formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a75c1c0-50de-4452-a862-fd16e2d27936)

CJK boundary formulas (fullwidth comma, CJK ideograph):
![CJK boundary formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98b80524-6179-4e3a-af53-7f36ea766e51)

Additional formula rendering (fractions, roots, Greek letters):
![Additional formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/650da05c-14e0-4b5d-ab21-79022feadcfb)

**Observed result after fix**: All LaTeX formulas render correctly in the OpenClaw chat interface — inline math, display math, complex nested formulas, and CJK boundary cases. KaTeX CSS loads correctly, no raw LaTeX visible, no red error messages, no layout issues.

**What was not tested**: Actual AI model integration (requires API key). Multi-session switching while KaTeX is loaded. Very long formula chains that might trigger maxSize fallback.

target.title: fix(codex): retire MCP app-server helpers
target.body:
## Summary
- Add shared Codex app-server client leases so active runs keep a shared app-server alive.
- Mark stdio app-server clients for retire-on-idle after turns that projected user MCP servers, closing the app-server process group only after active leases release.
- Cover retire-on-idle behavior in shared-client tests and preserve existing thread unsubscribe cleanup.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: Completed Codex app-server turns that start projected user MCP stdio servers no longer leave the shared app-server process alive indefinitely just to preserve client reuse.

Real environment tested: Ash macOS OpenClaw workspace with the live Codex app-server and Linear MCP configuration from TRR-25.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node integrations/linear-openclaw-bridge/scripts/check-helper-processes.mjs --owner-uid $(id -u) --min-age-seconds 180`, targeted SIGTERM of the reported Ash-owned stale Linear MCP process groups, then `mcporter call linear-stdio.save_comment ...`.

Evidence after fix: Live terminal output from the verifier reported 18 stale Ash-owned Linear MCP helper groups before cleanup, with ages between about 300 and 1047 seconds. After targeted SIGTERM, `ps -u ash` returned no `mcp-remote https://mcp.linear.app/mcp` rows, and `mcporter call linear-stdio.save_comment ...` returned a successful Linear comment JSON payload for TRR-25 comment `b6511717-1291-4a65-b402-d0ff03c1fe23`.

Observed result after fix: The stale Ash-owned Linear MCP helpers were gone, the built-in stale transport failure was reproduced, and a fresh Linear MCP stdio connection successfully posted to TRR-25. The code now retires MCP-backed stdio shared app-server clients after active leases drain, which is the lifecycle point that owns those child MCP helper process groups.

What was not tested: I did not restart a production installed OpenClaw runtime from this branch under concurrent real user traffic; concurrent active-client safety is covered by the shared-client lease tests.

Linear: https://linear.app/picardandelaine/issue/TRR-25/fix-codex-linear-mcp-helper-lifecycle-leaks

target.title: feat(web-search): expose Brave Goggles for custom search filtering and ranking
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Brave Search supports [Goggles](https://search.brave.com/help/goggles), a [query-time DSL](https://brave.com/static-assets/files/goggles.pdf) that redefines how results are ranked at the index level (not client-side post-filtering), but OpenClaw's `web_search` tool has no way to pass them.
- Why it matters: Agents can construct task-specific rules at query time to scope grounding content — e.g. restrict to academic sources, boost official docs over SEO tutorials, suppress low-signal domains — without burning API quota and context window on irrelevant results. The DSL supports site/URL pattern matching (`$site=`, `$inurl=`, wildcards) and ranking actions (`$boost=N`, `$downrank=N`, `$discard`). Rules can be passed inline (newline-separated) or referenced by hosted URL ([quickstart](https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart)). Goggles work on both Brave endpoints OpenClaw uses: web search and [llm-context](https://docs.openclaw.ai/brave-search).
- What changed:
  - Added `goggles` string parameter to the Brave tool schema (`src/agents/tools/web-search.ts`) with a self-contained description including DSL syntax and examples so the agent can use it without reading docs
  - Wired `goggles` through to both web and llm-context URL builders
  - Added Brave-only provider validation (`unsupported_goggles` error for non-Brave providers)
  - Included `goggles` in cache keys (different goggles = separate cache entries)
  - Updated `docs/tools/web.md` with paired Goggles + Perplexity examples for domain filtering
  - Updated `docs/brave-search.md` with parameter table entry and usage notes
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - Per-query only — no config schema changes (goggles is not static config)
  - No news search support (OpenClaw doesn't use that endpoint)
  - No changes to other providers
  - No test infrastructure changes

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `web_search` tool gains an optional `goggles` string parameter (Brave provider only)
- Non-Brave providers return an `unsupported_goggles` error if `goggles` is passed
- Different goggles values produce separate cache entries

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No new endpoints — goggles is appended as a query parameter on existing Brave web search and llm-context API calls.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Minimal — adds one optional string parameter to an existing tool; no new tool or command.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: Brave Search API
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Brave API key configured

### Steps

1. `pnpm build` — no TS errors
2. `pnpm test -- web-search.test` — all 40 tests pass
3. `pnpm check` — clean
4. Live API: inline goggles (`$discard\n$site=docs.rs\n$site=rust-lang.org`) returned only docs.rs and rust-lang.org results on both web search and llm-context endpoints

### Expected

- Build, tests, and format all pass
- Live queries with goggles return results scoped to the specified rules

### Actual

- All pass as expected

### Examples

```
// Restrict to trusted academic sources
goggles: "$discard\n$site=arxiv.org\n$site=*.edu\n$site=huggingface.co"

// Prioritize docs, suppress blog spam
goggles: "/docs/*$boost=5\n/blog/*$downrank=5"

// Boost actual source code on GitHub
goggles: "$boost=5,site=github.com,inurl=/blob/"
```

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: build pass, all 40 web-search tests pass, format clean, live API calls with inline goggles on both web search and llm-context endpoints
- Edge cases checked: non-Brave provider with goggles (returns `unsupported_goggles` error), cache key differentiation with different goggles values
- What you did **not** verify: hosted goggle URL (only inline rules tested)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — new optional parameter, no breaking changes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert the commit; goggles parameter is additive-only
- Files/config to restore: none
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `unsupported_goggles` errors appearing for Brave provider (would indicate provider detection issue)

## Risks and Mitigations

Low — additive optional parameter on existing Brave API calls; no behavior change when goggles is not supplied.
