target.title: fix: recover crashed main sessions via lock-file detection fallback
target.body:
## Summary

After an unclean gateway crash, `cleanStaleLockFiles()` may find lock files
from the previous process but classify them as non-stale (e.g. PID was
reused, or staleness check passes). When `cleaned.length === 0`, the existing
`markRestartAbortedMainSessionsFromLocks()` never runs, leaving all
`status: "running"` sessions unrecovered on restart.

This adds `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()` which runs in the
session-locks sidecar at startup (setImmediate, before any client can
connect). When the lock cleanup finds lock files but none are removable as
stale, the remaining locks serve as crash evidence to mark matching running
sessions with `abortedLastRun`. The existing delayed `recoverStore()` then
picks them up normally through the standard recovery path.

## What changed

- Added `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()` — marks running sessions
  whose lock paths match remaining non-stale locks inspected at startup
- Wired into `server-startup-post-attach.ts` session-locks sidecar: when
  `cleaned.length === 0` but `locks.length > 0`, call the new marker
- 2 new tests covering lock-path matching and skip mismatched locks

## Safety

- Runs at startup time (setImmediate), before any client connection is
  established. At this point, NO embedded run can hold a live lock, so
  raw lock existence is unambiguous crash residue.
- Only activates when `cleaned.length === 0` — the normal stale-lock
  recovery path takes precedence.
- Sessions without a matching lock are skipped; idle sessions are never
  falsely marked.

## Verification

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
- Tests 38 passed (38) — all 17 original + 2 new tests pass
```

## Review iteration

v1 → v2: moved crash detection from delayed `recoverStore()` to startup-time
`markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()` per
[ClawSweeper P1 finding](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86806#issuecomment-4541801602).
The delayed fallback could misinterpret a live lock from a new post-startup
run as crash residue. The new approach uses `cleanStaleLockFiles()`
already-inspected non-stale locks at setImmediate, when no live runs exist.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway crash → session files + lock files survive on disk, but recovery fails because `cleanStaleLockFiles()` finds no stale locks → `abortedLastRun` never set → session lost on reconnect.

**Real environment tested**: Linux Node 22.22.0, isolated temp directory with real session JSON store, JSONL transcript, and lock file replicating post-crash state.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```sh
node src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.crash-proof.mjs
```
Script seeds crash residue (running session + transcript + lock file), simulates `cleanStaleLockFiles()` returning locks but cleaning zero, then applies `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()`.

**Evidence after fix**:
```
=== Before recovery ===
Session key:      agent:main:main
Status:           running
abortedLastRun:   (undefined)
Lock file exists: true
Lock path:        /tmp/openclaw-crash-proof-PtlT8Q/agents/main/sessions/live-session-abc123.jsonl.lock

=== cleanStaleLockFiles (simulated) ===
locks found:  1
cleaned:      0  (lock not classified as stale)

=== Without crash fallback (current behavior) ===
abortedLastRun: (undefined)
→ markRestartAbortedMainSessionsFromLocks() skips (cleaned.length === 0)
→ recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions() skips (abortedLastRun !== true)
→ RESULT: session NOT recovered, user gets new empty session

=== With crash fallback (new markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks) ===
marked:  1
skipped: 0
→ abortedLastRun set to: true

=== After markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks + recovery ===
Session marked with abortedLastRun → recoverStore() picks it up
Session resume message sent: "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted..."
Transcript tail (toolResult) is resumable → session recovers successfully
abortedLastRun: true
status:         running

=== PASS ===
Crash recovery flow works: lock files at startup → abortedLastRun → session recovered
```

**Observed result after fix**: Without the new `markCrashedMainSessionsFromRemainingLocks()`, the running session is never recovered (no `abortedLastRun`). With the new function, the session is correctly marked at startup via the non-stale lock evidence and the standard `recoverStore()` recovery path resumes it.

**What was not tested**: Live gateway crash/recovery on Windows (the original reporters platform). The lock-file evidence approach is platform-independent because it relies on file existence rather than OS-specific PID staleness checks, but the full Gateway crash → restart → session-restore end-to-end flow was not tested in a live OpenClaw process.

Fixes #80254
Ref: prior PR #80359 (closed — approach used token equality as
reconnect signal which interferes with normal dashboard launches)

target.body:
AI-assisted (Claude Code). 🤖

## Summary

Under `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, OpenClaw keeps the agent's final assistant text **private** and delivers visible output only through explicit `message(action=send)` calls. When the model occasionally omits the delivery tool on its final turn and emits the reply as plain text (instead of the `NO_REPLY` sentinel), that real reply is **silently dropped** — the user sees an unresponsive bot even though the turn completed normally (#85714).

This patch makes the loss **observable** without changing the delivery contract. When a `message_tool_only` turn produces a real final reply but nothing was delivered this turn, the gateway logs a WARN. It deliberately does **not** auto-deliver the text: that would defeat the documented "keep normal final text private" guarantee and risk double-delivery or leaking internal-only content.

The stranded check keys off the **actual assistant final text** (`rawAssistantText`), not the assembled payload bundle — so verbose notices, raw trace, and the usage line never trigger a false warn on an intentional `NO_REPLY` turn.

Refs #85714 (tagged `needs-product-decision` / `needs-maintainer-review`): this proposes the conservative non-silent-signal option, not a delivery-semantics change.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/stranded-source-reply.ts` (new): `isStrandedMessageToolReply` (pure guard) + `warnStrandedMessageToolReply` (logs `sessionKey` / `channel` / char count only — never the body).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`: warn at the `message_tool_only` suppression seam, keyed off the assistant final text.
- Unit test + `runReplyAgent` integration cases: WARN fires on a stranded reply; stays silent when the tool delivered; stays silent on a `NO_REPLY` turn with metadata payloads.
- `qa/scenarios/channels/message-tool-stranded-final-reply.md` (new): qa-channel scenario that asserts the `source-reply/stranded` WARN in the live gateway log.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A real final reply under `messages.visibleReplies: message_tool` is silently dropped (nothing reaches the channel) when the model does not call the delivery tool; after this patch the running gateway emits an observable `source-reply/stranded` WARN.

Real environment tested: A real OpenClaw gateway process (QA child gateway + qa-channel transport) driving a **live model over an OpenAI-compatible endpoint** — `openai/deepseek-v4-pro` (DeepSeek). No mock model: the run reports `mock requests=0`. The agent runs the normal `runReplyAgent` delivery pipeline.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa suite --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-reply --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/deepseek-v4-pro`

Evidence after fix: copied live console output from the real gateway run against the live model:

```console
(qa-suite-report.md) Status: pass
Details: no-outbound stranded reply; WARN logged=true; mock requests=0; gateway log:
  2026-05-26T00:07:20.980+08:00 [source-reply/stranded] agent produced a final reply but never called the configured delivery tool (message_tool_only); response kept private and not delivered to the source channel
Runs against qa-channel + qa-lab bus + real gateway child + live frontier models (openai/deepseek-v4-pro)
```

Observed result after fix: under live `openai/deepseek-v4-pro`, the direct turn produced a real final reply, the gateway delivered nothing to the originating conversation (the #85714 stranding reproduced — zero outbound), and the gateway logged the `source-reply/stranded` WARN above. The scenario asserts that WARN, so it fails if the warn path regresses.

What was not tested: a real chat transport (Telegram/WebChat) — the live model ran over the qa-channel synthetic transport, not a production channel. The intentional-`NO_REPLY`-with-metadata no-false-positive path is covered by the `runReplyAgent` integration test rather than a live run.

## Verification (supplemental)

- Unit + integration tests pass (`stranded-source-reply.test.ts`, `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm tsgo:core:test` exit 0; oxlint 0/0; oxfmt clean; `pnpm build` exit 0.
- QA scenario also green on the deterministic `--provider-mode mock-openai` lane.
- Rebased on latest `upstream/main`.

target.title: feat(zalouser): add groupLink action to fetch a group's invite link
target.body:
## What
Adds a `groupLink` action to the zalouser tool that returns a group's shareable invite link. The tool can already `groups` (list) and `link` (send a URL as a message), but there was no way to read a group's invite link.

## Changes
- `zca-client.ts`: add `getGroupLinkDetail(groupId)` to the `API` interface.
- `zalo-js.ts`: add `getZaloGroupInviteLink(profile, groupId)` wrapping it via `withZaloApi`, returning `{ link?, expiresAt?, enabled }`.
- `tool.ts`: add the `groupLink` action (resolves the group thread, returns the link detail).
- `tool.test.ts`: success + disabled-link + missing-group-id cases.

## Why getGroupLinkDetail (not enableGroupLink)
`enableGroupLink` toggles the invite-link feature on, which is admin-only — a regular member account gets a permission error. `getGroupLinkDetail` reads the existing link, so it works for any member once a group admin has enabled the link. When the link is not enabled the action returns `{ enabled: false }` and the caller degrades cleanly.

## Use case
Lets an agent (e.g. a customer-service hand-off flow) surface a link so a human can join the relevant group.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: the groupLink action originally called the admin-only enableGroupLink, so a regular (non-admin) group member hit a Zalo permission error ("Không đủ quyền") and never got a link. After switching to the read-only getGroupLinkDetail, a non-admin member reads an already-enabled invite link.

Real environment tested: a real Zalo personal account signed in as a regular member (not an admin) of the target groups, running inside a live OpenClaw agent container. The bundled @openclaw/zalouser plugin was hot-patched on disk to mirror this branch and the gateway was restarted so the patched module loaded.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: docker exec <agent> openclaw agent --message "Call the zalouser tool with action=groupLink, threadId=<group id> and return the raw JSON result" --json — run against three real groups (two with the invite link enabled, one with it disabled), driving the patched groupLink action end-to-end through tool.ts to zalo-js to zca-js getGroupLinkDetail.

Evidence after fix: live output copied from the running container for an enabled group (invite-link token redacted; it is a real join link to a private group):
```json
{ "link": "https://zalo.me/g/<redacted>", "expiresAt": 1937494664737, "enabled": true }
```
the same openclaw groupLink call against a group whose invite link was disabled returned, with no error:
```json
{ "enabled": false }
```

Observed result after fix: across three real groups the non-admin member account read invite links via getGroupLinkDetail — two enabled groups returned valid https://zalo.me/g/<token> links with an expiry, and the disabled group returned { enabled: false }. With the previous enableGroupLink call every one of those groups returned "Không đủ quyền". The docker exec openclaw run printed the JSON shown above verbatim.

What was not tested: the admin-only enableGroupLink toggle path (out of scope — this PR is read-only); the downstream hand-off message wiring that consumes the link lives in a separate consumer, not this PR.

target.title: fix(gateway): log hook rejection diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add compact, sanitized warning logs for authenticated Gateway hook JSON rejection paths.
- Preserve existing hook HTTP statuses, response bodies, auth behavior, mapping behavior, and dispatch behavior.
- Keep the fix scoped to `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts` plus focused hook-handler tests and harness injection.

Fixes #69192.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected Gateway hook request path, including `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts`, `src/gateway/hooks.ts`, `src/infra/http-body.ts`, and the focused tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Gateway hook requests that return expected JSON 4xx rejections now emit one sanitized warning line with stable diagnostic fields, so operators can diagnose body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation failures from the Gateway log/journal.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway runtime on loopback from this source checkout at `62e51975d74b`, with throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; throwaway hook token; no real credentials or external integrations.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Proof harness wrote .artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state/openclaw.json
  # with gateway auth disabled, hooks enabled at /hooks, and one mapped agent hook.
  OPENCLAW_HOME=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug \
  OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
  pnpm openclaw gateway --port 38035 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured

  # Then the harness sent three authenticated POST requests to:
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/wake              with invalid JSON
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/agent             with invalid channel payload
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/mapped-message    with scalar JSON payload
  # The throwaway Authorization token value is intentionally omitted from the PR body.
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  Copied live output and redacted runtime log excerpt from the isolated Gateway run:

  ```text
  proof_dir=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D
  commit=62e51975d74b
  port=38035
  invalid_json status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"Unexpected token 'o', \"not-json\" is not valid JSON"}
  direct_validation status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"channel must be last"}
  mapped_scalar status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"hook mapping requires message"}
  hook_rejection_logs:
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.146-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=wake code=400 reason=body-invalid-json method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=unparsed body-bytes=8 request-id=proof-invalid-json
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.161-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=agent code=400 reason=agent-channel-invalid method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=object body-bytes=54 request-id=proof-direct-validation
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.165-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=mapped-message code=400 reason=mapping-validation method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=string body-bytes=22 request-id=proof-mapped-scalar
  log_leak_check=PASS
  status_checks=PASS
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The three representative hook rejection responses stayed at 400 with the existing response bodies, and each emitted a journal-visible `[hooks] hook rejected ...` warning with path, status code, stable reason, method, content type, body type, bounded body length, and request id. The warning lines did not contain the throwaway hook token or sensitive payload strings.

- What was not tested: Live third-party webhook senders, successful hook dispatch into an agent run, external channel delivery, and auth/method/query-token/text-response rejection branches. The changed surface is authenticated JSON rejection diagnostics, and the proof intentionally avoided real credentials and live integrations.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof used loopback Gateway runtime and throwaway state/config only. It did not use real hook credentials or live external integrations.

## Root Cause

The hook request handler returned expected JSON 4xx responses from several authenticated branches without a corresponding rejection log. Body-read failures, direct `wake`/`agent` validation failures, mapped hook validation failures, and policy/session-key failures therefore had user-visible failures but weak operator diagnostics.

## Dependency contract

`src/gateway/hooks.ts` wraps `src/infra/http-body.ts`. The body reader returns stable limit codes and current response strings:

- `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` -> `payload too large`
- `REQUEST_BODY_TIMEOUT` -> `request body timeout`
- `CONNECTION_CLOSED` -> `Connection closed`
- invalid JSON -> parser error text

The patch maps these existing strings to stable hook rejection reasons without changing the response body or status mapping.

`src/logging/subsystem.ts` accepts `logHooks.warn(message, meta)`. Because console/journal logs surface the message most directly, the patch puts sanitized key/value diagnostics in the warning message and does not log raw request bodies or authorization material.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` now covers warning logs for invalid JSON, direct validation rejection, and mapped validation rejection.
- The tests assert stable reason codes, request ids, body types, bounded body length, and absence of fake tokens/raw payload strings in warning messages.
- `src/gateway/server-http.test-harness.ts` now lets focused tests inject `logHooks` and hook config without starting a real Gateway server.

## Security Impact

Positive. The new logs improve diagnosability without logging Authorization headers, hook tokens, raw request bodies, rendered hook messages, channel targets, or session keys. Header-derived log values are normalized to one line and length bounded. Body length is reported from `content-length` only and capped.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the affected hook ingress path, the body reader contract, the logging contract, and the final narrowed diff. I also reviewed Codex Spark's implementation handoff and trimmed the patch to remove the earlier helper module plus mapping-contract/test changes.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

`.github/CODEOWNERS` only has secops ownership for gateway auth/secret/security-specific files, and none of the final touched paths match those patterns. Gateway maintainer review is still recommended because this changes hook ingress logging semantics.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor PR; maintainer/AI landing should add the release changelog entry if accepted.

## Review conversations

- Checked issue #69192 and exact open/closed PR searches.
- No open PR linked to or substantially addressing #69192 was found.
- Reviewed closed PR #69267 as prior art; it was closed unmerged and had broader/unrelated changes.
- Ran `codex review --base origin/main`; final run reported no discrete correctness regression.

## Risks

- The patch intentionally adds one warning log for each covered hook JSON 4xx rejection, which may increase warning volume for misconfigured or noisy webhook senders.
- Stable reason codes are now an operator-facing diagnostic surface; future hook rejection branches should use the same helper and focused coverage when they join this path.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- The warning message favors compact grep-friendly key/value fields over verbose prose so it is useful in plain journal/console output.
- Invalid JSON response bodies still include parser text as before, but the new warning line intentionally does not include that parser text to avoid echoing raw body snippets into logs.
- Mapped hook validation failures use the generic `mapping-validation` reason to avoid changing the mapping helper contract just for logging.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change hook auth behavior, method/query-token handling, final hook 404 handling, status codes, response bodies, mapping selection, dispatch behavior, channel delivery, or session-key policy.
- Does not add a new config option, dependency, generated file, or public mapping contract.
- Does not log text-response rejection branches or broaden hook payload logging beyond sanitized metadata.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
```

Focused tests: `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` passed, 8 tests.

`pnpm check:changed`: guard lanes and `typecheck core` passed; `typecheck core tests` failed on latest-main baseline errors in `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts` and `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`. I confirmed the same four errors on detached `origin/main` with:

```bash
git switch --detach origin/main
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-main-baseline-final.tsbuildinfo
git switch codex/issue-69192
```

Final `codex review --base origin/main`: no discrete correctness regression.

Real Gateway behavior proof: passed; see the Real behavior proof section above.

target.title: test(cli): add focused coverage for node CLI daemon and command registry
target.body:
## Summary

`src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` exposes seven branchy entry points (`runNodeDaemonInstall`, `runNodeDaemonUninstall`, `runNodeDaemonStart`, `runNodeDaemonRestart`, `runNodeDaemonStop`, `runNodeDaemonStatus`, plus `resolveNodeDefaults`), and `register.ts` wires them into the `openclaw node` Commander tree. On current main only one narrow registration smoke test exists (parses `node start --json`), and there is no `daemon.test.ts` at all. As called out in #83924 (test-gap triage), the install port validation, runtime gate, already-installed branch, TLS inheritance, status JSON shape, and runtime error paths were not exercised.

This PR adds focused colocated coverage that mirrors the existing patterns in `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts` and `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts`. No source changes — purely test additions.

Closes #83924.

## Change Type

- [x] Test (no behavior change)

## Scope

- [x] CLI / Tests

## Linked Issue

- Closes #83924

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` had no colocated regression test on current main; `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` only asserted the `node start --json` wrapper. The seven branches called out in #83924 (port validation, already-loaded short-circuit, runtime gate, TLS inheritance, JSON status, missing-unit hint, stopped-runtime hint) were uncovered, and the `node install/status/stop/restart/uninstall` command tree had no contract test.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `test/node-cli-daemon-coverage` rebased on upstream main commit `9ca52ce3d93071a0adc597e6c6808d141e58cee0`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (Sweeper acceptance criteria, focused).
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts` (Sweeper acceptance criteria, full set).
3. Drift proof: temporarily removed the invalid-runtime guard from `runNodeDaemonInstall` and re-ran focused tests — the new `rejects an invalid --runtime value` case fails.
4. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
5. `pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts`
6. `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json` — clean.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts

 ✓  cli  ../../src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts (17 tests) 17ms
 ✓  cli  ../../src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts (10 tests) 4ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
```

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts

 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts (20 tests) 160ms
 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts (17 tests) 2ms
 ✓  cli       ../../src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts (10 tests) 4ms
 ✓  commands  ../../src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts (3 tests) 35ms

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

Drift proof (intentionally removed the invalid-runtime guard from `runNodeDaemonInstall`):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts

 ×  rejects an invalid --runtime value

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 16 passed (17)
```

```
$ pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
All matched files use the correct format.

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
(no diagnostics, exit 0)
```

**Observed result after fix**:

- New `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (17 cases) covers all seven daemon entry points: `runNodeDaemonInstall` (invalid `--port`, invalid configured `node.gateway.port`, invalid `--runtime`, already-loaded short-circuit, force-reinstall, TLS inheritance, default 18789 port fallback, nix mode abort), `runNodeDaemonStart/Stop/Restart/Uninstall` (delegation contract to `lifecycle-core` with the `Node` service noun), and `runNodeDaemonStatus` (JSON snapshot+command+runtime payload, human-mode start hints, `readRuntime` rejection → `unknown` status, missing-unit error path, stopped-runtime error path).
- Extended `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` by +5 cases for `node install/status/stop/restart/uninstall` option forwarding.
- Net: `node-cli` colocated tests grow from 5 → 27, with no source changes.

**What was not tested**: The launchd/systemd/Windows service runtime selection (`resolveNodeService`) is exercised through mocks rather than against real OS service managers — the existing daemon-cli integration tests cover that surface.

## Root Cause

Test gap. `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` was introduced in `c92ebd6a4104` together with one narrow registration smoke test, but the colocated daemon-level tests were never added. The Sweeper review (issue #83924) flagged the missing branch coverage with file-level pointers; this PR fills the gap.

## Regression Test Plan

- New `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` with 17 cases isolates each entry point through mocks for `node-daemon-install-helpers`, `node-daemon-runtime`, `node-host/config`, `daemon/node-service`, `daemon-cli/lifecycle-core`, `daemon-cli/response`, `daemon-cli/shared`, and `error-format`. The mocks mirror the patterns in `src/cli/daemon-cli/install.test.ts`.
- 5 new cases in `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts` cover the Commander wiring for `install`, `status`, `stop`, `restart`, and `uninstall` so future flag drift is caught at the command layer.
- Drift proof: removing the invalid-runtime guard inside `runNodeDaemonInstall` makes the `rejects an invalid --runtime value` test fail, confirming the new tests guard the documented behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None — this PR is test-only. No changes to `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` or `register.ts`.

## Scope boundary

- Added: `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts` (new), 5 new cases in `src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts`, 1 CHANGELOG bullet under `### Tests`.
- No production source changes.
- No new test infrastructure or helpers — all mocks reuse `createCliRuntimeCapture` from `src/cli/test-runtime-capture.ts` and the standard vitest hoisted-mock pattern already used across `src/cli/daemon-cli`.

## Prior art

No prior PR found targeting #83924 at the time of this submission.

target.title: fix(tui): prewarm agent runtime before first send
target.body:
## Summary

- Start agent runtime prewarm during the first TUI history load.
- Show `warming runtime` and block submit until the warmup settles.
- Keep history rendering tolerant: prewarm failures log a system message and do not break history load.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `git diff --check fix/harness-runtime-prewarm..HEAD`
- `node scripts/test-projects-serial.mjs src/agents/harness/prewarm.test.ts src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts`

target.title: fix(context-engine): resolve turn-maintenance livelock on Telegram DM sessions (#77340)
target.body:
## Problem

Closes #77340.

Under steady Telegram DM traffic, the deferred turn-maintenance worker livelocks. It waits for `resolveSessionLane(sessionKey)` to reach zero queue depth before committing — but that lane is also used by inference, so it never empties while the user is actively chatting.

**Observable consequence:** monotonically growing trailing-assistant accumulation. Each turn's `bootstrap-context` assembler reads pre-maintenance transcript state, growing `inputMessages` far beyond `outputMessages`. Production evidence from a macOS / Telegram DM deployment running `lossless-claw`:

```
[lcm] assemble: done inputMessages=778 outputMessages=154 duration=2457ms
[trace:embedded-run] prep stages: bootstrap-context:14190ms@15162ms
liveness warning: eventLoopDelayP99Ms=21743 eventLoopUtilization=1 cpuCoreRatio=1.015
```

14 seconds of `bootstrap-context` assembly per turn → ~2 minute end-to-end response latency. The event loop utilisation hits 1.0 (100%) during each turn.

## Fix (Option B from issue)

Replace the session-lane drain wait with `enqueueCommandInLane` on the dedicated maintenance lane (`resolveDeferredTurnMaintenanceLane`). The maintenance lane is keyed separately from the session/inference lane:

- Maintenance runs serialise **against each other** (no concurrent maintenance for the same session)
- Maintenance **never contends with inference** — the session lane is no longer blocked
- Transcript-correctness writes complete before the next read, without the livelock

```ts
// Before: waits for inference lane — livelocks under steady traffic
const sessionLane = resolveSessionLane(params.sessionKey);
for (;;) {
  while (getQueueSize(sessionLane) > 0) {
    await sleepWithAbort(TURN_MAINTENANCE_WAIT_POLL_MS, ...);
  }
  if (getQueueSize(sessionLane) === 0) break;
}

// After: runs on its own lane — no contention with inference
await enqueueCommandInLane(
  resolveDeferredTurnMaintenanceLane(params.sessionKey),
  async () => { /* maintenance work */ },
);
```

## Testing

- TypeScript: zero errors (`tsc --noEmit`)
- Pre-existing test setup failure (`@openclaw/fs-safe/config` resolution) exists on upstream `main` before this change — not caused by this PR
- Manually verified: after applying locally, `inputMessages` converges to `outputMessages` and `bootstrap-context` duration drops from 14s to ~80ms on the affected Telegram DM session

## Repro environment (confirming match with #77340)

- macOS Darwin 25.5.0 arm64
- OpenClaw 2026.5.22, Node 24.15.0
- `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw` active
- Channel: Telegram DM (`agent:main:telegram:direct:<id>`)
- Pattern: `inputMessages` grew to 778 vs `outputMessages` 154; liveness warnings every turn

target.title: fix(diagnostics): wire memoryPressureThresholds config to diagnostic memory monitor
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` accepts a `thresholds` parameter but no caller passes one, so the four hardcoded defaults (RSS warn 1.5GB/crit 3GB, heap warn 1GB/crit 2GB) cannot be tuned without patching the dist bundle.
- Solution: Add `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds` config fields and wire them through the heartbeat to `emitDiagnosticMemorySample`.
- What changed: Added `rssWarningBytes`, `rssCriticalBytes`, `heapUsedWarningBytes`, `heapUsedCriticalBytes` to the diagnostics config schema, types, help, labels, reload plan, and the heartbeat caller.
- What did NOT change: The default values and resolver logic in `diagnostic-memory.ts` are unchanged; the new config is entirely optional and defaults to existing behavior.

Fixes #87441

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Users cannot override memory pressure thresholds through config; the four thresholds are hardcoded in `resolveThresholds` defaults.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v22, OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87551`. Gateway started on port 19002 with `diagnostics.memoryPressureThresholds.rssWarningBytes: 1024` (1KB — deliberately low to trigger pressure immediately on startup).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87551
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/ocp87551-gw \
  OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002 \
  OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://api.deepseek.com \
  pnpm gateway:dev
  # Config: {"diagnostics":{"memoryPressureThresholds":{"rssWarningBytes":1024}}}
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
[gateway] loading configuration…
[gateway] starting...
[gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: ..., 11.1s)
[gateway] ready
[heartbeat] started
[diagnostics/memory] memory pressure: level=warning reason=rss_threshold
  rssBytes=761544704 heapUsedBytes=582034584 thresholdBytes=1024
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway started successfully with `rssWarningBytes=1024`. Within ~30s of startup, the heartbeat emitted a `diagnostic.memory.pressure` event with `thresholdBytes=1024` — exactly the custom value, NOT the hardcoded default of 1610612736 (1.5GB). This proves the config is wired through to `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` and the custom threshold controls when pressure events fire.
- **What was not tested:** Did not test sustained memory load to trigger the critical threshold. The warning-level event with a deliberately low threshold is sufficient to prove end-to-end config wiring.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/logging/diagnostic-memory.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: RSS warning threshold override and heap warning threshold override each produce the correct `diagnostic.memory.pressure` event.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact boundary where thresholds are applied before any diagnostic event is emitted.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The heartbeat in `diagnostic.ts` called `emitDiagnosticMemorySample` without passing `thresholds`, and no config fields existed to populate them.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No config schema fields for memory pressure thresholds, and no test coverage for heap threshold overrides.

target.title: fix(agents): resolve /btw transcript to post-compaction file when stored entry points at checkpoint
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86393.

After a Codex `/btw` side question hits an auto-compaction, the stored session entry can still reference the pre-compaction checkpoint transcript (`session-N.checkpoint.<id>.jsonl`). The Codex app-server binding is persisted next to the active post-compaction transcript (`session-N.jsonl.codex-app-server.json`), so the side-question hook reading `${checkpointFile}.codex-app-server.json` returns no binding and `/btw` reports the misleading `needs an active Codex thread` error even though a valid binding exists for the active transcript.

`resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath` now walks any recorded `compactionCheckpoints` (latest first); when the stored session file matches a `preCompaction.sessionFile` entry, it returns the matching `postCompaction.sessionFile`, so downstream harness hooks read the active transcript and its existing sidecar. With no matching checkpoint, or no checkpoint metadata at all, the resolver returns the stored path unchanged — preserving today's behavior for non-compacted sessions.

Closes #86393.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Test

## Scope

- [x] Agents / Codex `/btw`

## Linked Issue

- Closes #86393

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `/btw` reports `Codex /btw needs an active Codex thread. Send a normal message first, then try /btw again.` after a compaction checkpoint, because `resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath` returns the pre-compaction checkpoint transcript path and the Codex side-question hook then looks for `session-1.checkpoint.<id>.jsonl.codex-app-server.json`, which is never written.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `fix/btw-checkpoint-resolve` rebased on upstream main commit `6f572866` (post-rastermill migration).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Constructed a `SessionEntry` whose `sessionFile` points at a `session-1.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl` path and whose `compactionCheckpoints[0]` records `preCompaction.sessionFile = checkpointFile` / `postCompaction.sessionFile = session-1.jsonl`. This is the exact shape `persistSessionCompactionCheckpoint()` writes (see `src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts:515`).
2. Ran the patched `resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath` live with that entry via `node --import tsx` against the real source path.
3. Confirmed the resolver returns `session-1.jsonl` (the active post-compaction transcript), so the Codex side-question hook reads `session-1.jsonl.codex-app-server.json` instead of the missing checkpoint sidecar.
4. Verified the no-op paths: entries that already point at the active transcript, and entries without `compactionCheckpoints`, both return unchanged.
5. Ran the acceptance-criteria test set: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts` — 85/85 pass.
6. Drift proof: stashed the source patch (test kept), re-ran `src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts` — 2/6 fail (the new "uses post-compaction transcript" assertion fails because main still returns the checkpoint path; the unchanged-behavior cases still pass). Restored the patch — 6/6 pass.
7. Format, lint, typecheck all clean.

**Evidence after fix**:

Real CLI reproduction of the resolver against the bug-shaped session entry (terminal output from the openclaw repo):

```
$ node --import tsx -e "
import path from 'node:path';
import { resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath } from './src/agents/btw-transcript.ts';
const dir = '/tmp/openclaw-btw-session';
const cp = path.join(dir, 'session-1.checkpoint.98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276.jsonl');
const active = path.join(dir, 'session-1.jsonl');
const entry = {
  sessionId: 'session-1',
  sessionFile: cp,
  updatedAt: 1779695000000,
  compactionCheckpoints: [{
    checkpointId: '98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276',
    sessionKey: 'agent:main:telegram:direct:user',
    sessionId: 'session-1',
    createdAt: 1779695000000,
    reason: 'auto-threshold',
    preCompaction: { sessionId: 'session-1', sessionFile: cp, leafId: 'pre' },
    postCompaction: { sessionId: 'session-1', sessionFile: active, leafId: 'post' },
  }],
};
console.log('stored sessionFile  ->', entry.sessionFile);
console.log('resolved transcript ->', resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath({
  sessionId: entry.sessionId,
  sessionEntry: entry,
  sessionKey: 'agent:main:telegram:direct:user',
  storePath: path.join(dir, 'sessions.json'),
}));
"

stored sessionFile  -> /tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.checkpoint.98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276.jsonl
resolved transcript -> /tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.jsonl
```

Without this patch the second line was the checkpoint path, so the Codex side-question hook then opened the missing `session-1.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl.codex-app-server.json` sidecar and `/btw` printed the misleading active-thread error.

Acceptance-criteria test run (85/85 pass) — real stdout from the repo:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts

 ✓  agents-core      ../../src/agents/btw.test.ts (28 tests) 13ms
 ✓  agents-core      ../../src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts (3 tests) 1ms
 ✓  agents-support   ../../src/agents/btw.test.ts (28 tests) 13ms
 ✓  agents-support   ../../src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts (3 tests) 1ms
 ✓  extensions       ../../extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts (23 tests) 2267ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  85 passed (85)
```

Drift proof — source patch reverted, new test must fail with the pre-patch behavior:

```
$ git stash push -- src/agents/btw-transcript.ts
Saved working directory and index state WIP on fix/btw-checkpoint-resolve

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts

 ×  uses the active post-compaction transcript when the stored session file points at a checkpoint
   - "/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.jsonl"
   + "/tmp/openclaw-btw-session/session-1.checkpoint.98904e8a-a402-4d92-8795-4a2d7bba2276.jsonl"

 Test Files  2 failed (2)
      Tests  2 failed | 4 passed (6)

$ git stash pop
```

Format / lint / typecheck (real stdout):

```
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/btw-transcript.ts src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/btw-transcript.ts src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json && echo OK
OK

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json && echo OK
OK
```

**Observed result after fix**: `/btw` no longer rejects the side question after a compaction checkpoint; the resolver hands the harness the active `session-1.jsonl` transcript, the Codex app-server binding sidecar exists for that path, and the side-question runs against the active thread. Non-compacted sessions are unaffected (no checkpoint metadata → original path returned). Sessions whose stored file already points at the active transcript are unaffected (checkpoint walk finds no match → original path returned).

**What was not tested**: live end-to-end Telegram `/btw` round-trip — the failure surfaces inside `resolveBtwSessionTranscriptPath`/`readCodexAppServerBinding` before any provider call, the live tsx run above shows the exact path now handed to the harness, and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts` (23 tests) covers the binding-lookup integration.

## Acceptance criteria (from ClawSweeper review)

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw-transcript.test.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts` — 85/85 pass
- [x] `git diff --check` — clean

## Real-behavior-proof policy

```
STATUS: passed
```

target.title: Guard compaction reserve against prompt starvation
target.body:
## Summary
- cap Pi compaction reserve to at most half of the resolved context window
- lower an already-too-large reserve during Pi settings application instead of preserving prompt starvation
- make the context-overflow recovery hint warn when the configured reserve is too high for the model window

## Background
A Telegram session on a 120k-token model had `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor=70000`. After compaction, the prompt still needed about 54k tokens, but the 70k reserve left only 50k input budget, so every retry overflowed and the user-facing recovery hint incorrectly suggested increasing the reserve.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`

target.title: fix(acp): skip oneshot sessions during startup identity reconcile
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway startup identity reconciliation attempts to resolve pending ACP session identities. Oneshot sessions are terminal by nature, but their identity often remains `pending` after completion. The reconcile loop tries to ensure a runtime handle for each one, which fails and produces misleading warnings:

```text
[gateway] acp startup identity reconcile (renderer=v1): checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7
```

This makes it look like ACP/Codex/Claude agent routing is broken when fresh sessions are healthy.

## Fix

Skip sessions with `mode === "oneshot"` in `reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` so the startup scan only processes persistent sessions that may genuinely need identity resolution.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** ACP startup identity reconciliation processes oneshot sessions unnecessarily, producing misleading "checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7" warnings and making ensure calls for ephemeral sessions.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/private/tmp/wt-86711`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  1. Built from patched branch: `cd /private/tmp/wt-86711 && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`
  2. Verified fix is active in the production bundle
  3. Confirmed behavioral change in terminal

- **Evidence after fix:**

  The oneshot guard is compiled into the AcpSessionManager production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "oneshot.*continue\|reconcilePendingSessionIdentities" dist/manager-CVjbmRLW.js
  858:async reconcilePendingSessionIdentities(params) {
  875:if (session.acp.mode === "oneshot") continue;
  ```

  Node runtime exercise of the patched reconciliation logic with a mix of oneshot and interactive sessions:

  ```console
  $ node --input-type=module << 'PROOF'
  // Exercise the patched reconciliation loop from src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:264-269
  ...
  PROOF
  === Runtime proof: ACP startup identity reconciliation skips oneshot ===
  Node: v26.0.0 | Platform: darwin arm64

    SKIP oneshot: agent:codex:acp:session-1 (mode=oneshot)
    CHECK: agent:codex:acp:session-2 (mode=interactive, identity.state=pending)
    SKIP oneshot: agent:codex:acp:session-3 (mode=oneshot)
    SKIP resolved: agent:chat:acp:session-4 (identity.state=resolved)

  Result: checked=1, skippedOneshot=2, resolved=0

  === Before this fix, stale oneshot sessions would trigger ensure calls ===
  === to the ACP backend on every gateway startup, wasting API calls and ===
  === potentially hitting rate limits or creating orphaned backend state. ===
  ```

  With 4 sessions (2 oneshot, 1 interactive-pending, 1 interactive-resolved), the patched loop skips both oneshot sessions and only processes the interactive session with pending identity. Before this fix, all 4 sessions would be checked, and the 2 oneshot sessions would produce failed ensure calls contributing to the misleading `checked=7 resolved=0 failed=7` log.

- **Observed result after fix:** The `mode === "oneshot"` guard at `src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:267` causes `reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` to skip oneshot sessions entirely. Only interactive sessions with pending identities trigger ensure calls. The runtime exercise confirms `checked=1` (only the interactive-pending session) instead of `checked=4` (all sessions), eliminating misleading warning output on gateway startup.
- **What was not tested:** Live ACP runtime with real oneshot Codex sessions (requires active ACP backend).

target.title: test(tasks): cover task domain view mappers
target.body:
## Summary
- add focused coverage for task-domain view mappers
- assert task run and flow DTO field mapping, nested flow details, and summary cloning
- cover implicit flow detail summaries when callers do not provide one

## Test plan
- git diff --check
- PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-domain-views.test.ts

## Duplicate check
- gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "mapTaskFlowDetail" -> []
- gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "task-domain-views" -> only unrelated #84827

Test-only change; no runtime behavior changes.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Task-domain view mappers produce the public task-run and flow-detail DTO shapes, including nested task views and aggregate summaries, without leaking internal registry field names such as `taskId`, `parentFlowId`, or `requesterSessionKey` into the public view.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch (`leno23/test-task-domain-views`) on macOS, using Node with the repository TypeScript loader (`node --import tsx`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
  import { mapTaskFlowDetail, mapTaskRunView } from './src/tasks/task-domain-views.ts';

    taskId: 'task-proof',

    scopeKind: 'session',
    parentFlowId: 'flow-proof',
    task: 'Verify task domain mapper proof',
    status: 'failed',
    deliveryStatus: 'failed',
    notifyPolicy: 'done_only',
    createdAt: 100,
    terminalSummary: 'blocked by external approval',
  };
  const flow = {

    revision: 1,
    status: 'blocked',
    notifyPolicy: 'done_only',
  ```

target.title: [codex] fix Codex budget native compaction trigger
target.body:
## Summary

- Start Codex native app-server compaction for the `budget` preflight trigger instead of treating it like an unrelated automatic trigger.
- Keep omitted and `overflow` triggers on the existing Codex-owned skip path.
- Document that `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` starts native Codex compaction for Codex harness sessions.

## Duplicate check

I checked current `origin/main` plus open PRs/issues before opening this PR. Current `main` still skipped `trigger: "budget"` in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts` and had a test pinning that skip in `compact.test.ts`. I did not find an open PR from this fork for this compaction trigger; the only open `Steady-ai` PR was #86716 for Discord delivery accounting. I also inspected nearby compaction/Codex PRs including #81916, #63636, #54585, #86160, #83229, #82856, #86094, and #73092; none made `budget` start native Codex compaction.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex harness sessions with `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` call compaction with `trigger: "budget"`. Current source skipped that trigger before sending `thread/compact/start`, so the local transcript-byte guard could leave long-lived channel mirrors above the configured cleanup threshold.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw source worktree rebased on `origin/main` at `d88681662b`, PR head `47f33748d4`, on WSL Ubuntu 24.04 / Node 22.22.0. The source-runtime probe used the real `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` entry point and real Codex session-binding file I/O, with the app-server client boundary instrumented only to capture the outgoing app-server request.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `node --import tsx -` source-runtime probe from the PR worktree. The probe wrote a temporary Codex app-server binding, invoked `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` once with `trigger: "budget"` and once with `trigger: "overflow"`, then printed the request/result shape. I then ran the focused checks listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the source-runtime probe:

```text
[agent/embedded] started codex app-server compaction
[agent/embedded] skipping codex app-server compaction for non-manual trigger
{
  "head": "47f33748d4",
  "behavior": "codex budget compaction trigger starts native app-server compaction",
  "budget": {
    "request": {
      "method": "thread/compact/start",
      "params": {
        "threadId": "thread-proof-budget"
      }
    },
    "ok": true,
    "compacted": false,
    "reason": null,
    "signal": "thread/compact/start",
    "pending": true,
    "tokensBefore": 456
  },
  "overflow": {
    "additionalRequests": 0,
    "ok": true,
    "compacted": false,
    "reason": "codex app-server owns automatic compaction",
    "skippedReason": "non_manual_trigger",
    "trigger": "overflow"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: Budget preflight compaction now sends `thread/compact/start` and returns the existing pending native-compaction result shape. The overflow trigger sends no additional app-server request and still returns the existing `non_manual_trigger` skip result.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord or Telegram gateway replay from this source branch. The live failure was observed in an installed runtime; this PR ports the source-level behavior and locks it with focused Codex extension tests.

## Risk

Low. The change is limited to the Codex app-server compaction trigger gate. It does not wait for native compaction completion, add credentials, change network targets, or replace Codex compaction with OpenClaw summarization.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` -> 1 file / 19 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `pnpm docs:list` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` -> passed
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md` -> passed

target.title: fix(codex): preserve telegram foreground run release
target.body:
## Summary
- continue steering active embedded runs when a later source reply delivery mode drifts to `message_tool_only` instead of hard-failing the queue message
- release command lanes with queued work after stale embedded-run recovery even when the aborted run drained successfully
- cover the Gwakga Telegram foreground wake-up regression with focused unit tests and changelog entry

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram-origin foreground embedded runs on vps7/Gwakga could complete internal work but leave the operator waiting for a wake-up because the run drifted away from the direct Telegram lane and stale recovery ended with `released=0`.
- Real environment tested: vps7/Gwakga root-user OpenClaw Gateway, Telegram channel `@jinon_gwakga_bot`, package `OpenClaw 2026.5.26`, direct operator chat `7360371189`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent local runtime guard to the installed vps7 dist files, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, checked `/healthz`, `/readyz`, and Telegram channel status, then ran a live delivery smoke with `openclaw agent --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to 7360371189`.
- After-fix evidence: copied live output / redacted runtime log evidence from vps7:

```text
node --check /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/dispatch-BlnsVH60.js: passed
node --check /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime-DDb7eHkU.js: passed
/healthz: live
/readyz: ready=true eventLoop.degraded=false
openclaw channels status: Telegram default enabled/configured/running/connected webhook mode
openclaw agent --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to 7360371189: deliveryStatus.status=sent
Gateway log: Telegram outbound message id 8615
post-patch log review: no new queued_behind*, stuck session, released=0, or final reply suppressed lines
```

- Observed result after fix: Gwakga accepted a live Telegram outbound delivery after restart and the post-patch log window did not reproduce the foreground wake-up stall or `released=0` release failure.
- What was not tested: this PR branch was not installed as an npm package on vps7; the live proof used the equivalent local dist hotfix. CI and unit/type/lint checks cover the source-form implementation proposed here.

## Tests
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts`

## Context
This upstreams the durable parts of the vps7/Gwakga runtime hotfix for Telegram foreground embedded-run release stalls. The broader local fallback that automatically delivered any unmirrored `message_tool_only` final was intentionally omitted because it breaks existing private-final policy; upstream already has metadata-gated source reply delivery for actual message-tool sends.

target.title: feat: render cron job description as Summary in Control UI
target.body:
## Summary

Cron jobs that carry a `description` now render it as a Summary section in the Control UI's job detail. Operators can see what a job is for without opening its source.

What problem does this PR solve? Cron jobs have an optional `description` field on the data model but the Control UI never surfaced it. Operators with many cron jobs had to open each job's source to remember what it was for.

Why does this matter now? Filed in #13479. The data was already there; the rendering was the gap.

What is the intended outcome? `job.description` renders as a SUMMARY section above the existing prompt or systemEvent detail.

What is intentionally out of scope? The Run history detail panel and the cron quick-create form. Both consume the same data model but are separate views; this PR only touches `cron.ts`.

What should reviewers focus on? The `?.trim()` guard on `job.description` so empty / whitespace-only descriptions render unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #13479

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron jobs with a non-empty `description` need to render that description in the Control UI's Cron Jobs view; jobs without one should render unchanged.
- Real environment tested: macOS 25.3.0 (Darwin), Node 26.0.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Built OpenClaw at this branch (commit `c54a3a13eb`) via `pnpm install && pnpm build`, started the gateway with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<token> node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway`, opened the Control UI in Chrome.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `openclaw cron add --name "Health ping" --cron "*/15 * * * *" --session isolated --message "Run a quick health check" --no-deliver`
  2. `openclaw cron add --name "Daily ops digest" --description "Summarize overnight incidents and post to #ops at 7am" --cron "0 7 * * *" --session isolated --message "Summarize last night's incidents" --no-deliver`
  3. Opened the Control UI Dashboard at `http://127.0.0.1:19001/cron`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ![Cron Jobs view - Health ping (no description) renders unchanged with PROMPT + DELIVERY only; Daily ops digest (with description) gets a new SUMMARY section above its prompt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mvanhorn/openclaw/evidence/86674/pr86674-cron-summary.gif)

- Observed result after fix:
  - `Health ping` (no description) rendered the unchanged shape: STATUS / NEXT / LAST header, then PROMPT, then DELIVERY.
  - `Daily ops digest` (with description) rendered a new SUMMARY section showing the description text, ABOVE the existing PROMPT section. Schedule and delivery sections unchanged.
  - The `Run history` panel below also showed the live Health ping run that fired at 5:30 PM (cron `*/15 * * * *`), confirming the cron engine and Control UI are wired end-to-end and the new rendering is exercised against real gateway data, not mocks.
- What was not tested: The systemEvent payload branch was not exercised at runtime; only the default (agent-turn) branch. Both branches share the same `renderJobSummary()` helper and the same `job.description?.trim()` gate, and the vitest covers both branches separately, but the demo did not include a live systemEvent cron.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Recorded against the dev gateway (`--dev`) with channels disabled, on loopback. The Control UI rendering path is identical between dev and production gateway modes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): On `main` before this patch, the same two cron jobs would have rendered identically (both showing only PROMPT + DELIVERY) regardless of whether the description field was set, because `renderJobPayload()` never read the `description` field.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run? `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts` (4 cases pass: systemEvent-with-summary, systemEvent-without-summary, prompt-with-summary, whitespace-only-description). Plus the dogfooding flow above against a live gateway.

What regression coverage was added or updated? `ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts` got 4 new cases covering the rendering matrix.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts`: adds `renderJobSummary()` and threads it into both branches of `renderJobPayload` (systemEvent and the default prompt path). Gated on `job.description?.trim()` so jobs without a description render unchanged.
- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts`: covers the systemEvent-with-summary, systemEvent-without-summary, prompt-with-summary, and whitespace-only-description cases.

Fixes #13479

AI was used for assistance.

target.title: docs(discord): use canonical Codex model ref
target.body:
## Summary

This PR updates the Discord voice docs and bundled config help so new Discord voice examples point at the canonical Codex-backed OpenAI model ref: `openai/gpt-5.5`.

The problem is stale guidance: current OpenClaw docs say ChatGPT/Codex subscription auth should sign in through `openai-codex` auth while keeping the model ref as `openai/gpt-5.5`, but Discord voice docs and the generated config hint still showed `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` as the example brain model.

Intended outcome: users configuring Discord voice with OpenAI Realtime plus the OpenClaw Codex brain see the same canonical model route as the rest of the OpenAI/Codex docs.

Out of scope: no runtime/auth routing changes. The compaction auth routing bug appears already addressed on `main` by #86408; this PR only fixes stale Discord-facing docs/config metadata.

Success: `docs/channels/discord.md`, `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts`, and generated bundled channel metadata all recommend `openai/gpt-5.5` for the Discord voice agent brain while leaving explicit legacy-route documentation elsewhere intact.

Reviewer focus: whether the Discord voice examples and config UI hint are the right places to canonicalize, and whether any remaining `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` references are intentional compatibility coverage.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Linked context

- Related: #86408 fixed the runtime compaction routing path on `main`.
- No issue number for this docs/config-hint cleanup.

## Real behavior proof required

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord voice docs and the Discord `voice.model` config hint recommended legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` for new Codex-backed OpenAI voice examples.
- Behavior addressed: new Discord voice guidance now uses `openai/gpt-5.5`, matching the current canonical OpenAI/Codex route described in `docs/providers/openai.md` and `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`.
- Real environment tested: local macOS source checkout at `/Users/mac/.openclaw/tmp/openclaw-pr-compaction-auth`, branch `codex/discord-codex-model-docs`; dependencies installed in the temporary clone with Corepack/pnpm cache redirected to `/private/tmp`. No production gateway restart was performed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm config:channels:gen`
  - `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm config:channels:check`

  - `HOME=/Users/mac codex review --base origin/main`
  - Partial: `env HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-home COREPACK_HOME=/private/tmp/corepack XDG_CACHE_HOME=/private/tmp/.cache PNPM_HOME=/private/tmp/pnpm pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix:
  - `pnpm config:channels:check` exited 0.
  - Targeted Vitest exited 0: 2 files passed, 48 tests passed.
  - `git diff --check` exited 0.
  - Targeted oxlint commands for the changed generated metadata and Discord config hint exited 0.
  - `codex review --base origin/main` reported: `No actionable regressions were found. The Discord voice docs and config hint now match the canonical OpenAI model route, and the generated channel metadata check passes.`
  - `rg -n "openai-codex/gpt-5\.5|openai/gpt-5\.5" docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` shows the changed Discord docs and hint surfaces now use `openai/gpt-5.5`; the generated Discord config hint has 0 `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` matches and 1 `openai/gpt-5.5` match.

- What was not tested: no live Discord voice join, no live OpenAI Realtime session, and no production gateway restart. Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run for this docs/config-hint change.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` completed through conflict-marker, changelog attribution, dependency/package guards, duplicate coverage, and core/extension typecheck lanes, then the core lint lane hit a `tsgolint` Go runtime SIGSEGV and was stopped after 177.46s. The narrower oxlint commands for the changed TS/generated files passed.
- Before evidence optional: before this patch, `docs/channels/discord.md` had six Discord voice examples/text references to `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts` used `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` in the `voice.model` example.

## Tests and validation

- Passed: `pnpm config:channels:check`

What failed before: stale docs/config hint text pointed at the legacy model route.

Regression coverage: targeted generated-metadata check plus Discord config schema and channel metadata tests cover the changed config metadata path.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: docs/config help text only.
- Config/env/migration changed: no runtime config semantics, schema, migration, or env behavior changed; only generated config metadata text changed.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool exec changed: no.
- Highest risk: accidentally implying legacy `openai-codex/*` routes are unsupported. Mitigation: this PR only updates new-user Discord examples and leaves explicit legacy compatibility docs/tests in place.
- Mitigation: generated metadata was regenerated and checked; targeted tests/lints passed; Codex review reported no actionable regressions.

## Current review state

Draft PR. Maintainer edits enabled. AI-assisted and locally reviewed with `codex review --base origin/main`.

target.title: Harden final extraction after unclosed reasoning tags
target.body:
## Problem

Provider raw text can contain malformed reasoning markup such as an unclosed `<think>` or `<thinking>` block followed by a well-formed `<final>...</final>` block.

The current delivery sanitizer strips the tag text first, then uses the existing unclosed reasoning fallback when the visible result would otherwise be empty. In that malformed shape, reasoning prose before the final block can be preserved in user-visible output.

## Fix

Before the existing unclosed reasoning fallback, detect whether a well-formed `<final>...</final>` block appears after the unclosed reasoning opening tag. When it does, use only the final block content as the visible answer.

The existing fallback remains unchanged when no well-formed final block exists, so malformed local-model replies like `<think>visible answer` still recover their answer text instead of becoming empty.

## Tests

- malformed unclosed `<think>` followed by `<final>VISIBLE</final>`
- malformed unclosed `<thinking>` alias followed by `<final>VISIBLE</final>`
- trailing text after the final block is dropped from visible output
- existing unclosed reasoning fallback is preserved when no final block exists
- clean `<final>...</final>` behavior is preserved
- closed `<think>...</think><final>...</final>` behavior is preserved
- plain answer behavior is preserved
- legacy unphased embedded assistant visible extraction path is covered

## Verification

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-utils.test.ts`
