target.title: fix(agents): preserve delivery context through nested ACP completions
target.body:
## Summary

- Recover delivery channel from requester session entry when nested ACP completion origins lose their external channel after `stripNonDeliverableChannelForCompletionOrigin` strips a non-deliverable (internal) channel and neither `directOrigin` nor `requesterSessionOrigin` provide a deliverable fallback.
- Add `[warn]` log when completion delivery context resolves to no deliverable channel, making silent message loss detectable in gateway logs.
- Enrich `message-tool-only` delivery failure error with channel and requester info for easier debugging.

## Context

Addresses the remaining sub-issues from #67502 that were explicitly out of scope in PR #67785 (which fixed the global nested lane congestion):

1. **Delivery context corruption** — the session's delivery channel silently degraded after nested ACP runs, causing responses to route to the wrong target or be silently dropped.
2. **No detectability** — when delivery failed due to missing channel context, nothing was logged.

## Real behavior proof

**Before fix** — gateway logs from production Telegram session showing repeated delivery failures with no channel info:

```
2026-05-27T01:09:19 [warn] Subagent completion direct announce failed for run 2631a265: completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery
2026-05-27T01:09:22 [warn] Subagent completion direct announce failed for run 2631a265: completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery
2026-05-27T01:09:22 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=2631a265 child=agent:main:subagent:c3f717e9 requester=agent:main:telegram:direct:REDACTED retries=3 endedAgo=30s deliveryError="completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"
```

The agent generated a response (`stopReason: "stop"`, visible in session transcript) but it never reached Telegram. This repeated across multiple runs throughout the night:

```
2026-05-27T03:25:22 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=b4c4e6ad ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"
2026-05-27T04:55:49 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=e26ef1d1 ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"  
2026-05-27T05:57:48 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=9d376781 ... deliveryError="announce deferred or direct delivery failed"
2026-05-27T08:28:26 [warn] Subagent announce give up (retry-limit) run=ac1ffaa0 ... deliveryError="completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"
```

**After fix** — the enriched error now includes channel and requester info for debugging:

```
completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery; channel=telegram requester=agent:main:telegram:direct:REDACTED
```

And the new warning log fires when no deliverable channel is found, before the delivery attempt even starts:

```
[warn] Subagent completion delivery context has no deliverable channel; response may be lost. requester=... completionChannel=none directChannel=none sessionChannel=none entryChannel=telegram
```

**Environment:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 24.16.0, OpenClaw gateway (local mode) with Telegram channel, Claude Sonnet 4.6 via claude-cli.

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts
# 71/71 passed

pnpm build
# ✓ built successfully

pnpm check:changed
# passed

pnpm changed:lanes --json
# core: true, coreTests: true
```

Behavior addressed: nested ACP completion delivery context preservation
Real environment tested: macOS, Node 24, OpenClaw gateway with Telegram channel
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (71/71 pass)
Evidence after fix: delivery channel recovered from session entry when effective origin has no channel; enriched error messages include channel info
Observed result after fix: all 71 tests pass including 3 new regression tests
What was not tested: live end-to-end Telegram delivery with a real nested ACP run that triggers the internal-channel stripping path (requires specific subagent spawn configuration)

Closes remaining sub-issues from #67502.

target.title: feat(skills): add SkillRouter abstraction for pre-routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Introduces a pluggable `SkillRouter` interface that allows plugins to pre-filter skills before the system prompt is built
- When a router is configured and matches, the narrowed result replaces the full `<available_skills>` catalog in the system prompt, protecting prefix cache
- Core only defines the protocol; it does not bind to any routing strategy
- Aligns router candidates with the same prompt-visible skill state used for the catalog, so disabled skills and agent skill filters cannot leak into routing
- Skips skill routing for raw model runs and restrictive `toolsAllow` runs, while preserving routing for wildcard `toolsAllow: ["*"]` runs that existing tool policy treats as unrestricted

This is a draft implementation of the [SkillRouter Abstraction Layer Proposal](https://github.com/lanzhi-lee/openclaw/blob/self/skill-router-abstraction/xiaomi/proposal/skill-router-abstraction-proposal-en.md).

## Design

**Router interface** (`router-types.ts`):
```typescript
interface SkillRouter {
  readonly name: string;
  route(query: string, candidates: SkillForPrompt[]): Promise<SkillRouteResult>;
}
```

**4-way return** (`router-integration.ts`):
- `{ xml, mode }` — valid match, inject into user prompt
- `{ mode: "nomatch" }` — router said no skill applies, suppress full catalog
- `{ error, reason }` — router failed, fall back to full catalog
- `undefined` — no router configured, no candidates; use full catalog as-is

**Plugin registration** (`router-registry.ts`):
```typescript
registerSkillRouter("my-router", (config) => new MyRouter(config));
```

**Configuration**:
```json
{ "skills": { "router": { "name": "my-router", "config": {} } } }
```

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `router-types.ts` | SkillRouter interface, SkillRouteResult discriminated union |
| `router-registry.ts` | registerSkillRouter / resolveSkillRouter |
| `router-integration.ts` | Pure resolveSkillRoute with 4-way return; candidates now come from prompt-visible resolved skills only |
| `attempt.ts` | Pre-routing + prompt injection with error fallback; raw model runs and restrictive tool allowlists skip routing |
| `attempt-tool-construction-plan.ts` | Shared helper for existing restrictive runtime tool allowlist semantics |
| `skill-contract.ts` | SkillForPrompt type, widened formatSkillsForPrompt |
| `workspace.ts` | Shared prompt state helper returning both prompt and filtered resolved skills |
| `types.skills.ts` | router config in SkillsConfig |
| `skills-runtime.ts` | plugin-sdk exports |

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/skills.resolveskillspromptforrun.test.ts` — 4 files passed, 142 tests passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: router candidate selection now matches the same filtered skill set shown in `<available_skills>`; raw model probe runs do not invoke skill routing; restrictive runtime `toolsAllow` runs use minimal prompt, suppress the skills catalog, and skip router execution; wildcard `toolsAllow: ["*"]` keeps the existing unrestricted behavior and can still route skills.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout with focused Vitest coverage.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt-tool-construction-plan.test.ts src/agents/skills.resolveskillspromptforrun.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests cover snapshot state, entries fallback, hidden skill filtering, agent skill filters, raw-run routing suppression, restrictive `toolsAllow` routing suppression for non-empty and empty allowlists, catalog suppression for restricted runs, and wildcard `toolsAllow` preserving routing.

Observed result after fix: Vitest reported 4 passed files / 142 passed tests; diff check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: no live plugin-provided router implementation yet; short-prompt routing with bounded recent conversation context remains a design follow-up.

## Next Steps

- [ ] Community alignment on the abstraction design
- [ ] Implement a reference router (e.g., token matching, keyword-based)
- [ ] Add router diagnostic events
- [ ] Document plugin integration guide and refresh plugin SDK API baseline before merge
- [ ] Design bounded routing context for short prompts, using current prompt plus a small sanitized recent conversation window

target.title: feat(ui): render image blocks in tool output cards
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tool output messages containing `image` content blocks are not rendered in the Control UI tool cards — images in tool results are invisible to the user.
- Solution: Extract image URLs from tool result content blocks (`type: "image"`) and include them in the tool card output, supporting direct URLs, inline base64 data, and structured `{ source: { type: "base64", data, media_type } }` objects.
- What changed: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` (image extraction + rendering), `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts` (preserve image blocks), `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` (stream handling), `ui/src/styles/chat/tool-cards.css` (styling), `ui/src/ui/types/chat-types.ts` (types).
- What did NOT change: No new plugins, no dependency changes, no lockfile modifications, no config changes. This is a UI-only renderer fix.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Tool output messages with `image` content blocks are invisible in the Control UI tool cards.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22, worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87548`. Directly called `extractToolCards()` via `npx tsx` with a tool message containing three image blocks in different source formats.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87548
  npx tsx -e "
  import { extractToolCards } from './ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts';
  const msg = { role: 'tool', content: [
    { type: 'text', text: 'Generated chart:' },
    { type: 'image', url: 'https://example.com/chart.png' },
    { type: 'image', data: 'iVBORw0KGgo=', mimeType: 'image/png' },
    { type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', data: 'R0lGODlh...', media_type: 'image/gif' } }
  ]};
  console.log(JSON.stringify(extractToolCards(msg)));
  "
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
$ npx tsx proof_ui.mjs

Tool: tool
Images extracted: 3
  https://example.com/chart.png
  data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo=
  data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7
Output text: Generated chart:...
```
- **Observed result after fix:** All three image source formats are correctly extracted and converted to displayable URLs:
  1. Direct `url` field → kept as-is
  2. Inline `data` + `mimeType` → converted to `data:image/png;base64,...`
  3. Structured `source.data` + `source.media_type` → converted to `data:image/gif;base64,...`
- **What was not tested:** Did not capture a browser screenshot of the rendered Control UI (requires a running gateway with a tool that produces real image output).

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test (existing)
- Target test file: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Image content blocks in tool messages are extracted and included in the tool card output array.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The `extractToolImages` function handles all three image source formats used by the OpenClaw protocol (direct URL, inline base64, structured source).

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The tool card builder in `tool-cards.ts` only extracted text from tool output messages, ignoring `image` content blocks entirely.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No image extraction logic existed for tool output content blocks.

target.title: feat(volcengine): add allowPrivateNetwork option for TTS behind TUN proxy
target.body:
## Summary
Adds `allowPrivateNetwork` boolean option to Volcengine TTS provider to support TUN proxy stacks that use RFC 2544 fake-IP ranges for DNS resolution.

**Security approach (revised from original PR):** Instead of passing the broad `allowPrivateNetwork: true` SSRF flag (which would open all of RFC 1918), this PR uses two narrow policy bits already in `SsrfPolicy`:
- `allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange: true` — opens 198.18.0.0/15 (RFC 2544, the fake-IP range used by Surge/Clash/sing-box)
- `allowIpv6UniqueLocalRange: true` — opens IPv6 ULA (same use case)

Full RFC 1918 space (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x) and metadata addresses remain blocked.

## Motivation
When running behind a TUN proxy (Surge, Clash, sing-box) that uses fake-IP DNS (198.18.0.0/15), the SSRF guard blocks the resolved Volcengine API hostname. This makes TTS fail silently.

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/volcengine/tts.ts` | Add `allowPrivateNetwork` to both `seedSpeechTTS` and `legacyVolcengineTTS`; spread narrow fake-IP bits conditionally in `policy` |
| `extensions/volcengine/speech-provider.ts` | Read flag from config, propagate through `readProviderConfig`, forward to `volcengineTTS()` |

## Behavior proof

### Real-world reproduction

Cherry voice (OpenClaw STT-TTS) behind Surge TUN proxy on macOS. Surge uses fake-IP DNS; API hostnames resolve to 198.18.x.x. Without `allowPrivateNetwork`, the SSRF guard blocked the outbound TTS call.

After setting `allowPrivateNetwork: true` in the Cherry voice config, TTS resumed:

```
# gateway.log — 2026-05-16
[reload] config change detected (channels.discord.accounts.cherry.voice.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork)
[reload] config hot reload applied
```

### Unit test proof (2026-05-29 addition — all 4 pass)

New tests in `volcengineTTS > allowPrivateNetwork SSRF policy` (`tts.test.ts`):

```
✓ seed speech: omits fake-IP policy bits when allowPrivateNetwork is false (default)
✓ seed speech: adds narrow fake-IP policy bits when allowPrivateNetwork is true
✓ legacy TTS: omits fake-IP policy bits when allowPrivateNetwork is false (default)
✓ legacy TTS: adds narrow fake-IP policy bits when allowPrivateNetwork is true
```

These tests confirm:
- Default (`false`): `allowRfc2544BenchmarkRange` and `allowIpv6UniqueLocalRange` are NOT present in policy
- Opt-in (`true`): both narrow bits ARE present; the broad `allowPrivateNetwork` flag is NOT present (full RFC 1918 stays blocked)

## Test plan
- [x] Default: SSRF guard blocks fake-IP range (policy does not contain fake-IP bits)  
- [x] Opt-in: only 198.18.0.0/15 + IPv6 ULA opened; RFC 1918 metadata addresses remain blocked
- [x] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [x] Lint passes (oxlint)

🤖 Updated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): isolate RTL lines on outbound assistant text
target.body:
## Summary

- Extract the existing TUI-only `applyRtlIsolation` helper into a shared module so the canonical outbound assistant-visible text sanitizer can reuse it.
- Apply U+2067/U+2069 line-level isolation in the `delivery` profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, so Hebrew/Arabic/other RTL prose delivered through Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Telegram, and any other channel plugin keeps trailing LTR punctuation on the visually-correct side.
- Keep the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles byte-for-byte with the model output (transcript storage and runtime inspection stay clean); keep the TUI behavior unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #68105

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a queueable source-reproducible fix with the existing TUI implementation as the precedent.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Outbound delivery sanitizer drops bidi isolation that the TUI already applies, so Hebrew/Arabic text such as `שלום?` reaches Discord/Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp without RLI/PDI wrappers and renders trailing LTR punctuation on the wrong visual side.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran two local OpenClaw source smokes with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real shared sanitizers, once with the patch temporarily stashed on the same branch and once with it restored.
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from those local OpenClaw source smokes.

  Before-fix (patch stashed):

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR (RTL bidi missing on outbound) ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText  : \"⁧שלום?⁩\" wraps? true
  Outbound sanitizeVisibleText: \"שלום?\" wraps? false
  bug repro confirmed: true
  ```

  After-fix (patch restored):

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText still wraps: \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery now wraps:            \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery (Arabic):             \"⁧كيف حالك؟⁩\"
  History profile stays byte-clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Internal-scaffolding stays clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Idempotent (delivery twice === once):   true
  Mixed LTR/RTL outbound:
  \"Hello there.\n⁧مرحبا!⁩\nSee you.\"
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The outbound sanitizer now wraps RTL-script lines line-by-line; LTR-only lines pass through unchanged; mixed-direction text wraps only the RTL lines; the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles intentionally remain byte-for-byte with the model output; the function is idempotent against already-isolated text.
- What was not tested: A live screenshot from Discord/Slack/Telegram showing the visual punctuation move. The fix is at the sanitizer boundary that those channels already call, so the proof above demonstrates the byte-level change at the exact seam those clients consume.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real OpenClaw shared sanitizers (`sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, `sanitizeAssistantVisibleTextWithProfile`, `sanitizeRenderableText`) imported from the source checkout, but does not run a full channel send because this environment is not configured with messaging credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured above via `git stash`-ing this patch.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.ts src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `rtl-isolation.test.ts` + `assistant-visible-text.test.ts`: `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 111 passed (111)` (6 new RTL isolation cases + 5 new delivery-vs-history profile cases land alongside the existing assistant-visible-text and tool-call XML coverage).
- `tui-formatters.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 47 passed (47)` — TUI rendering behavior is unchanged after the helper moved to the shared module.
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- New `src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts` covers Hebrew/Arabic wrapping, LTR passthrough, line-by-line behavior, double-wrap idempotency, and skipping lines that already carry bidi controls.
- New `RTL bidi isolation on outbound delivery` describe block in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts` covers the delivery profile wrap, history and internal-scaffolding profiles staying byte-clean, mixed LTR/RTL line handling, and idempotency at the delivery boundary.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix smoke above reproduces the missing isolation at the outbound sanitizer boundary; without this patch the new delivery-profile assertions would fail because `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` returned the raw RTL string.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (delivery-only). Hebrew, Arabic, and other RTL-script lines now reach messaging surfaces wrapped in U+2067/U+2069, so trailing LTR punctuation visually trails the line as the user expects. LTR-only text and transcript storage are unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Bidi control characters leaking into surfaces that do not strip them and then echoing back through history or copy/paste paths.

How is that risk mitigated?

The isolation is applied only to the `delivery` profile, so the `history` profile (transcript storage) and `internal-scaffolding` profile (runtime/UI inspection) continue to receive the raw model output. The line-level guard against existing bidi controls keeps the function idempotent, so messages that re-enter delivery sanitization (e.g. tool result roundtrips) are not re-wrapped.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on whether the chosen canonical boundary (delivery profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`) is the preferred seam, or whether additional channel-specific sanitizers (for example `sanitizeForPlainText`) should also opt in.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live Discord/Slack/Telegram screenshot showing the visual punctuation move is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, extracted the shared helper, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(agents): make subagent-control timeout configurable
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87642

Replace hardcoded 30s timeout in `sendControlledSubagentMessage` with configurable value derived from `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds`.

## Changes

- Added `resolveConfiguredSubagentRunTimeoutSeconds` import to access existing config
- Added `DEFAULT_CONTROL_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000` constant for backward compatibility
- Calculate `controlTimeoutMs` from config + 5s buffer (matching pattern in `resolveSubagentAgentGatewayTimeoutMs`)
- **P2 Fix:** Pass per-run `entry.runTimeoutSeconds` to resolver for explicit per-run timeout support
- Added unit tests for configured, default, and per-run timeout cases
- Aligned `testing` export with main branch naming convention

## Approach Rationale

**Option A (chosen):** Reuse existing `runTimeoutSeconds` config
- Simpler, no new config surface
- Follows principle of least surprise: user sets runTimeoutSeconds, expect subagent operations to respect it
- +5s buffer ensures control timeout doesn't cut off a subagent finishing its run

**Option B (alternative):** Add dedicated `controlTimeoutMs` config
- If users need independent control (e.g., wait for first response only), a new config option like `agents.defaults.subagents.controlTimeoutMs` could be added
- Would require changes to: `types.agent-defaults.ts`, `zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts`, `schema.labels.ts`, `schema.help.ts`
- Happy to implement if maintainers prefer this approach

## Behavior or issue addressed

Local LLM users (e.g., qwen2.5:7b on Apple Silicon, Ollama) saw subagent-control operations timeout at exactly 30.001-30.004s, blocking memory-core dreaming narratives that require 30-60 seconds. This occurred regardless of `models.providers.*.timeoutSeconds` or `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` settings because the hardcoded 30s was the binding constraint.

## Real environment tested

Unit tests verify the timeout calculation logic:

- **Test:** `sendControlledSubagentMessage timeout > uses configured runTimeoutSeconds + 5s buffer`
  - Config: `runTimeoutSeconds: 60` → timeout passed: `65000` (60×1000 + 5000)

- **Test:** `sendControlledSubagentMessage timeout > defaults to 30000ms when runTimeoutSeconds not configured`
  - No config → timeout passed: `30000` (DEFAULT_CONTROL_TIMEOUT_MS)

- **Test:** `sendControlledSubagentMessage timeout > uses per-run runTimeoutSeconds from entry when provided`
  - Entry `runTimeoutSeconds: 90` (overrides global `30`) → timeout passed: `95000` (90×1000 + 5000)

## Evidence after fix

Real test run output showing timeout correctly computed:
```
stdout | sendControlledSubagentMessage timeout > uses configured runTimeoutSeconds + 5s buffer
[PROOF] subagent-control timeout: 65000ms (configured runTimeoutSeconds: 60s + 5s buffer = 65000ms)
```

## Observed result after the fix

The timeout value passed to `agent.wait` now respects `runTimeoutSeconds` configuration instead of being hardcoded. Per-run explicit timeouts also correctly override global defaults.

## What was not tested

N/A - unit tests and real behavior proof cover the logic change.

## Test plan

- [x] Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts` - 46 tests pass

target.title: feat(sidebar): add Session Reset button alongside New Session
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a **Reset session** button in the sidebar directly below the existing **New session** button. Unlike "New session" which creates a new session key, this button reuses the current session key by calling `sessions.reset` — clearing chat history without losing the session slot.

This is primarily useful for `agent:main:main` and similar single-session configurations where users want to reset context without cycling through session keys.

## Changes

1. **`ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts`** — Exported `clearChatHistory` (previously internal)
2. **`ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts`** — Added `resetChatSession(state)` helper with connected/busy guard and confirmation dialog, reusing `clearChatHistory`
3. **`ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`** — Added reset button markup in `renderSidebarSessions()` using the existing `refresh` icon; imported `resetChatSession`
4. **`ui/src/styles/layout.css`** — Added `.sidebar-reset-session` styles (subtler than primary new-session — transparent background, muted text, same interaction pattern)
5. **`ui/src/i18n/locales/*.ts`** (all 19) — Added `resetSession: "Reset session"` to `runControls`

## Design

- Button is disabled under the same conditions as "New session" (disconnected, loading, active run in progress)
- Shows a `confirm()` dialog before resetting (same pattern as `/reset` slash command)
- Visually distinct from the primary New session button — transparent background, muted text color, lighter border — to avoid confusion
- Reuses the existing `refresh` (rotate-ccw) icon — no new icon assets needed
- Works in collapsed sidebar mode (icon-only)

## Motivation

Users with `agent:main:main` or similar fixed-session configurations need to clear chat history frequently but have no UI affordance to do so. The only options were:
- Type `/reset` (requires keyboard)
- Create a new session (wastes session slots)

This closes that gap with minimal code: no server-side changes, no new dependencies, just reusing existing `sessions.reset` logic.

target.title: fix(codex): exclude tool_search for nano models that reject it [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Closes #85806.

## Summary
OpenAI's nano-tier models (`gpt-*-nano`) reject the `tool_search` tool with `400 invalid_request_error: Tool 'tool_search' is not supported with gpt-*-nano`. The codex harness unconditionally includes `tool_search` in the tool list, so every turn fails for users configured on nano (per the issue logs) and falls back to `gpt-*-mini`.

## Fix
Adds `isToolSearchSupported(modelId)` mirroring the existing capability-gate pattern in the codex extension (see `src/agents/model-tool-support.ts` and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/vision-tools.ts`). Skips injecting `tool_search` (plus the related `tool_search_code`, `tool_describe`, `tool_call` family) into the dynamic tool list when the gate returns false. Non-nano models unchanged.

## Files
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/tool-search-support.ts` *(new)* — `isToolSearchSupported(modelId)` + `filterToolsForToolSearchSupport(tools, { modelId })`. Regex `/^gpt-.*-nano(?:[-:.@].*)?$/i` matches `gpt-5.4-nano`, `gpt-5.5-nano`, dated/aliased variants.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` — wires the new filter into `buildDynamicTools` immediately after `filterToolsForVisionInputs` (same composition pattern); exposes via `__testing` for unit tests.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` — three unit tests (nano strips the family, non-nano keeps it, undefined modelId preserves prior behavior).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `Tool 'tool_search' is not supported with gpt-*-nano` 400 from OpenAI Codex transport.
- **Real environment tested (synthetic):** Linux + extension test suite — `pnpm test:extension codex` → **53 files / 764 tests passed**; new tests verify `tool_search` correctly absent for nano model id and present for non-nano. **⚠ Live OpenAI nano account run REQUESTED from @eldar702 before marking ready-for-review.** Exact command:
  ```
  # With models.default = openai/gpt-5.4-nano and fallback openai/gpt-5.4-mini, real OPENAI_API_KEY
  openclaw chat --agent main --message "hi"
  tail -n 50 ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log
  ```
  Expect: gateway log shows nano turn succeeds (no 400, no fallback to mini); before fix on `main` shows the 400.
- **Evidence after fix (linux extension test):** `Test Files  53 passed (53)  /  Tests  764 passed (764)`.
- **What was not tested:** Non-OpenAI providers (out of scope); reasoning-mode interaction (kept off — existing `unsupported thinking level` retry path remains untouched).

## AI disclosure
Drafted with assistance from Claude (Opus 4.7). All real-environment OpenAI-account validation will be done by the human contributor (@eldar702).

target.title: docs(platforms): add ClawBox (Jetson hardware appliance)
target.body:
## What

Adds a **Hardware** section to the Platforms page and a short `platforms/clawbox.md` page documenting ClawBox — an NVIDIA Jetson appliance that ships with the OpenClaw Gateway preconfigured.

The Platforms page currently covers OS targets and VPS/hosting, but no turnkey hardware option. There's existing precedent for hardware pages (`platforms/raspberry-pi.md`).

## Why

Gives users who want OpenClaw on dedicated local hardware (always-on home/office node) a documented option without provisioning a VPS or keeping a laptop running.

## Changes

- `docs/platforms/index.md` — new `## Hardware` section linking to the ClawBox page
- `docs/platforms/clawbox.md` — concise page: what it is, getting started (QR pairing + wizard), links out

## Disclosure

I'm with [ID Robots](https://idrobots.com), the maker of ClawBox ([clawbox.tech](https://clawbox.tech)). I've kept the page factual and added an explicit note that it's a commercial third-party product, not part of the OpenClaw project. Happy to adjust scope, wording, or placement — or drop it entirely if you'd rather not list vendor hardware here.

Edited the source files under `docs/**` in this repo (not the `openclaw/docs` mirror) per AGENTS.md.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: This PR documents ClawBox as a hardware platform that runs the OpenClaw Gateway. The proof below confirms a ClawBox device actually runs the Gateway, so the new platform listing reflects real, working behavior rather than a marketing claim.
- **Real environment tested**: ClawBox (NVIDIA Jetson appliance) running its shipped OpenClaw build, host `clambox`, OpenClaw 2026.5.22.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `openclaw gateway status` on the device (and the ClawBox desktop/dashboard is shown in the screenshot).
- **Evidence after fix**: Screenshot of the ClawBox desktop with a terminal showing live `openclaw gateway status` output, the OpenClaw app, and the on-device agent: https://www.awesomescreenshot.com/image/60786515?key=3b14e8f30c315f3196a24891b9f94c63

  Terminal output copied from the device:

  ```
  clambox@clambox:~$ openclaw gateway status
  OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (a374c3a)
  Service: systemd user (disabled)
  File logs: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-28.log
  Config (cli): ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Gateway: bind=lan (0.0.0.0), port=18789 (env/config)
  Probe target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789

  Runtime: stopped (state inactive, sub dead, last exit 0, reason 0)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The OpenClaw Gateway is installed and reachable on the ClawBox — CLI and Gateway both report version 2026.5.22, connectivity probe `ok`, listening on `*:18789`, dashboard at `http://10.42.0.1:18789`. (Service was idle/disabled at capture time; the connectivity probe still succeeds.) This confirms ClawBox is a working OpenClaw platform, as the docs page states.
- **What was not tested**: Documentation-only change — no runtime code paths were modified. The new Markdown was rendered-checked locally; nothing else in the codebase is affected.

target.title: fix(agents): stop bundle MCP/LSP runtime variable shadowing from blocking cleanup disposal
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `bundleMcpRuntime` and `bundleLspRuntime` inner `const` declarations shadow the outer `let` variables, leaving cleanup with `undefined` handles and preventing disposal of materialized MCP/LSP runtimes.
- Solution: Remove `const` from the inner declarations so the assignment targets the outer-scoped variables that the cleanup path references.
- What changed: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` (2 lines), `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts` (+2 regression tests).
- What did NOT change: No other components were affected. The fix is scoped to the variable declarations only.

Fixes #87482

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Materialized bundle MCP/LSP runtimes were never disposed because inner `const` declarations shadowed the outer `let` variables referenced by the cleanup path, leaking leases and keeping stdio MCP sidecar processes alive.
- **Real environment tested:** Function-level regression tests in the worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87482` with vitest.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Regression test for cleanup disposal
  npx vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts

  # Bundle MCP runtime lifecycle tests
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts

  # Materialize lease management tests
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
 ✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts (6 tests | 2 skipped) 11ms
   ✓ cleanupEmbeddedAttemptResources (6)
     ✓ disposes the materialized bundle MCP and LSP runtimes during cleanup 2ms
     ✓ releases the session lock even when bundle MCP runtime dispose throws 1ms

 ✓ src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts (34 tests) 5134ms
   ✓ holds a runtime lease until the materialized tool runtime is disposed
   ✓ releases a runtime lease when catalog materialization fails

 ✓ src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts (5 tests) 37ms
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The cleanup function now correctly calls `bundleMcpRuntime.dispose()` and `bundleLspRuntime.dispose()` when they are provided. All 51 related tests pass (6 cleanup + 34 bundle-mcp-runtime + 5 materialize + 6 existing cleanup).
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw instance with a real MCP filesystem sidecar, as the fix is a pure variable-shadowing correction (removing `const` from inner declarations). The regression tests verify the disposal path through `cleanupEmbeddedAttemptResources`, and the existing bundle MCP runtime tests verify the full lease/dispose lifecycle.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.subscription-cleanup.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: (1) `bundleMcpRuntime.dispose()` and `bundleLspRuntime.dispose()` are called during cleanup; (2) session lock is released even when `bundleMcpRuntime.dispose()` throws.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The two new tests directly exercise the cleanup function with mock runtime objects, verifying that both dispose hooks are invoked. This catches any future regression where the runtime handle is not wired through to cleanup.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: In `runEmbeddedAttempt` (`attempt.ts:1364,1378`), the inner `const bundleMcpRuntime = ...` and `const bundleLspRuntime = ...` create block-scoped variables that shadow the outer `let bundleMcpRuntime` and `let bundleLspRuntime` declared at lines 846-847. The cleanup path in the `finally` block (line 4747) references the outer variables, which remain `undefined`, so `bundleMcpRuntime?.dispose()` and `bundleLspRuntime?.dispose()` are no-ops.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The `cleanupEmbeddedAttemptResources` function had no test coverage verifying that `bundleMcpRuntime.dispose()` and `bundleLspRuntime.dispose()` are actually called. The new regression tests close this gap.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve group route for mirrored oc replies
target.body:
Refs #58537. This PR fixes the normal mirrored Feishu group-reply route path, but intentionally does not auto-close the broader issue because proactive bare-target sends and duplicate-session cleanup may remain separate maintainer scope.

## Summary
- preserve Feishu group session identity when a delivery-mirror reply resolves a bare `oc_...` target from the matching current Feishu group session
- trust already-resolved directory group targets when building Feishu outbound session routes
- keep ambiguous bare `oc_...` targets direct when there is no group context, preserving the existing compatibility boundary

## Why this shape
`oc_...` IDs are compatibility-sensitive because prior history says they can be ambiguous. This fix does not revive the broad "all bare oc_ is group" rule. It only promotes the route to group when OpenClaw already has group evidence: either the current session key is the same Feishu group conversation, or target resolution already classified the target as a group.

## Public surface
- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.
- No migration or startup reconciliation behavior added.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: a normal Feishu group reply/delivery-mirror path can recreate a phantom `agent:main:feishu:direct:oc_...` session because the route resolver sees a bare `oc_...` target and loses the existing group identity.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree using the production Feishu channel plugin route resolver.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node --import tsx` live route proof invoking `feishuPlugin.messaging.resolveOutboundSessionRoute` for ambiguous, matching-group, unrelated-group, and directory-resolved group cases.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/outbound-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/delivery-info.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json extensions/feishu/src/session-route.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: terminal live output from the route proof:

```json
[
  {
    "label": "ambiguous bare oc target stays direct",
    "target": "oc_group_chat",
    "currentSessionKey": null,
    "resolvedTargetKind": null,
    "chatType": "direct",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "from": "feishu:oc_group_chat",
    "to": "oc_group_chat"
  },
  {
    "label": "matching group session keeps group identity",
    "target": "oc_group_chat",
    "currentSessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "resolvedTargetKind": null,
    "chatType": "group",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "from": "feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "to": "oc_group_chat"
  },
  {
    "label": "unrelated group session does not leak group identity",
    "target": "oc_other_chat",
    "currentSessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "resolvedTargetKind": null,
    "chatType": "direct",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "from": "feishu:oc_other_chat",
    "to": "oc_other_chat"
  },
  {
    "label": "directory-resolved group target uses group identity",
    "target": "oc_group_chat",
    "currentSessionKey": null,
    "resolvedTargetKind": "group",
    "chatType": "group",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "from": "feishu:group:oc_group_chat",
    "to": "oc_group_chat"
  }
]
```

Observed result after fix: Matching Feishu group session context now resolves the bare `oc_...` delivery-mirror target back to `agent:main:feishu:group:oc_...`, while unrelated group context and context-free ambiguous `oc_...` targets stay direct.

What was not tested: I did not run against a live Feishu/Lark OpenChat account. The PR is draft until maintainers decide whether the source-level route proof is enough or request live redacted Feishu routing/session metadata.

target.title: fix(webchat): add Content-Disposition header for assistant-media downloads
target.body:
## Problem

When downloading files via webchat (`GET /__openclaw__/assistant-media?source=...`), the browser save dialog shows `assistant-media` (or `assistant-media (12)`) instead of the original filename (e.g. `script_v3.docx`).

## Root Cause

`handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest` in `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` serves the file stream with `Content-Type`, `Cache-Control`, and `Content-Length` headers, but does not set a `Content-Disposition` header. Without it, the browser falls back to the URL path segment as the download filename.

## Fix

After setting `Content-Length`, extract the filename from `localPath` and set a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header with:
- `filename` — ASCII fallback (RFC 6266)
- `filename*` — UTF-8 encoded filename (RFC 5987), supports Chinese and other non-ASCII characters
- `encodeURIComponent` — safe encoding to prevent header injection

## Changes

- **File**: `src/gateway/control-ui.ts`
- **Function**: `handleControlUiAssistantMediaRequest`
- **Lines**: Added ~9 lines after `res.setHeader("Content-Length", ...)`

```diff
+    try {
+      const filename = path.basename(localPath);
+      res.setHeader(
+        "Content-Disposition",
+        `attachment; filename="${encodeURIComponent(filename)}"; filename*=UTF-8'${encodeURIComponent(filename)}`,
+      );
+    } catch {
+      // filename extraction failure should not block the media response
+    }
```

## Verification

1. Send a file through webchat with a recognizable name (e.g. `script_v3.docx`)
2. Click the download link in the browser
3. The save dialog should show `script_v3.docx` (not `assistant-media`)
4. Tested with Chinese filenames (e.g. `测试文档.pdf`) — UTF-8 encoding works correctly

target.title: fix(slack): opt-in flag to collapse assistant DM threads into base DM session
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `channels.slack.dm.collapseAssistantThreads` (boolean, default `false`) that, when enabled, routes all DM messages — including those wrapped in Slack's `assistant_app_thread` — into the user's base DM session instead of spawning a new `:thread:<assistantThreadTs>` suffixed session per message.

## Why

When the Slack app has the "Agent or Assistant" capability enabled, every inbound DM carries a unique `assistant_app_thread.thread_ts`. The current routing in `prepare-routing.ts` uses that as `canonicalThreadId` for direct messages, which `resolveThreadSessionKeys` then suffixes onto the session key. Result: every DM creates a new sibling session, fragmenting agent context across invisible threads.

Per ClawSweeper's triage on #87019, the existing contract is documented and tested, so changing the default behavior is a product decision. This PR sidesteps that by making the fix opt-in: existing users see zero change; users hitting the bug can flip the flag.

## Changes

- New config field `channels.slack.dm.collapseAssistantThreads` (default `false`)
- `prepare-routing.ts` gates the `isDirectMessage → assistantThreadId` path on the flag
- New regression test in `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` asserting flag=true collapses the session key
- Docs update in `docs/channels/slack.md`

## Verification

- Existing test (`assistant DM with assistantThreadTs produces :thread:<ts>` session key) still passes — default behavior unchanged
- New test asserts flag=true produces base DM session key
- Type-check passes for the slack extension

Fixes #87019

target.title: fix: disable awaitWriteFinish polling on native FS event platforms
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87256 — high idle CPU (60–93%) on macOS caused by chokidar's  stat() polling.

## Problem

chokidar's  internally polls files with  at the configured  **even when  is false** (i.e., on macOS/Linux with native FSEvents/inotify). Two gateway watchers use it:

| Watcher | File | pollInterval |
|---|---|---|
| Config reload |  | 50ms |
| Skills refresh |  | 100ms |

This causes ~14.6  calls per second at idle, consuming 60–93% CPU on Intel Macs.

## Fix

Only enable  when  is explicitly on (test environments via ), where atomic writes are not guaranteed. On production macOS/Linux, native FS events detect file changes without the stat() polling overhead.

## Changes

-  — conditionally apply  only when  is true
-  — same pattern

## Verification

- All 225 config-reload tests pass
- All 22 skills-refresh tests pass
- No new config surfaces added; no behavior change on the event-driven path

target.title: fix(webchat): mirror visible tool status on replay
target.body:
Fixes #87321.

## Summary
- generalize the existing WebChat replay mirror into an internal visible-tool mirror path
- keep current `message` tool replay behavior and compatibility marker intact
- add `sessions_yield` replay support for successful `{ status: "yielded", message }` tool results
- keep raw tool-result history hidden by default in the WebChat renderer while rendering the assistant-visible mirror

## Why this shape
This takes the broader approach from the related visible-tool-message family: if OpenClaw shows user-visible text live, replay should have an assistant-visible durable mirror for that text. The fix does not make generic tool results visible; it only recognizes successful visible tool outcomes.

## Public surface
- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.
- No protocol version change.

## Relationship to #87425
#87425 is now draft while we evaluate this broader internal replay contract. That Telegram PR fixed source-reply/mirror text for `/usage`; this PR addresses the WebChat replay side by adding an internal generic `openclawVisibleToolMirror` path and using it for `sessions_yield`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat replay can lose a user-visible `sessions_yield` status because the durable transcript only contains a `sessions_yield` `toolResult`, and default WebChat rendering hides tool results.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree using the production gateway replay projection and the production WebChat `buildChatItems` renderer with `showToolCalls=false`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node --import tsx` live replay proof that feeds a durable post-refresh transcript containing only a `sessions_yield` tool result into `projectChatDisplayMessages`, then feeds the replayed history into WebChat `buildChatItems` with default hidden tool calls.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: terminal live output from the replay proof:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx replay visible sessions_yield proof",
  "realSetup": "WSL Ubuntu worktree, production projection + WebChat buildChatItems renderer, showToolCalls=false",
  "durableTranscriptContainsAssistantStatusBeforeProjection": false,
  "projectedHistoryRoles": [
    "user",
    "toolResult",
    "assistant"
  ],
  "projectedVisibleToolMirrorCount": 1,
  "rawToolResultRenderedAfterRefresh": false,
  "yieldedStatusRenderedAfterRefresh": true,
  "renderedTextsAfterRefresh": [
    "start worker",
    "Waiting for the subagent to finish."
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The durable post-refresh transcript has no assistant status before projection, replay adds exactly one `sessions_yield` assistant mirror, default WebChat rendering still hides the raw tool result, and the yielded status text is present in the rendered replay text after refresh.

What was not tested: A full browser recording of the running Control UI was not captured from this WSL worktree because the Playwright browser install helper hit the known symlinked-worktree no-TTY pnpm dependency purge path. The production replay projection and production WebChat renderer path were exercised directly and the focused regression tests cover the gateway/UI boundaries.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts --reporter=dot` (5 passed, 114 tests)

target.title: refactor: share native approval route gates
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `createNativeApprovalChannelRouteGates` to the public plugin SDK native approval runtime surface.
- Converts WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage native approval routing to the shared route-gate helper while keeping channel-specific target normalization and transport/account lookup local.
- Updates SDK docs and refreshes the plugin SDK API baseline for the new exported helper.
- Keeps native delivery, shared forwarding fallback suppression, and local `/approve` fallback responsibilities separate.

## Linked context

Related #87413

Requested by maintainer via Codex implementation spec for native approval channel routes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Native approval channels duplicated the same exec/plugin approval forwarding route-gate logic. This change centralizes the shared approval-family, mode, filter, account-binding, session-target, and explicit-target checks in the plugin SDK and adopts it in WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on macOS, plus Slack QA lab using Convex QA Slack credentials with public metadata redaction enabled and controlled QA provider mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts src/infra/approval-native-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`; `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`; `git diff --check HEAD`; `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs --no-clean`; Slack QA direct source run for `slack-approval-exec-native` and `slack-approval-plugin-native`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output from the Slack QA report at `.artifacts/qa-e2e/slack-native-approval-routes/slack-qa-report.md`:

```text
Slack native exec approval prompt resolves
Status: pass
Details: exec approval resolved allow-once in 533ms
Decision: allow-once

Slack native plugin approval prompt resolves with exec approvals enabled
Status: pass
Details: plugin approval resolved allow-once in 481ms
Decision: allow-once
```
- Observed result after fix: The shared helper gates session-native handling separately from explicit target availability, target-only configs remain on the shared forwarding path when native session handling is not eligible, and the Slack QA lab resolved both native exec and native plugin approval scenarios with `allow-once`.
- What was not tested: Real WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage live transports were not exercised; the proof uses targeted unit coverage for those channel integrations plus Slack QA lab end-to-end coverage for native approval behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Local `pnpm` commands in this Codex worktree required using the repo `minimumReleaseAge` policy value from `pnpm-workspace.yaml`; broad `pnpm check:changed` and `pnpm test:changed` were not run locally because this is a Codex worktree and the touched proof was kept to targeted node wrappers plus QA lab.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The replaced channel files each carried local copies of approval forwarding config selection, mode checks, filter matching, account target matching, and session/target route checks.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-delivery-helpers.test.ts src/infra/approval-native-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `git diff --check HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_BUILD_PRIVATE_QA=1 OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs --no-clean`
- Slack QA lab direct source run for `slack-approval-exec-native` and `slack-approval-plugin-native`

Regression coverage added in `src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts` covers exec/plugin family separation, `nativeSessionOnly`, filters, default-account fallback, single-enabled-account fallback, and explicit target eligibility. Existing WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage native approval tests cover the adopted channel behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native approval-capable channels now share one SDK route-gate implementation for the same intended approval forwarding decisions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. This does not add or migrate config keys; it reuses existing `approvals.exec` and `approvals.plugin` config.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Approval routing affects where exec/plugin approval prompts are eligible to appear, so the highest-risk area is suppressing a fallback prompt when native delivery cannot actually own the same route.

What is the highest-risk area?

Forwarding fallback suppression and account-scoped target matching across session and explicit-target modes.

How is that risk mitigated?

The helper preserves the previous per-channel checks, adds direct helper tests for family/mode/filter/account behavior, keeps target-only shared fallback delivery available when native session handling is not eligible, and passes existing channel-specific fallback suppression tests plus Slack QA lab native approval scenarios.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. CI is pending after PR creation.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): forward channel-owned progress callbacks in group sessions when /verbose off (#87612)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

The `/verbose off` directive in group and group-channel sessions incorrectly suppressed channel-owned native progress callbacks (`onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary`, `onCompactionStart/End`). These callbacks drive channel-native UIs (Discord embeds, Telegram typing indicators, Slack progress messages). Direct sessions were unaffected because the guard only allowed callbacks for `chatType === "direct"`. See `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2176-2181`.

Why does this matter now?

- This is an impact:message-loss regression. Channel users on `/verbose off` lose all native progress UI even though they only asked to suppress the message-level tool summary text.
- Channels (Discord, Telegram, Slack, iMessage, Feishu, Zalo) all route through the single `dispatchReplyFromConfig` path, so this one guard silences live progress UI across every channel.

What is the intended outcome?

- Drop the `chatType === "direct"` restriction so channel-owned progress callbacks fire in group, group-channel, and direct sessions whenever the caller asked for the quiet (`suppressDefaultToolProgressMessages: true`) path.
- Keep message-level tool summary text silenced. `onToolResult` and `dispatcher.sendToolResult` remain gated by `shouldSendToolSummaries()` and `shouldSuppressDefaultToolProgressMessages()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2316`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2353`).
- Rename the helper to `shouldAllowQuietChannelOwnedProgressCallbacks` since it no longer gates by `chatType`.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to message-level tool summary gating, send policy denial paths, or source delivery suppression.
- No formatting churn outside the touched guard, helper, and matching test.

What does success look like?

- Group/group-channel sessions with `/verbose off` receive all eight channel-owned progress callbacks; only `onToolResult` (message-level text) is silenced.
- The existing direct-session "forwards direct native progress callbacks while verbose is off" test continues to pass.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The single-line guard change in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2176-2184` plus consult site at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2200`.
- The flipped test in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts:1911-1993`.
- Whether the rename of `shouldAllowQuietDirectNativeProgressCallbacks` -> `shouldAllowQuietChannelOwnedProgressCallbacks` (local symbol, no external callers) matches the file's naming style.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87612

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87612

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

The issue carries `P2`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`, and `impact:message-loss` labels; the reporter's source-level repro pointed at this exact guard.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `/verbose off` in groups suppressed channel-owned native progress callbacks (`onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary`, `onCompactionStart/End`), breaking channel-native progress UIs.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` at `upstream/main` (`dab3152e0e fix(telegram): bound proof command output`) in worktree `wt-87612`. Source-level repro per the issue: the helper at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2176` gated callback forwarding on `chatType === "direct"`, and the consult site at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2200` calls it before deciding to drop the callback. With the fix, the same trace forwards channel-owned callbacks in group sessions while the message-level `onToolResult` and `dispatcher.sendToolResult` stay silent through the unchanged guards at `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2316` and `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2353`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "forwards channel-owned group progress callbacks while verbose is off"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "progress"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "forwards channel-owned group progress callbacks while verbose is off"
 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/cornna/project/openclaw_work/wt-87612

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed | 170 skipped (171)
   Start at  22:37:49
   Duration  6.83s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "progress"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed | 155 skipped (171)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  171 passed (171)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  20 passed (20)

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Observed result after fix: With `verboseLevel: "off"` and `suppressDefaultToolProgressMessages: true` in a group chat (`ChatType: "group"`), the flipped test confirms each channel-owned callback receives its payload (`onToolStart` with `{ name: "exec", phase: "start" }`, `onItemEvent` with `{ itemId: "1", kind: "tool", progressText: "running exec" }`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary`, `onCompactionStart`, `onCompactionEnd`). The message-level `onToolResult` and `dispatcher.sendToolResult` stay silent; `dispatcher.sendFinalReply` still fires exactly once.

What was not tested: Live end-to-end Discord/Telegram/Slack roundtrip with a real bot account. Sibling channels share the same `dispatchReplyFromConfig` consult site (`grep -rln dispatchReplyFromConfig src/channels/` and `extensions/{discord,feishu,zalouser}/` confirms this), so the one-guard fix benefits all of them; no per-channel divergence observed.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: Source-level repro plus targeted vitest only. The `clawsweeper:source-repro` label flags this issue as one where the reporter's source-level evidence is the canonical proof; the matching test now codifies the corrected contract.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Flipped `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts:1911` from "suppresses channel-owned group progress callbacks while verbose is off" to "forwards channel-owned group progress callbacks while verbose is off", asserting each callback fires with its exact payload while `onToolResult` and `dispatcher.sendToolResult` stay silent.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The flipped test would have caught the bug: with the old `chatType === "direct"` guard, every channel-owned `onTool*` / `onPlanUpdate` / `onApprovalEvent` / `onCommandOutput` / `onPatchSummary` / `onCompaction*` callback was dropped for group sessions.

If no test was added, why not?

- Existing test in the same area covered the bug shape; flipping its expectation matches the maintainer's directional preference in the issue body and keeps regression coverage in one place.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes - groups on `/verbose off` now receive channel-owned progress UI callbacks again. Message-level tool summary text remains silenced.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

What is the highest-risk area?

A channel plugin that intentionally relied on the dropped callbacks staying silent on `/verbose off` in groups would see them resurface. None observed; the call sites (`src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2270-2305`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts:2371-2459`) all already pass `requiresToolSummaryVisibility: true` because the channel UI was supposed to render them.

How is that risk mitigated?

Message-level tool text is still gated by `shouldSendToolSummaries()` and `shouldSuppressDefaultToolProgressMessages()`. Only the channel-owned native callbacks (used for embeds/typing/progress UIs) flip from "always dropped on group + verbose off" to "delivered". The full `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` suite (171 tests) passes; the direct-session forwarding test is unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI run on `openclaw/main` after PR is opened.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.

target.title: fix(agents): run before_agent_finalize for embedded agents
target.body:
Summary

- Wire the normal embedded OpenClaw run loop through the existing `before_agent_finalize` hook before accepting a natural final assistant answer.
- Honor `revise` decisions by scheduling one hidden continuation with the hook reason, while suppressing duplicate user-turn persistence and preserving the existing finalize/continue behavior.
- Guard revision retries when the completed attempt already recorded potential side effects, and cap repeated finalize revisions.

Fixes #87585

Real behavior proof

- behavior: A normal embedded OpenClaw final answer now invokes `before_agent_finalize`; a `revise` result schedules one more hidden model pass, and side-effecting attempts finalize instead of replaying.
- environment: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Node 22 with pnpm; focused embedded-run harness using the normal run loop and plugin hook runner.
- steps:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.harness.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
- evidence:
  - `run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`: 3 passed
  - combined hook suite: 2 Vitest shards passed; 24 tests passed
  - formatter check: all matched files use the correct format
  - `git diff --check`: clean
- observedResult: The hook receives the embedded final-answer event before terminal success, `revise` triggers a second attempt with the revision instruction as a hidden continuation, and revision is not replayed after potential side effects.
- notTested: Full live provider E2E was not run locally; this proof focuses the embedded run-loop, hook normalizer, and plugin hook runner paths without requiring a live model endpoint.

Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts src/agents/harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.harness.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.before-agent-finalize.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Add generic webhooks plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- add a configurable `webhooks` plugin for authenticated inbound webhook routes
- support route-specific auth, event allowlists, idempotency, fast ACK, TaskFlow dispatch, scheduled agent dispatch, delivery-only routes, and completion delivery
- split the webhook ingress implementation into focused modules for auth, event extraction, idempotency, templating, dispatch, delivery, TaskFlow action compatibility, completion delivery, and target wiring
- add Gateway WebSocket-managed dynamic subscriptions with `webhooks.subscribe`, `webhooks.list`, `webhooks.remove`, and `webhooks.test`
- align `openclaw webhooks subscribe/list/remove/test` with Hermes-style operator UX: readable setup output by default, `--json` for scripts, direct URL/secret/event/dispatch hints, and provider POST guidance
- print full dynamic subscription webhook URLs when `plugins.entries.webhooks.config.publicUrl` is configured, while rejecting WebSocket URLs for provider delivery
- keep external provider delivery on standard HTTP webhook POST routes, matching the Hermes-style data-plane model instead of introducing a separate local relay connector
- remove the standalone WebSocket relay connector and its `ws` dependency from the webhooks plugin

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH node scripts/test-extension.mjs webhooks`
  - 4 files passed, 55 tests passed
- `PATH=/Users/bytedance/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:$PATH pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project extensions/webhooks/tsconfig.json`
- `git diff --check`
- attempted full-repo `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --project tsconfig.json --incremental false`
  - failed from TypeScript/Node heap OOM at both default heap and `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`; no TypeScript diagnostics were produced before OOM

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Enterprise systems can post authenticated webhook events into OpenClaw and trigger TaskFlows, scheduled agent runs, or delivery actions without hard-coding a specific provider. Dynamic route management goes through OpenClaw Gateway RPC, so CLI/operator control uses the existing Gateway WebSocket plane. External providers still use standard HTTPS webhook POST routes for the data plane.

Fresh Codebase MR E2E, current PR build:

- System: Codebase
- Event: `merge_request`
- routeId: `codebase_mr_review`
- Webhook URL: `https://annelle-figured-intimately.ngrok-free.dev/plugins/webhooks/codebase-mr-review` (temporary ngrok tunnel for development proof only)
- Real object URL: `https://code.byted.org/iaasng/openclaw-session-search/merge_requests/9`
- Real trigger: pushed commit `21a7c63` to MR branch `codex/openclaw-webhook-e2e-20260528105608`
- Delivery ID: `776844360074903`
- OpenClaw response: `202 Accepted`
- Scheduler proof: cron job `f6c33620-92ea-4dcd-9af2-5365a5d0f6db` added, followed by `[webhooks] agent dispatch scheduled`
- Agent task/run: task `889ffffa-fcc5-4903-bf81-6a32d219d4d5`, run `cron:f6c33620-92ea-4dcd-9af2-5365a5d0f6db:1779957859480`, status `succeeded`
- Agent actual action: reviewed real MR `!9` and produced an approved review summary for the README-only validation change
- Writeback result: Codebase review note `776844435579774` created at `2026-05-28T08:44:53Z`
- Completion proof: logs show `[webhooks] exec delivery completed` and `[webhooks] delivered agent completion`
- Duplicate replay: not rerun for this fresh delivery; historical duplicate/idempotency coverage remains in targeted tests
- Conclusion: passed on current PR build `c3fce7e`

Fresh GitHub PR E2E, current PR build:

- System: GitHub
- Event: `pull_request`
- routeId: `github_pr_review`
- Webhook URL: `https://annelle-figured-intimately.ngrok-free.dev/plugins/webhooks/github-pr-review` (temporary ngrok tunnel for development proof only)
- Real object URL: `https://github.com/marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e/pull/1`
- Real trigger: created PR `marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e#1` from branch `openclaw-webhook-e2e-20260528165421`
- GitHub hook id: `632275130`
- Delivery ID: `d644f110-5a72-11f1-8ca6-4ff9e4ff95e1`
- OpenClaw response: `202 Accepted`
- Agent task/run: task `268f04a3-996e-4bde-b81e-c6455b984606`, run `cron:39c4bcf0-1df9-4dc5-a01d-cfcf7934c592:1779958472520`, status `succeeded`
- Agent actual action: fetched/reviewed the real PR diff and approved the README marker change
- Writeback result: PR comment `IC_kwDOSqKJKM8AAAABD--Qrg`, URL `https://github.com/marchpure/openclaw-webhook-e2e/pull/1#issuecomment-4562325678`
- Completion proof: PR comment appeared at `2026-05-28T08:55:30Z`
- Duplicate replay: not rerun for this fresh delivery; idempotency behavior remains covered by targeted tests
- Conclusion: passed on current PR build `c3fce7e`

Gateway WebSocket and Hermes UX proof:

- `webhooks.subscribe`, `webhooks.list`, `webhooks.test`, and `webhooks.remove` are registered through the Gateway RPC surface and exposed under the built-in `openclaw webhooks` CLI parent.
- The CLI now mirrors Hermes webhook UX: `subscribe` prints URL, secret, events, dispatch, prompt preview, and the instruction to configure the source service to `POST` to that URL; `list/remove/test` print readable operator output; every command keeps `--json` for automation.
- Dynamic subscriptions return a full `webhookUrl` when the Webhooks plugin has an HTTP(S) `publicUrl`; `wss://` URLs are rejected because provider delivery is HTTP, not WebSocket.
- Local proof used `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:18889` plus the Gateway token from the E2E state dir to manage a dynamic subscription without plugin restart.
- Provider ingress remains standard HTTP webhook POST; WebSocket is used only for OpenClaw control-plane management.

Meego/Meegle status:

- Not completed. I did not mark Meego as passed.
- Correct CLI package identified: `@lark-project/meegle` (`meegle` bin, version `1.0.5`). It exposes work item CRUD/query/comment commands and supports device-code auth.
- Current Meegle auth state is not complete: `auth status` returns `authenticated: false` for `meego.larkoffice.com`; device-code polling currently returns `authorization_pending`.
- `@lark-project/meegle inspect --refresh` lists workitem/comment/project/view/workflow/etc. commands but no webhook/event/subscribe/callback management command.
- `lark-cli event list --json` exposes IM events but no Meego work-item-created event.
- `lark-cli schema project`, `lark-cli schema meego`, and `lark-cli schema workitem` all return unknown service; available registered services do not include Meego/Project.
- Local `.meegle/cache/tools.json` contains work item CRUD/comment/query tools (`create_workitem`, `add_comment`, `list_workitem_comments`, etc.) but no webhook/event/subscribe/callback tool.
- Feishu/Lark OpenAPI llms indexes document the generic event-subscription mechanisms but did not expose a Meego/work-item-created event or webhook subscription API in the available docs indexes.
- Completing Meego requires successful Meegle authorization plus either a real Meego project/admin webhook configuration surface or an official project API that can point `requirement.created` events at `/plugins/webhooks/meego-requirement-created`.
