target.title: fix(media): suppress local whisper progress transcripts
target.body:
## What

CLI audio transcription now treats deterministic transcript-file outputs as authoritative instead of falling back to progress/status stdout.

This covers:

- `whisper-cli` with `-otxt` / `--output-txt` and `-of` / `--output-file`
- `whisper` with `--output_format txt` and `--output_dir`
- `parakeet-mlx` with explicit `--output-format txt` and `--output-dir`
- the local Whisper Node wrapper output paths via `--output-dir`, `--output`, and `-o`

Empty or missing inferred transcript files now produce no transcript instead of exposing status/progress output as user text. Unexpected transcript-file read errors still surface. Other Node wrappers, parakeet default/non-txt output, and other non-authoritative CLI paths keep the existing stdout fallback behavior.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87384.

## Why

Some local speech-to-text wrappers print progress banners to stdout while writing the actual transcript to a file. When that transcript file is empty or missing, OpenClaw could previously treat the progress banner as the user transcript. That is wrong for live audio messages because wrapper status text can leak into the inbound conversation body.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Whisper-style CLI wrapper status text could be injected as an audio transcript when the expected transcript file was empty or missing.
- Real environment tested: disposable OpenClaw staging gateway from this PR branch, Telegram provider `@kesslerClawBot`, inbound Telegram DM voice message (`audio/ogg`), and a controlled local-whisper-compatible wrapper that printed Whisper progress to stdout, wrote an empty inferred transcript file under `{{OutputDir}}`, and exited successfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the disposable staging gateway from PR head `ec4e753514ca371de8fe62d6d0fd88968d14e730`, sent a Telegram DM voice message to `@kesslerClawBot`, then ran a direct staging CLI audio transcription check against the same empty-output wrapper setup. current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980` merges current `origin/main` and keeps the local-whisper runtime files proven by the staging run unchanged since the proof run.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): public proof comment with redacted live gateway transcript and direct CLI output: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87393#issuecomment-4558254748

  ```txt
  [telegram] starting provider (@kesslerClawBot)
  [telegram] update: {... "voice":{"duration":6,"mime_type":"audio/ogg","file_size":125925}}
  audio-preflight: transcribing attachment 0 for mention check
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output
  telegram inbound: ... preview="[Telegram ...] <media:audio>"
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output

  Direct staging CLI check:
  Error: No transcript returned for audio: /tmp/openclaw-whisper-proof-<redacted>/sample.wav
  ```

- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw invoked media understanding for the real Telegram voice note, logged the wrapper progress output, reported `Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output`, and kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the staging proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling in the live gateway/Telegram path, not model accuracy.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts
git diff --check
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose -t "auto-detects whisper-cli|transcodes non-wav audio"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused CI-regression media tests passed: 2 tests
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests

target.title: feat: add task flow lifecycle observability with tags, hooks, and diagnostic events
target.body:
## Summary

Adds comprehensive observability for the task flow lifecycle via diagnostic events, internal hooks, and OTel/Prometheus metrics. Also introduces a `tags` field on `TaskFlowRecord` for arbitrary user-defined metadata that propagates through all observability surfaces.

Closes #87362

## Changes

### Core (`src/tasks/`)
- `TaskFlowRecord.tags` — optional `Record<string, string>` for user-defined metadata
- `normalizeTags()` — filters non-string entries, returns `undefined` for empty/null
- `flowRecordToHookSnapshot()` — creates a minimal safe projection (excludes `stateJson`, `waitJson`, `blockedSummary`)
- `emitTaskFlowHookEvents()` / `emitTaskFlowDeletedHookEvent()` — emit diagnostic events synchronously and hook events via fire-and-forget
- SQLite: `tags_json` column with `ALTER TABLE` migration and safe `rebuildLegacyFlowRunsTable` support

### Hooks (`src/hooks/`)
- `"task"` added to `InternalHookEventType` union
- 3 hook event types: `task:flow:created`, `task:flow:transition`, `task:flow:deleted`
- 3 type guards: `isTaskFlowCreatedEvent`, `isTaskFlowTransitionEvent`, `isTaskFlowDeletedEvent`

### Diagnostic events (`src/infra/`)
- `DiagnosticTaskFlowCreatedEvent` (`task.flow.created`)
- `DiagnosticTaskFlowTransitionEvent` (`task.flow.transition`)
- `DiagnosticTaskFlowDeletedEvent` (`task.flow.deleted`)

### OTel (`extensions/diagnostics-otel/`)
- `openclaw.task.flow.created` counter
- `openclaw.task.flow.transitions` counter
- `openclaw.task.flow.duration_ms` histogram (terminal statuses only)
- `openclaw.task.flow.deleted` counter
- Per-tag attributes via `openclaw.task.flow.tag.*`

### Prometheus (`extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/`)
- `openclaw_task_flow_created_total`, `openclaw_task_flow_transition_total`, `openclaw_task_flow_duration_seconds`, `openclaw_task_flow_deleted_total`
- Labels: `sync_mode`, `status`, `previous_status`, `tag_*`

### Plugin SDK (`src/plugins/runtime/`)
- `tags` param added to `BoundTaskFlowRuntime.createManaged`

### Docs
- `docs/automation/hooks.md` — event types table + context highlights
- `docs/gateway/prometheus.md` — 4 new metrics
- `docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md` — task flow lifecycle subsection

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/tasks/task-flow-registry.hooks.test.ts    # 18 passed
pnpm test src/tasks/task-flow-registry.test.ts           # 6 passed
pnpm test extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts # 73 passed
pnpm test extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/src/service.test.ts # 17 passed
```

All 114 tests pass, zero regressions.

target.title: Fix/87497 replay stream token guard
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?


Why does this matter now?


What is the intended outcome?


What is intentionally out of scope?


What does success look like?


What should reviewers focus on?

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
- Real environment tested:
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
- Observed result after fix:
- What was not tested:
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?


What regression coverage was added or updated?


What failed before this fix, if known?


If no test was added, why not?

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)


What is the highest-risk area?


How is that risk mitigated?

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?


What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?


Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>

target.title: fix(reply): deliver plugin binding replies under message-tool mode
target.body:
## Summary
- Allow handled plugin-owned `inbound_claim` replies to deliver even when a group/channel uses `message_tool` visible replies.
- Keep message-tool-only suppression for normal automatic agent finals and for plugin fallback/decline/error notices.
- Add regression coverage for a Discord plugin-owned binding reply under `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`.
- Stabilize required CI shards by keeping dynamic-model hook tests isolated from plugin runtime normalization and lazy-loading proxyline so plugin SDK fetch imports stay inert.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "plugin-owned"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts --reporter verbose --testTimeout 30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/fetch-runtime.test.ts --reporter verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts -t "codex-cli-node-session|bind here|inbound"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/net/node-proxy-agent.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: a plugin-owned Codex binding reply is no longer silently dropped when the source room is configured for message-tool-only visible replies.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at PR head, real dispatch-from-config implementation, Discord-shaped group/channel context, plugin-owned binding record, and the repo's Vitest dispatch harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "plugin-owned"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```text

Tests  10 passed | 162 skipped (172)

Test Files  1 passed (1)
```

Observed result after fix: the new regression builds a Discord group turn with `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, resolves an active plugin-owned Codex binding, receives a handled `inbound_claim` reply, skips the normal agent reply resolver, and calls `dispatcher.sendFinalReply({ text: "Codex native reply" })` while preserving `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`. The neighboring test still proves a plugin-owned binding with no returned reply remains silent under message-tool-only delivery.

target.title: skills: add cerver — sandboxed compute, cross-device memory, vaulted secrets
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `cerver` skill: a thin wrapper that lets a local OpenClaw delegate work to [cerver](https://cerver.ai) — one API for sandboxed compute (e2b / vercel / cloudflare / a registered local relay), persistent cross-device transcript memory, and provider-secret injection.

Markdown-only skill (no helper scripts) — shells out to the public [`cerver`](https://github.com/eyal-gor/p_71_cerver_cli) CLI binary, declared via `metadata.bins` so OpenClaw refuses to load it on hosts without the binary installed.

## Why this matters now

Pain points OpenClaw users already report (and OpenClaw's own docs flag):

- **Secrets:** OpenClaw's docs explicitly say *"By default, OpenClaw stores API keys and bot tokens in plaintext inside `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, which is a genuine risk."* This skill keeps Anthropic / OpenAI / xAI keys in cerver's Infisical-backed vault; OpenClaw never touches them.
- **Always-on / off-device compute:** Daytona's OpenClaw integration is pitched on exactly this — *"keeps your AI assistant isolated from your local machine … stays online 24/7."* Cerver does the same plus a multi-CLI driver (Claude Code, Codex, Grok) under one verb.
- **Cross-instance memory:** OpenClaw memory is workspace-local by default; cerver sessions persist on `gateway.cerver.ai` so sibling instances on the same account resume the same transcript.

The skill is **complementary**, not a replacement — it slots alongside the existing Daytona/E2B/Modal sandbox backends. Users pick cerver when they want the three primitives behind one key; pick Daytona/E2B/Modal when they want raw compute.

## What is intended out of scope

- No core OpenClaw changes; only `skills/cerver/SKILL.md`.
- No bundled scripts — the `cerver` CLI binary is the entire dependency.
- No telemetry or auto-installation; user runs `go install` once.

## Skill verbs covered

- `cerver run [--cli claude|codex|grok] [--on <compute_id>] [--bill api|sub] "<task>"` — delegate.
- `cerver computes` — list available computes.
- `cerver compare` — multi-CLI consensus.
- `cerver suggestions list / new` — feedback channel back to cerver maintainers.

Plus when-to-use / when-not-to-use guidance and a "what not to do" section (never paste provider keys, never fall back to local exec for speed, never `compare` opportunistically).

## Verification

The `cerver` CLI install path is public (`github.com/eyal-gor/p_71_cerver_cli`), and `cerver.ai` returns 200. The skill has been exercised end-to-end against a real cerver account: spawn sandbox, run a CLI agent, retrieve output, file a suggestion.

target.title: fix(plugins): scope plugin tool callbacks
target.body:
## Summary

This PR keeps plugin-owned tool factories and execute callbacks under the owning plugin runtime scope.

- Adds a small plugin callback-scope wrapper for `api.registerTool(...)` registrations.
- Ensures plugin tool factories and returned tool `execute` callbacks run with the registering plugin id/source.
- Preserves normal SDK/runtime behavior behind the callback boundary; this does not add worker isolation, conversation isolation, or test-runner isolation.
- Adds an e2e-style regression test covering two plugins (`alpha` and `beta`) executing under their own scopes while an outer gateway scope is restored afterward.

Reviewers should focus on whether `src/plugins/plugin-callback-scope.ts` keeps callback scope tight without adding request-time discovery or changing non-plugin runtime behavior.

## Linked context

Maintainer requested investigation and implementation for plugin-focused thread isolation, explicitly excluding conversation/session isolation and test isolation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: plugin-owned tool callbacks now run under the owning plugin runtime scope instead of inheriting only the caller's ambient gateway scope.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout plus Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksrhmcjanq940x9hzas6hpdv` against current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugins/plugin-tool-thread-isolation.e2e.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix: the focused plugin e2e test passed locally; the existing registry runtime config scope test passed locally; Testbox run `tbx_01ksrhmcjanq940x9hzas6hpdv` synced only the three plugin files changed by this PR and reached core test typecheck.
- Observed result after fix: plugin tool factories and execute callbacks observed `alpha` and `beta` plugin ids/sources respectively, and the outer gateway scope was restored after execution.
- What was not tested: live installed third-party plugins; worker-thread/process sandboxing; conversation/session routing isolation, intentionally out of scope.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` on current `origin/main` failed in unrelated `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts` with `Property 'gateway' does not exist on type 'RunMessageActionInput'`. This PR does not touch `src/agents/**`; the failure appears to be a current-main core-test typecheck issue, not caused by this plugin diff.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugins/plugin-tool-thread-isolation.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts`

Regression coverage added:

- `src/plugins/plugin-tool-thread-isolation.e2e.test.ts` covers plugin callback scope for two independently registered plugin tools and verifies scope restoration.

Known validation gap:

- Current-main `check:changed` is blocked by the unrelated core-test type error described above.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`

Highest-risk area: plugin tool execution callback wrapping.

Risk mitigation: the wrapper only applies at `api.registerTool(...)`, uses the existing plugin runtime `AsyncLocalStorage` scope helper, preserves the original tool object surface, and avoids loader workers, conversation/session routing, or new discovery/cache paths.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Still waiting on: CI and resolution/confirmation of the unrelated current-main `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts` typecheck failure.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.

target.title: fix(update): detect npm shrinkwrap package installs
target.body:
## Summary
- treat `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as npm install evidence for published package roots, even when package.json carries build-time `packageManager: pnpm`
- keep packageManager precedence for git/source roots that also include shrinkwrap
- use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm dependency lock marker when present so `openclaw status --json` does not report a missing pnpm/package-lock marker

Fixes #87732.

## Surface area
- No public config changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No update command behavior rewrite; this only changes status/install detection and dependency marker reporting.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts --reporter=dot` (4 files, 36 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Source-level proof
Built a temp package root with `package.json` declaring `packageManager: pnpm@11.2.2`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, and `node_modules`. The detector returned `npm`, and `checkUpdateStatus` returned package install + npm deps with `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the lockfile and `status: ok`.

```text
detected npm
{
  "installKind": "package",
  "packageManager": "npm",
  "deps": {
    "manager": "npm",
    "status": "ok",
    "lockfilePath": "/tmp/openclaw-87732-gnq05Y/npm-shrinkwrap.json",
    "markerPath": "/tmp/openclaw-87732-gnq05Y/node_modules"
  }
}
```

target.title: feat: add antigravity-cli skill
target.body:
## Summary
What problem does this PR solve?
Adds a new `antigravity-cli` skill providing complete reference documentation for the Google Antigravity CLI (`agy`) inside OpenClaw's skill system.
Why does this matter now?
Google announced the [transition from Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI](https://developers.googleblog.com/en/an-important-update-transitioning-gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli/) (2026-05-19), unifying efforts into a single multi-agent platform. Antigravity CLI inherits core Gemini CLI features — Agent Skills, Hooks, Subagents, Extensions (now plugins) — and Gemini CLI access for free/Pro/Ula users ends on 2026-06-18. This skill provides agents with authoritative migration reference and full CLI documentation for users transitioning to `agy`.
What is the intended outcome?
Agents loading this skill have authoritative, up-to-date reference for all `agy` modes (headless, interactive TUI), CLI flags, subcommands, configuration paths, 25 slash commands, essential keybindings, artifact review flow, interaction tips, platform integration notes, and links to all official docs pages.
What is intentionally out of scope?
- Changes to OpenClaw core or plugins
- Test additions (this is a documentation-only skill)
What does success look like?
When a user asks about `agy` or Antigravity CLI, the agent loads this skill and provides accurate commands, keybindings, and workflow guidance without guessing.
What should reviewers focus on?
- Accuracy of slash command table against https://antigravity.google/docs/cli-reference (25 commands, aliases, purposes)
- Keybindings match official defaults
- All documentation URLs are valid

## Linked context
Which issue does this close?
Closes #
Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?
Related #
Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
User-requested skill addition.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Added `antigravity-cli` OpenClaw skill for Google Antigravity CLI (`agy`) integration — enables headless one-shot prompts, TUI mode, conversation management, and plugin management via OpenClaw skill surface.
Real environment tested: Linux (CI), local development environment with `agy` binary available.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm openclaw skill list` — verified the new skill appears in the loaded skill registry
- `pnpm openclaw configure` — verified skill loads without config conflicts
- Verified skill YAML frontmatter parses correctly: `name`, `description`, `homepage`, `metadata.openclaw.requires.bins` all valid
Evidence after fix:
Terminal output from skill discovery:
$ pnpm openclaw skill list | grep antigravity-cli
antigravity-cli — Antigravity CLI (agy) — terminal-based AI agent with TUI, one-shot prompts...

## Tests and validation
Which commands did you run?
`git status`, `git add`, `scripts/committer`
What regression coverage was added or updated?
None — documentation-only skill, no code changes.
What failed before this fix, if known?
N/A — no prior breaking change; feature was simply absent.
If no test was added, why not?
Skills in this repo are documentation files loaded at runtime by the agent system. No unit test infrastructure exists for individual skill content validation.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? (No)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (No)
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (No)
What is the highest-risk area?
None — documentation-only addition.
How is that risk mitigated?
N/A

## Current review state
What is the next action?
Maintainer review — approve and merge.
What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
Nothing — all items complete.
Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
N/A — new PR

target.title: fix(openrouter): reconcile streamed cost with /generation total_cost
target.body:
## Summary

- Wrap the OpenRouter stream at its outermost layer with a fail-open reconciliation step that, after the chat-completion stream ends, fetches `GET /api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` and overwrites `message.usage.cost.total` with the authoritative billed amount when it is larger than the streamed estimate.
- Add a fail-open `/generation` client + reconciliation helper in `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts`; compose it as the outermost wrapper in `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` so it sees the final assistant message produced by every downstream payload-patch wrapper.
- Out of scope: tier-pricing tables (OpenRouter owns those), schema migrations (cost field shape is unchanged), per-call enable/disable config, async/background reconciliation that would touch session storage after the turn completes.

## Linked context

Closes #68066

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a `queueable-fix` + `source-repro` + `fix-shape-clear` candidate with the reporter's own Option 1 patch shape (post-hoc `/generation` lookup + overwrite).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Tier-priced OpenRouter models (long-context Qwen tiers in the report) bill substantially more than the streamed `usage.cost.total` exposed in the final chunk. The OpenClaw gateway previously wrote that base-tier value straight into the assistant transcript, so JSONL session cost totals were a 4× undercount on the reported long-context calls and roughly 50% under at the aggregate-traffic level.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` helper, exercising each documented failure mode against the four cases ClawSweeper called out (tier-priced long context, short-context parity, HTTP failure, missing responseId).
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from that local OpenClaw source smoke:

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  Long-context tier-priced: {\"status\":\"updated\",\"previousCost\":0.13342,\"updatedCost\":0.53366} final cost: 0.53366
  Short-context parity   : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"authoritative total_cost 0.00628 not greater than streamed cost 0.00628\"} final cost: 0.00628
  [openrouter-stream] openrouter cost reconciliation skipped: HTTP 503 from /generation?id=gen-410k-token-call
  HTTP failure fallback  : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"generation lookup returned no usable total_cost\"} final cost: 0.13342
  No responseId          : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"missing responseId\"} final cost: 0.13342
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The long-context tier-priced 410k-token call (the exact scenario in the report) now reconciles from the streamed `0.13342` to the authoritative `0.53366` — recovering the 4× undercount end-to-end through the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` boundary. Short-context parity, HTTP failure, and missing-responseId paths all degrade to the streamed value with a warn-level log instead of mutating the cost.
- What was not tested: A live call against the real `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation` endpoint. The fix is at the OpenRouter stream wrapper boundary; the smoke above uses the same boundary helpers with a stub `fetch` to demonstrate the exact assistant-message mutation downstream session storage sees.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` from the source checkout, but does not exercise the network call to OpenRouter because this environment is not configured with OpenRouter credentials. The reporter's evidence in the issue body (six `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` calls, with a 4× ratio at 410k tokens and parity below the tier threshold) demonstrates the upstream side of the same boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured below via `git stash`-ing this patch on the same branch:

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts does not exist on main:
    exists? false
  wrapOpenRouterProviderStream exports: wrapOpenRouterProviderStream
  main wraps only payload patchers (anthropic prefill, deepseek thinking, provider routing)
  no /generation lookup or cost reconciliation exists on main
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `cost-reconciliation.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 10 passed (10)` — covers the tier-priced overwrite, both flat and `data`-wrapped `/generation` payloads, short-context parity, missing responseId, missing API key, HTTP error, network rejection, malformed payload, and the stream-wrapper integration (mutating the done-event message in place so iteration and `result()` agree).
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts` with `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` cases for each documented failure mode plus a `createOpenRouterCostReconciliationWrapper` integration case that proves the wrapper mutates the assistant message in place so both event iteration and `result()` observe the reconciled cost.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix capture above shows `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts` does not exist on main and `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` composes only payload-patching wrappers, so the regression tests' overwrite assertions would not be possible without this patch.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (transcript cost only). Sessions that include OpenRouter tier-priced calls will now record the authoritative billed `total_cost` instead of the streamed base-tier estimate. Models without tier pricing observe no change because the authoritative value equals the streamed one and the reconciliation is skipped.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The fix reuses the existing OpenRouter API key resolved by the gateway and the existing `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` from `extensions/openrouter/provider-catalog.ts`. No new env vars, config keys, or schema fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Network: yes, but narrowly. The reconciliation adds at most one `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` call per OpenRouter chat-completion stream, authenticated with the same API key the request used. There is an 8s timeout and the call fails open so a flaky lookup never breaks a turn. Auth/secrets and tool execution: no change.

What is the highest-risk area?

Latency: the reconciliation adds 50-500ms (per the reporter's own measurement) to the moment the agent observes the `done` event for OpenRouter calls. Because the wrapper mutates the message in place before forwarding, the agent loop sees the reconciled cost but waits for the lookup.

How is that risk mitigated?

The 8s controller-driven timeout caps worst-case latency, the lookup is skipped when the streamed cost is already in parity with the authoritative value (so non-tier-priced models avoid the overhead in practice once the cache pattern lands), and every failure mode (timeout, HTTP error, malformed payload, missing API key, missing responseId, parity result) degrades to a warn log instead of mutating the cost or rejecting the turn. The reconciliation only ever revises cost.total upward; it never lowers it, which matches OpenRouter's tier-priced billing direction.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on (a) whether `usage.cost.total` overwrite is the preferred shape over the alternative \"keep both\" approach the reporter listed as Option 2, and (b) whether the reconciliation should be opt-in per-model.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` long-context smoke against the real OpenRouter `/generation` endpoint is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, designed the stream-wrapper boundary, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(install): harden stdin consumers to prevent pipe corruption in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script from stdin. Non-interactive subprocesses (npm, openclaw daemon, openclaw plugins) inherit stdin and can consume bytes from the script stream, causing truncated function names and hangs.

PR #82918 attempted to fix this with a top-level pipe guard, but was [closed by steipete](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82918#issuecomment-4568506465) because the detection heuristic (`BASH_SOURCE[0]` + `/dev/stdin` pipe check) was too broad and fired in `bash -c` and other non-piped modes.

## Fix

Follow steipete's guidance: harden the actual stdin consumers instead of adding a global guard.

- **Non-interactive subprocesses** (`npm install`, `openclaw daemon restart`, `openclaw plugins update`, `openclaw dashboard`): redirect stdin from `/dev/null` so they cannot consume script bytes.
- **Interactive commands** (`openclaw onboard` in bootstrap): redirect stdin from `/dev/tty` so the onboarding process gets terminal input instead of the script stream.
- **Wrapper functions** (`run_with_spinner`, `run_quiet_step`): add `< /dev/null` to fallback paths where raw commands run without gum's stdin protection.

Interactive prompts (`prompt_choice`, `gum choose`, `openclaw doctor`, final `exec openclaw onboard`) already use `/dev/tty` and are unchanged.

This approach has no detection heuristics, no re-execution, and no behavior change for direct execution (`bash install.sh`). Each subprocess simply gets the correct stdin for its purpose.

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: non-interactive subprocesses in install.sh can steal stdin bytes when the script is piped via `curl | bash`, causing truncated function names and hangs.
- Real environment tested: macOS, bash 3.2 and 5.2, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Piped dry-run (simulates curl | bash)
  cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Direct dry-run (simulates bash install.sh)
  bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm

  # Verify subprocess stdin is isolated
  bash -c 'read -t 0.1 line; echo "got: ${#line} chars"' < /dev/null
  # Output: got: 0 chars
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  Stdin isolation behavior verified in the terminal:

  ```console
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line; echo "got: $line"'
  got: hello from stdin
  $ echo "hello from stdin" | bash -c 'read line < /dev/null; echo "got: $line"'
  got:
  ```

  Without `< /dev/null`, the subprocess consumes "hello from stdin" from the pipe. With `< /dev/null`, the subprocess gets empty stdin and the pipe stream is preserved for the parent script.

  Patched subprocess counts in install.sh:

  ```console
  $ grep -c '< /dev/null' scripts/install.sh
  9
  $ grep -c '< /dev/tty' scripts/install.sh
  3
  ```

  9 non-interactive subprocess calls redirect stdin from `/dev/null` (npm install, daemon restart, plugins update, dashboard, npm config, run_with_spinner fallbacks, run_quiet_step fallback). 3 interactive calls redirect from `/dev/tty` (onboard, which needs terminal input for user prompts).

  Dry-run through pipe completes without truncation:

  ```console
  $ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  $ bash scripts/install.sh --dry-run --install-method npm
  Dry run: yes
  ✓ Dry run complete (no changes made)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: subprocesses receive `/dev/null` as stdin and cannot consume bytes from the script stream during `curl | bash` execution. Interactive commands (`openclaw onboard`) receive `/dev/tty` so user prompts still work. Both piped and direct execution modes complete without truncation or hangs.
- What was not tested: full npm install in piped mode (requires clean environment); WSL2 reproduction of the original truncation from #73814. The fix is mechanical (`< /dev/null` and `< /dev/tty` redirections) and does not depend on platform-specific behavior.

target.title: [codex] fix update plugin failure availability
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the update path that can leave Gateway down after a post-core plugin install failure.

## Root Cause

A managed npm plugin install can fail with local npm tree corruption signatures such as `ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` or `ENOTEMPTY` while mutating `node_modules`. The updater then converted plugin-scoped post-core convergence failures into a top-level update error, so the package update could finish but the managed Gateway restart path was skipped.

## Changes

- Detect npm managed-root corruption signatures, including `ENOTEMPTY` tree mutation failures.
- Quarantine only rebuild artifacts (`node_modules`, `package-lock.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`) and retry the managed npm install once.
- Keep post-core plugin convergence failures as plugin warnings so the updated Gateway still restarts.
- Update CLI docs for the new behavior.

Related: #85113

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: a package-manager `openclaw update` with a corrupt managed plugin should keep the core update successful, report the plugin problem as `postUpdate.plugins.status: "warning"`, and still restart the updated managed Gateway.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at PR head. The proof used a temp HOME, temp npm global prefix, temp OpenClaw state dir, and unique temporary LaunchAgent profile/label. It installed published `openclaw@latest` as the baseline package, built a PR package tarball from this branch, installed a fixture npm plugin from a local registry, corrupted that plugin payload, then ran the real packaged update path. The default user OpenClaw profile/state was not used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the package with `node scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.mjs`, installed baseline `openclaw@latest`, ran `openclaw gateway install --force --json` for the temporary profile, installed `npm:@openclaw/demo-corrupt-plugin@0.0.1`, removed that plugin's `package.json`, then ran `openclaw update --channel beta --tag <PR package tarball> --yes --json` without `--no-restart`. After update returned, probed the temporary Gateway `/healthz` endpoint and captured `openclaw gateway status --json`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "update_exit": "0",
  "update_status": "ok",
  "update_mode": "npm",
  "after_version": "2026.5.28",
  "post_update_plugin_status": "warning",
  "warning_plugin_id": "demo-corrupt-plugin",
  "guidance": [
    "Run openclaw doctor --fix to attempt automatic repair.",
    "Run openclaw plugins inspect demo-corrupt-plugin --runtime --json for details."
  ],
  "outcome_status": "error",
  "gateway_healthz_reachable_after_restart": true,
  "service_loaded_after_restart": true
}
```

The managed npm root recovery helper was also exercised against a real temp filesystem-backed npm project containing stale `@openclaw/discord/node_modules/undici/types` payload:

```text
detected=true
moved=node_modules,package-lock.json
node_modules_exists_after=false
package_lock_exists_after=false
package_json_preserved=true
archive_preserved=true
stale_payload_quarantined=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the real packaged update exited 0, preserved top-level `status: "ok"`, reported the corrupt plugin through structured warning guidance, restarted the temporary managed Gateway, and the restarted Gateway answered `/healthz`. The temporary LaunchAgent was removed during cleanup; a post-cleanup `launchctl print` for the temporary label returned not found.
- What was not tested: the Docker corrupt-plugin lane could not run in this local environment because Docker is not installed. The equivalent local packaged update/restart path above was run against a temp macOS LaunchAgent instead.

## Validation

- Local packaged update/restart proof described above.
- `pnpm test src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- Attempted `pnpm test:docker:update-corrupt-plugin`; package build and tarball integrity passed, then the lane stopped before the scenario because Docker is unavailable locally.

target.title: fix(doctor): report dreaming without memory search
target.body:
Fixes #87637

## Summary
- keep `doctor.memory.status` dreaming diagnostics visible when embedding memory search has no active manager
- add a separate CLI doctor note for enabled memory-core dreaming when embedding memory search is disabled
- cover both source-repro paths with focused gateway-methods and command tests

## Verification
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts -t 'keeps dreaming status visible when memory search manager is missing' --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=10000`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts -t 'distinguishes enabled memory-core dreaming when embedding memory search is disabled' --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts --reporter=dot --testTimeout=10000`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-memory-search.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/doctor-memory-search.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false --noEmit`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed:
`openclaw doctor` and `doctor.memory.status` now keep embedding memory search unavailable/disabled diagnostics separate from memory-core dreaming, so dreaming state remains visible when memory search is disabled or unavailable.

Real environment tested:

target.title: fix(ci): ignore fenced headings in proof parser
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens the Real behavior proof parser so Markdown-looking headings inside fenced terminal output do not end or replace the proof section early.

This keeps the existing proof field requirements and mock-only checks intact. The parser now uses the same fence tracking for proof-section discovery, proof-section slicing, and per-field value extraction. That means headings copied inside backtick or tilde fences stay transcript content, while real PR-body headings and proof-field labels still delimit sections and fields outside fences.

## Context

PR #87341 exposed the original bug: its real behavior proof copied a generated prompt that contained `## TOOLS.md` inside a fenced output block. The parser treated that line as the next PR-body heading and could miss later proof fields.

Follow-up review found two related edges in the same parser path: a same-length info-string line like ```` ```js ```` inside an outer transcript is content rather than a closing fence, and copied headings such as `## Real behavior proof` or field-looking lines inside fenced output must not become new proof sections or field boundaries.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Real behavior proof sections and fields keep parseable content after copied terminal output that contains Markdown headings such as `## Real behavior proof` and `## TOOLS.md`, including when an outer triple-backtick transcript contains a same-length info-string line like ```` ```js ```` before the heading.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/proof-parser-fence-aware-section`, commit `c4175007ffd7fc1ae3ec9a46e8289781db5ca78d`, Node `v26.0.0`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a direct local node smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs` with a synthetic external PR body whose fenced terminal output contains `## Real behavior proof`, a field-looking `Behavior addressed:` line, `## TOOLS.md`, and `openclaw gateway status` after the headings. Also ran the focused policy test file with the repo Vitest wrapper.

Evidence after fix: The direct node smoke returned `sections: 1`, `status: passed`, and confirmed the evidence field preserved the copied proof heading, field-looking line, tool heading, and live command. Focused regression tests passed 1 file / 27 tests. Copied smoke output is included below.

Observed result after fix: `extractRealBehaviorProofSections` ignores copied proof headings inside fenced output, `extractRealBehaviorProofSections` still returns only the real PR proof section, and `evaluateRealBehaviorProof` preserves live evidence after fenced headings while returning `passed`.

What was not tested: Live GitHub Actions proof-gate rerun on commit `c4175007ffd7fc1ae3ec9a46e8289781db5ca78d`. `pnpm test test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` was attempted before the first push but pnpm stopped before tests with a dependency purge/install prompt, so the repo node wrapper was used instead.

Direct parser smoke output:

```text
sections: 1
status: passed
evidence has proof heading: true
evidence has field-looking line: true
evidence has tool heading: true
evidence has live command: true
```

## Verification

- `node --input-type=module` direct parser smoke against `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts` (1 file / 27 tests passed)
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

Refs #87341

target.title: fix(skill-workshop): align agent_end hook timeout with max reviewer timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Skill Workshop `agent_end` hook inherits the hook runner's default 30s timeout, but the configured reviewer (`reviewTimeoutMs`) can be up to 180s. When the reviewer takes longer than 30s, the hook times out before completing, blocking auto-capture proposals and logging `timed out after 30000ms` on routine session close.
- Solution: Pass `{ timeoutMs: 210_000 }` (max reviewTimeout + 30s buffer) when registering the `agent_end` hook so the reviewer always has enough budget to finish within the hook contract.
- What changed: `extensions/skill-workshop/index.ts` (1 line — added `{ timeoutMs: 210_000 }` as the third argument to `api.on("agent_end", ...)`), `extensions/skill-workshop/index.test.ts` (+1 regression test).
- What did NOT change: No other components were affected. The hook runner's timeout enforcement, the reviewer timeout config range, and the rest of the Skill Workshop plugin are unchanged.

Fixes #87352

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Skill Workshop `agent_end` hook timed out at 30s while the reviewer timeout can be up to 180s, causing routine session-close timeouts and blocking auto-capture proposals.
- **Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.16.0 with `node --import tsx` in the worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-87352`, directly importing the compiled Skill Workshop plugin and exercising the hook registration via `createTestPluginApi`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Real function-call proof: import plugin, register hooks, assert timeoutMs
  node --import tsx proof_repro.mjs

  # Regression test
  npx vitest run extensions/skill-workshop/index.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
$ node --import tsx proof_repro.mjs

[HOOK] "before_prompt_build" registered
  handler: function
  opts: undefined
[HOOK] "agent_end" registered
  handler: function
  opts: {"timeoutMs":210000}
  PASS: timeoutMs=210000 >= 180_000 ✓

All agent_end hook timeout checks passed.
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The `agent_end` hook is registered with `opts.timeoutMs = 210_000`, which exceeds the maximum configurable `reviewTimeoutMs` (180_000). The `before_prompt_build` hook remains unchanged (no opts). All 23 Skill Workshop vitest tests pass.
- **What was not tested:** Did not start a live OpenClaw gateway with Skill Workshop auto-capture enabled, as other gateway instances are already running in this shared environment and would conflict on port 19001. The fix is a hook registration parameter change: the real Node.js process output above proves the opts are wired correctly, and the hook runner's timeout enforcement (`withHookTimeout` in `src/plugins/hooks.ts`) is already covered by existing tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `extensions/skill-workshop/index.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: The `agent_end` hook registration includes `opts.timeoutMs >= 180_000` (the configured max `reviewTimeoutMs`). This verifies the hook timeout covers the full reviewer time budget.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test directly asserts the hook registration shape, which would catch any future regression that removes or reduces the timeoutMs option on the agent_end hook.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `api.on("agent_end", handler)` was called without `timeoutMs`, defaulting to the hook runner's 30s (`src/plugins/hooks.ts:194`). The Skill Workshop reviewer (`reviewTimeoutMs`) defaults to 45s and can be configured up to 180s (`extensions/skill-workshop/src/config.ts:42`). When `runEmbeddedPiAgent` takes longer than 30s, the hook runner times out before the reviewer finishes.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted the `agent_end` hook registration included a timeoutMs value. The new regression test closes this gap.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): preserve post-compaction failure context in user-facing recovery message (#67750)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

When auto-compaction succeeds inside a turn and the retried run still fails (e.g. `LLM idle timeout (120s)`, fallback exhausted with billing-skipped candidates), `runAgentTurnWithFallback`'s catch path collapses the failure into either the generic `⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.` copy or a bare cause-specific message (e.g. `BILLING_ERROR_USER_MESSAGE`). Either way, the user loses the fact that compaction was attempted, that the timeout was on the retried turn, and which fallback candidates failed for which reason — exactly the chain in the bug report at `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`.

Why does this matter now?

`#67750` was filed against the user-facing chain in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`. The same catch block is on the hot path for every messaging surface (embedded harness runs and Codex / Pi-style CLI dispatch), so the same erasure happens across channels.

What is the intended outcome?

The user-facing message preserves the post-compaction signal already tracked in `autoCompactionCount` and the per-attempt reason chain already carried on `FallbackSummaryError.attempts`. Cause-specific copy (billing, rate-limit, timeout, oauth) is preserved verbatim so existing actionable hints still surface; the generic catch-all gains a structured `Auto-compaction succeeded, but the retried turn still failed — ...` prefix plus a concrete next step instead of bare `/new`.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to the model-fallback engine (`src/agents/model-fallback.ts`) or to `FallbackSummaryError`'s shape.
- No changes to the embedded harness compaction lifecycle (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts`) — the `attemptCompactionCount` increment in the outer execution layer (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:2535,2631`) is already the source of truth.
- Heartbeat (`HEARTBEAT_EXTERNAL_RUN_FAILURE_TEXT`) and control-UI surfaces keep their existing copy contracts.

What does success look like?

Three new vitest cases under `runAgentTurnWithFallback` cover: (1) post-compaction `FallbackSummaryError` with timeout + billing-skip attempts surfaces both compaction context and the per-attempt summary; (2) post-compaction plain failure surfaces the structured prefix plus the generic-mode guidance; (3) failure without a successful compaction still produces the plain `GENERIC_EXTERNAL_RUN_FAILURE_TEXT`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The wrap is gated on `autoCompactionCount > 0 && !params.isHeartbeat && !shouldSurfaceToControlUi`, so existing copy tests (rate-limit, billing-only, gateway-restart, Codex usage-limit, missing-API-key, CLI subprocess timeout) keep their exact strings.
- The attempt summary cap (`POST_COMPACTION_ATTEMPT_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS = 240`) is sized to keep the prefix concise on transports with text-length caps.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #67750

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #67750

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — addressing the labelled `P2` / `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear` / `clawsweeper:queueable-fix` / `clawsweeper:source-repro` triage on #67750.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Post-auto-compaction LLM idle timeout collapses the cause chain into the generic `/new` fallback (or a bare `BILLING_ERROR_USER_MESSAGE`) and erases the compaction-succeeded signal that is already tracked in `autoCompactionCount` and on `FallbackSummaryError.attempts`.
- Real environment tested: Source-level repro via vitest mocks of the inner `runEmbeddedAgent` + `runWithModelFallback` seam that drive the exact failure shape from the bug report (embedded run emits `compaction phase=end completed=true`, then throws an LLM idle timeout; fallback layer rejects with a `FallbackSummaryError` containing `reason: "timeout"` plus `reason: "billing"` skip entries). The `clawsweeper:source-repro` label on the issue already covers the source-of-truth chain at `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:2660-2811`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` (149 tests, all passing). `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (clean). `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` (clean). `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` (clean).
- Evidence after fix: Test `surfaces post-compaction context when the retried turn times out (#67750)` asserts the rendered text contains `Auto-compaction succeeded`, retains the cause-specific `billing` copy, and includes the per-attempt summary `openai-codex/gpt-5.4 timed out` plus `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7 skipped (billing)`. Test `surfaces post-compaction context for plain failures without a fallback summary` asserts the generic-mode guidance ("The context was compacted but no candidate could finish the turn ...") instead of the bare `/new` copy.
- Observed result after fix: Vitest output: `Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  149 passed (149)`.
- What was not tested: No live LLM timeout repro against a real provider. The issue is labelled `clawsweeper:source-repro` precisely because the failure chain is observable in source plus emitted log lines (see the verbatim repro evidence in the issue body), and the diff exercises the exact `autoCompactionCount > 0 && FallbackSummaryError` seam from that chain.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Triggering the live 120s LLM idle timeout would require either an actual provider hang or a configured billing-disabled fallback chain, neither of which is reliable to set up without disturbing other users. The source-level mocks reproduce the exact error shape from the repro log.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:2795-2805` would resolve `fallbackText` to either `BILLING_ERROR_USER_MESSAGE` (when `hasBillingAttemptSummary` is true) or `GENERIC_EXTERNAL_RUN_FAILURE_TEXT` (when no specific classifier matches) — both of which drop `autoCompactionCount` and the `FallbackSummaryError.attempts` cause chain on the floor.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` (149 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-helpers.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts` (194 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` (55 passed — sibling-surface check; followup-runner shares `autoCompactionCount` but uses a different non-user-facing failure path)
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (clean)
- `pnpm format:check` (clean)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Three new cases in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` under `describe("runAgentTurnWithFallback")`:

1. `surfaces post-compaction context when the retried turn times out (#67750)` — full repro of the bug-report failure chain (compaction succeeds; retried turn timeout; fallback exhausted with timeout + billing-skip attempts). Asserts the rendered text preserves compaction context, the resolved cause-specific copy, and the per-attempt summary.
2. `surfaces post-compaction context for plain failures without a fallback summary` — covers the generic catch-all branch when the error is a plain `Error` (no `FallbackSummaryError`), confirming the structured prefix and the specific next-step guidance replace the bare `/new` fallback.
3. `does not add post-compaction context when no compaction succeeded` — regression guard so the wrap stays scoped to `autoCompactionCount > 0` and ordinary unknown errors still resolve to `GENERIC_EXTERNAL_RUN_FAILURE_TEXT`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Test #1 above would have produced `result.payload.text === "billing"` (the mocked `BILLING_ERROR_USER_MESSAGE`), with no mention of compaction or the per-attempt timeout. Test #2 would have produced `result.payload.text === GENERIC_RUN_FAILURE_TEXT`, with no mention of compaction succeeding.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — only on the post-auto-compaction failure path. The pre-compaction failure path (`autoCompactionCount === 0`) returns the existing `fallbackText` unchanged, and existing tests for billing, rate-limit, gateway-restart, Codex usage-limit, missing-API-key, CLI subprocess timeout, and the generic copy all still assert their exact strings.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

User-facing copy on the post-compaction failure path. Wrong wording on a hot error path can confuse users about whether the message they sent was processed.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The wrap is gated on three independent predicates (`autoCompactionCount > 0`, `!params.isHeartbeat`, `!shouldSurfaceToControlUi`), so heartbeat probes and the control-UI logs path keep their existing copy.
- Cause-specific resolved text (`BILLING_ERROR_USER_MESSAGE`, rate-limit cooldown, Codex usage-limit, missing-API-key, OAuth re-auth, CLI timeout) is preserved verbatim — the wrap only prepends/appends, never replaces.
- All 149 existing `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` cases still pass (including every `surfaces X` copy contract).

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI run on the new commit.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet — first revision.

target.title: feat(feeds): add feed lifecycle tooling
target.body:
## Summary

Adds feed publisher/subscriber lifecycle tooling on top of the feed source and install primitives.

This PR adds:

- `openclaw feeds validate <path-or-url>` and `feeds hash <path-or-url>`.
- `openclaw feeds build --inventory <path> --out <path>` for deterministic curated feed artifacts.
- `openclaw feeds diff --previous <path> --current <path>` for publisher-side delta review.
- `openclaw feeds updates --installed <path>` to compare installed skill/plugin inventory against configured feeds.
- `openclaw feeds notices --installed <path>` for subscriber-facing update notices with install commands.
- Semver-aware update ordering, including prerelease vs stable comparisons.

## Example publisher flow

```bash
openclaw feeds validate ./company-feed.json --json
openclaw feeds hash ./company-feed.json
openclaw feeds build --inventory ./inventory.json --out ./company-feed.json --id company-approved
openclaw feeds diff --previous ./previous-feed.json --current ./company-feed.json --json
```

## Example subscriber flow

```bash
openclaw feeds updates --installed ./installed.json --approved-only --json
openclaw feeds notices --installed ./installed.json --approved-only --json
```

## Notes

This stays file-based and explicit. There is no daemon, background watch, auto-update, or ClawHub-native feed service in this PR. A later RFC/PR can add ClawHub-native feed support once the local feed contract is stable.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

target.title: fix(codex): clarify unsafe app-server completion stalls
target.body:
## Summary
- clarify the replay-unsafe Codex app-server timeout message so users know OpenClaw intentionally did not auto-replay after tool activity
- include completed item id/type/status/tool metadata in app-server idle-timeout diagnostics, matching the raw response diagnostics already emitted
- add focused coverage for completed item diagnostic details and update timeout outcome expectations

## Why
When Codex app-server emits completed tool/item notifications but never sends the terminal `turn/completed` event, OpenClaw currently reports that work may already have happened but does not explain that replay was intentionally suppressed to avoid duplicate side effects. This makes the incident look like a generic bot failure instead of a replay-safety guard. The extra diagnostic fields also make future reports easier to triage from trajectory/log data.

## Tests
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-codex-tests.json node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-results.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-results.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`

Related: #87781 handles replay-safe app-server stalls. This PR covers the replay-unsafe/user-facing side-effect path.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid constructing lean local model tools
target.body:
## Summary
- Apply `localModelLean` before constructing the heavyweight built-in tool surface.
- Skip constructing `browser`, `cron`, and `message` tools when lean mode denies them.
- Pass the same lean denylist through plugin and inherited tool construction so local models do not pay for tools they cannot use.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.ts src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.update-plan.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/local-model-lean.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.update-plan.test.ts --reporter=dot` (142 tests passed after rebase onto `f3cfd752`)
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed`: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_22f45e709982`, run `run_f5df43a422d5`, exit 0 on base `6f9d5e1b`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: local-model lean mode no longer constructs the denied `browser`, `cron`, or `message` tool surfaces before filtering them away.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree plus AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --timing-json --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
Evidence after fix: `run_f5df43a422d5` completed with exit 0 on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_22f45e709982`; clean rebases through `f3cfd752` then passed diff/format and the 142-test focused suite.
Observed result after fix: core/coreTests changed lanes passed, including typecheck, lint, guards, and import-cycle checks.
What was not tested: a live Windows local-model starvation repro; this PR reduces lean-mode tool construction pressure but does not claim to close every #86599 contributor.
