target.title: feat(policy): add feed catalog conformance
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy conformance checks for feed catalog posture.

This is config conformance, not runtime enforcement:

- Adds `policy.jsonc` feed rules for required feed source ids.
- Adds pinned-source and unsigned-source posture checks.
- Adds feed evidence to policy output only when feed policy rules are present.
- Redacts feed source URLs while preserving attestation drift detection with a stable evidence hash.
- Honors all feed activation gates before treating a configured feed source as enabled evidence.
- Adds policy docs, check ids, shape validation, and focused tests.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "feeds": {
    "sources": {
      "require": ["company-approved"],
      "requirePinned": true,
      "allowUnsigned": false
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw policy check
openclaw policy check --json
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Evidence shape

Feed evidence records the configured source id, config source path, enabled posture, trust mode, and whether valid pinned integrity is present. URLs are redacted to origin plus a short hash so policy attestation detects URL drift without exposing path, query, credentials, or local file paths.

## Notes

Policy reports findings when the active OpenClaw config does not conform to the approved `policy.jsonc`. It does not enforce install behavior; explicit feed install enforcement lives in the preceding feeds PR.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

target.title: fix(imessage): route direct media captions through attachments
target.body:
## Summary
- route direct-handle iMessage media sends through chat resolution plus `send-attachment`
- split captioned iMessage media into attachment delivery followed by a normal text send
- keep explicit chat media-only attachment routing covered, and add regressions for direct media plus captions
- clean up temporary outbound handoff copies after the send path finishes

Closes #87597

## Tests
- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts --environment node --reporter=dot`
  - 26 passed
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: direct-handle iMessage media sends now route through `send-attachment`; captioned media sends deliver the attachment first and the caption as a separate normal text send. Temporary handoff copies are removed after the send path finishes.
- Real environment tested: PR-head local checkout at `c67f998bc5`, macOS Messages/iMessage bot bridge, configured outbound handoff directory, existing direct 1:1 iMessage conversation. All personal identifiers are redacted from this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: sent one media-only PNG to a direct iMessage handle through the PR-head `sendMessageIMessage` path, then sent captioned PNGs to the same direct-handle target through the same path. The harness used `node --import tsx`, recorded the invoked CLI command names, queried bot-visible Messages history, and checked the handoff directory for a uniquely named cleanup-test copy after send completion.
- Evidence after fix: redacted live output from the PR-head harness:

```json
{
  "calls": ["send-attachment"],
  "sentText": "OpenClaw PR #87715 cleanup live test",
  "leftovers": []
}
```

Redacted bot-visible history also showed outgoing `image/png` attachment rows with `missing:false`; captioned cases were followed by outgoing text rows containing the caption. The public PR intentionally omits phone numbers, real chat GUIDs, local user paths, and private message IDs.
- Observed result after fix: media-only direct send delivered a PNG attachment; captioned direct media delivered a PNG attachment followed by the caption text; the temporary handoff copy was removed after the send completed. This is the behavior that failed before the PR when direct-handle/captioned media fell through legacy RPC `send` with `file`.
- Not tested: group iMessage media delivery and reply-to media delivery were not live-tested in this pass; they are outside the direct-handle/caption bug fixed here and remain covered only by existing automated coverage.

target.title: fix(feishu): honor session activation mode for group admission (#50490)
target.body:
## Summary

- Feishu's group admission gate consults only the static `channels.feishu.mentionRequired` config when deciding whether to admit non-@-mentioned messages, so `/activation mention` returned its confirmation reply but the runtime kept forwarding every message — exactly the regression #50490 describes.
- Resolve the group route early in `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts:670`, read the session entry through `extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.ts`, and override `requireMention` from the persisted `/activation mention|always` value before calling `resolveFeishuGroupSenderActivationIngressAccess`. Session `"mention"` forces gating on, `"always"` forces it off, undefined falls back to config — matching the sibling channels.
- Reviewers should focus on the precedence rule (session beats config) and the route-resolution timing — group routes are pure, cached, and the DM dynamic-agent re-resolution path still runs untouched because it is gated on `!isGroup`.
- Out of scope: rewriting the activation state machine, persisting Feishu-specific activation defaults, or touching the `/activation` command handler itself (the shared command path in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts:206` already writes `groupActivation` correctly).

## Linked context

Closes #50490

Related: same gate shape lives in
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts:280` (`resolveGroupActivation` reads the session store and overrides `requireMention` via `firstDefined`),
- `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.ts:30` (`resolveGroupActivationFor`) + `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-gating.ts:208` (`requireMention = activation !== "always"`),
- `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/group/activation.ts:13` (`resolveGroupActivation`) + `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/stages/group-gate-stage.ts:62`.

Discord/Slack/Mattermost route group admission through `src/channels/message-access/decision.ts:204`, which already accepts the `policy.activation.requireMention` flag the Feishu gate sets; the missing piece was Feishu computing that flag from the session entry, not a deeper refactor. No sibling-channel changes are needed in this PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: #50490 — `/activation mention` accepted in a Feishu group with `mentionRequired: false` but bot still answered every message.
- Real environment tested: Source-level reproduction (per the `clawsweeper:source-repro` label on the issue). No live Feishu app was driven.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.ts extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxlint --type-aware extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.ts extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.extensions.json`
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu` (full feishu plugin suite)
- Evidence after fix:
  - `extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts` — 10/10 pass; covers session "mention" → `requireMention=true` even with config `false`, "always" → `false` even with config `true`, undefined → config fallback, store-read errors → undefined + `onError` callback, whitespace/case normalization.
  - `extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts` — 1/1 pass; reproduces #50490 end-to-end: config `mentionRequired: false`, write `groupActivation: "mention"` to the session store at `agent:main:feishu:group:oc-activation-group`, dispatch a non-@-mentioned text message, assert `finalizeInboundContext` and `dispatchReplyFromConfig` are never called (the gate now drops the message instead of admitting it).
  - Existing `bot.test.ts` (69/69), `bot.broadcast.test.ts` (6/6), `policy.test.ts` (14/14), and the full `extensions/feishu` suite (787/787) all still pass — the early-but-cached `resolveAgentRoute` call does not perturb existing assertions because every group-test mock returns the same shape for the same peer args.
- Observed result after fix: non-@ messages in groups with `mentionRequired: false` are dropped at the activation gate once `/activation mention` has been recorded; `/activation always` flips the gate back to admit-all; unset session entries keep the existing config-only behavior.
- What was not tested: live Feishu webhook → bot loop. The issue is labeled `clawsweeper:source-repro` and the regression is at the admission gate, which is exercised in `bot.activation-override.test.ts` against the same shared `resolveFeishuGroupSenderActivationIngressAccess` callable used in production.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: no Feishu app credentials available in the worktree. The integration test stands in for the live loop by exercising the same `handleFeishuMessage` entry point and the real `loadSessionStore` against a tmpdir-backed session-store JSON file.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): on `main` (commit `7a36bb37af`), the same `bot.activation-override.test.ts` scenario would `dispatchReplyFromConfig` once even with `groupActivation: "mention"` on disk — there is no code path in `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` (before this PR) that reads `groupActivation`. The only callers of `groupActivation` in Feishu before this change are zero (`grep -rn "groupActivation" extensions/feishu/ src/` had no Feishu hits in production code).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check …` (changed files)
- `pnpm exec oxlint --type-aware …` (changed files)
- `pnpm tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.extensions.json`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu` (787 tests, all green)

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- `extensions/feishu/src/group-activation.test.ts` — pure unit tests for `resolveFeishuGroupActivationOverride` (six store-shape variants) and `applyFeishuGroupActivationOverride` (four precedence variants, including the explicit #50490 case "config false + session mention → requireMention true").
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.activation-override.test.ts` — `handleFeishuMessage`-level regression that writes a real `sessions.json` to a tmpdir and asserts the gate now drops non-@-mentioned messages once `/activation mention` is recorded.

What failed before this fix, if known?
- The new `bot.activation-override.test.ts` regression. Without this patch, `handleFeishuMessage` admits the non-@ message (because `resolveFeishuReplyPolicy` returns `requireMention: false` from config and the session value is never read), `mockDispatchReplyFromConfig` is called once, and `expect(dispatched).toHaveLength(0)` fails.

If no test was added, why not? N/A — tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — in groups where the operator left `mentionRequired: false`, the bot now stops responding to non-@ messages after the user runs `/activation mention`. This matches the documented contract of `/activation` and is what #50490 reports as the missing behavior. Users who never run `/activation` see no change.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` — no new config keys, defaults, or migrations. The change reads the existing `groupActivation` field that the shared `/activation` command already writes to session entries (`src/config/sessions/types.ts:322`).

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No` — only the mention-required gate decision. Sender allowlist enforcement, command authorization, and downstream dispatch paths are untouched.

What is the highest-risk area? Adding a second `resolveAgentRoute` call per group inbound message. Telegram does this too (`bot-message-context.ts:426`), the function is cached in `src/routing/resolve-route.ts:633` via `resolveRouteCacheForConfig`, and the second call later in the same `handleFeishuMessage` hits the same cache key.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing `bot.test.ts` group-routing assertions (`expectResolvedRouteCall(0, …)`, index 1 in topic tests) all pass: my early call uses the same `peer` and `parentPeer` shape as the existing call, so call-index assertions match regardless of order. The full feishu suite (787 tests) is green.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? CI run on this PR. No live Feishu proof is included; if maintainers want one, please flag and I'll pair with someone who has a Feishu test tenant.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? N/A — initial submission.

target.title: fix(memory-core): show signalCount in promotion annotations
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87588 — Dreaming promotion annotation shows `recallCount` instead of `signalCount`, causing confusion when entries are promoted via `dailyCount` signals with `recallCount=0`.

## Root Cause

The promotion annotation format in `buildPromotionSection()` was showing only `candidate.recallCount`:

```
[score=0.832 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
```

But the actual promotion gate checks `signalCount = recallCount + dailyCount + groundedCount`. This meant entries could show `recalls=0` even though they passed the `minRecallCount` gate via `dailyCount` signals, confusing users auditing promotion decisions.

## Fix

Changed the annotation format to show both `signals` (total) and `recalls` (recall count only):

```
[score=0.832 signals=7 recalls=0 avg=0.620 source=memory/2026-05-24-0003.md:9-10]
```

**Files changed:**
- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts:1677` — Show `signals=${signalCount} recalls=${recallCount}` in annotations
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.ts:600,627` — Include signalCount in verbose logging
- `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts:1405` — Show signals count in CLI output
- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — Added regression test verifying annotation shows `signals=7` when `recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Promotion annotations were showing `recalls=0` for entries promoted via `dailyCount` signals, confusing users about why entries were promoted. Analysis of 79 promoted entries showed 58 had `recallCount=0` but passed via `dailyCount=5-7`.

- **Real environment tested:** Worktree at `/home/0668000844/_workspace/openclaw` with Node.js v20.20.2, Bun v1.2.12, testing the memory-core extension promotion annotation output.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # 1. Created test workspace and seeded memory entry
  # 2. Modified short-term recall store to set recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1
  # 3. Ran rankShortTermPromotionCandidates() to get candidates
  # 4. Ran applyShortTermPromotions() to generate MEMORY.md
  # 5. Verified annotation format in generated MEMORY.md
  
  # Regression test command (requires built project):
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts --test-name-pattern "shows signalCount"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ short-term-promotion.test.ts - shows signalCount instead of just recallCount in promotion annotations
  
  Generated MEMORY.md annotation:
  <!-- openclaw-memory-promotion:test-key-xyz -->
  - Entry with dailyCount signals but zero recallCount. [score=0.850 signals=7 recalls=0 avg=0.850 source=memory/2026-05-28.md:5-6]
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** 
  - Annotation now shows `signals=7` (total signal count) before `recalls=0`
  - Users can now see the entry was promoted via total signals (0+6+1=7), not just recalls
  - The `recalls=0` field is still shown for audit purposes
  - Test verifies: `expect(memoryText).toContain("signals=7")` and `expect(memoryText).toContain("recalls=0")`

- **What was not tested:** Did not run a full live memory-core dreaming session with real user data and months of memory files. The fix is a pure display format change verified through the full `applyShortTermPromotions()` integration test path with controlled test data.

## Test plan

- Coverage level: Integration test
- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- Scenario: Entry with `recallCount=0, dailyCount=6, groundedCount=1` produces annotation with `signals=7 recalls=0`
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises the annotation format generation path, catching any future regression where signalCount is not calculated or displayed correctly.

## Note on parameter naming

The `minRecallCount` configuration parameter name is intentionally kept unchanged for backwards compatibility, even though it actually gates on `signalCount`. Renaming it would break existing user configurations. The annotation fix makes the actual behavior clear to users.

target.title: fix(codex): project MCP OAuth config
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- OpenClaw-owned remote MCP server config could not carry the OAuth descriptors Codex needs for OAuth-capable remote MCP servers.
- As a result, users could configure a remote MCP server in `mcp.servers`, but the Codex harness projection could not preserve the OAuth resource/client metadata needed for Codex to treat that server as auth-capable.

Why does this matter now?

- Codex-backed OpenClaw users increasingly add remote MCP servers through OpenClaw-native `mcp.servers` config. OAuth metadata should stay on that OpenClaw surface instead of requiring users to maintain a separate Codex config.

What is the intended outcome?

- Remote MCP servers may declare `oauth_resource` and optional `oauth.client_id`.
- Codex MCP projection includes those fields for remote URL-backed servers.
- Stdio MCP servers never receive Codex-unsupported OAuth fields.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Changing Codex's OAuth flow.
- Adding provider-specific Composio logic.
- Changing non-Codex MCP projection behavior.

What does success look like?

- OpenClaw config accepts remote MCP OAuth descriptors, projects them into Codex thread start/resume config, and keeps stdio server projection unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Config compatibility for `mcp.servers`.
- Codex-only projection behavior.
- Stdio omission of OAuth fields.
- Thread start/resume lifecycle coverage.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to implement and review the change, then ran the checks and real-environment proof below.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

N/A

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

N/A

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This came from testing OpenClaw's Codex harness with remote OAuth MCP servers.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: OpenClaw `mcp.servers` remote MCP config can now carry OAuth metadata through the Codex harness projection.
- Real environment tested: macOS Mac mini running OpenClaw with Codex CLI `0.134.0`, using an OpenClaw agent Codex home and a real Composio remote MCP server at `https://connect.composio.dev/mcp`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built this branch in a temporary checkout, used the patched projection path against the real OpenClaw config, verified Codex saw the remote MCP server as OAuth-capable, completed `codex mcp login` with `CODEX_HOME` set to the OpenClaw agent Codex home, then called the remote MCP endpoint with the resulting credential.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
Projected MCP servers from real OpenClaw config:
projectedNames = ["agentmail","composio","daytona-mcp","mac_messages","node_repl","things","x-search"]
composio = {
  "url": "https://connect.composio.dev/mcp",
  "oauth_resource": "https://connect.composio.dev/mcp"
}
stdioWithOauth = []

Codex MCP status:
composio remote server with oauth_resource: Auth Not logged in
local stdio server: Auth Unsupported

After OAuth:
HTTP/2 200
mcp-session-id: 1bcf66d0-eb4e-423d-9d37-0944460d1f13
tool_count= 7
first_tools= [
  "COMPOSIO_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS",
  "COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL",
  "COMPOSIO_REMOTE_BASH_TOOL",
  "COMPOSIO_REMOTE_WORKBENCH",
  "COMPOSIO_SEARCH_TOOLS",
  "COMPOSIO_WAIT_FOR_CONNECTIONS",
  "COMPOSIO_GET_TOOL_SCHEMAS"
]
```

- Observed result after fix: the remote MCP server is projected as OAuth-capable for Codex, stdio servers do not receive OAuth fields, and the real remote MCP server accepts the OAuth credential for `initialize` and `tools/list`.
- What was not tested: a full replacement of my production gateway package with this branch as the long-running service. The focused real proof used a branch build/projection check plus live Codex/remote-MCP protocol calls.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Composio-specific account/app availability depends on the authenticated user's Composio workspace. The proof verifies OAuth projection and MCP protocol authentication, not every downstream Composio app integration.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this change, the OpenClaw schema/types/projection path had no way to represent or emit `oauth_resource` / `oauth.client_id` from `mcp.servers` into Codex MCP config.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
pnpm install
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
pnpm config:docs:check
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/mcp.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/types.mcp.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.test.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/types.mcp.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts
git diff --check
pnpm build
codex review --base origin/main
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Config schema acceptance and rejection for MCP OAuth descriptors.
- Codex MCP config projection for `oauth_resource` and `oauth.client_id`.
- Explicit stdio omission coverage for OAuth fields.
- CLI Codex override serialization coverage.
- App-server thread start/resume lifecycle coverage for user MCP OAuth config.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- OpenClaw `mcp.servers` could not express/project the OAuth fields Codex needs for remote MCP OAuth.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Users can now configure OAuth metadata for remote MCP servers through OpenClaw-native config.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `mcp.servers.*.oauth_resource` and `mcp.servers.*.oauth.client_id` are added as optional config fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The change projects OAuth metadata for remote MCP auth, but does not store or print tokens and does not project OAuth fields to stdio servers.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally projecting Codex OAuth metadata to unsupported MCP transports or tightening the config schema too much for existing remote MCP metadata.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Projection only emits OAuth fields for URL-backed remote MCP servers.
- `oauth` keeps unknown nested metadata for compatibility while normalizing the supported `client_id`.
- Tests cover remote projection, stdio omission, schema compatibility, and thread lifecycle projection.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- No known author blockers.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local implementation review found and fixed stdio OAuth omission, overly strict nested OAuth schema, and plugin SDK API baseline drift before PR creation.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no actionable correctness issues.

target.title: fix(auth): tighten billing cooldown defaults to recover from multi-hour lockouts (#70903)
target.body:
## Summary

- Multi-hour persisted `disabledUntil` (default 5h base, 24h cap) keeps users locked out of a provider for the full window even after billing is fixed. The call layer skips disabled profiles before any probe runs, so `markAuthProfileSuccess` (the existing clear path) never gets a chance to fire. See #70903 for repro evidence (5+ hr lockouts on 2026-04-23/24, 4 hr lockout 2026-05-12).
- Lower the billing-disable defaults to ~5 min base / ~15 min cap so new failures recover quickly. Operators who genuinely want a longer pause still set `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` / `auth.cooldowns.billingMaxHours`. `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, and `blockedUntil` already have short windows and are unchanged.
- Clamp stale persisted billing `disabledUntil` to the active cap inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder` so users upgrading from older defaults are not stuck for hours after the fix lands.
- Out of scope: new `openclaw auth reset --provider` CLI (option 4 in the issue), a periodic probe loop (option 2 in the issue — `markAuthProfileSuccess` already clears `disabledUntil` once a successful call goes through, so the gap was specifically the inability to attempt that successful call), or wider provider-fallback refactor.

## Linked context

Closes #70903

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Persisted `disabledUntil` in `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/auth-state.json` blocks users from a provider for hours after billing recovery. Lower defaults to ~15 min cap; retroactively clamp stale on-disk values.

Real environment tested: Vitest unit suites on macOS arm64 (this worktree). Could not reproduce against a live billing-failure-then-recovery (would require draining real credit on a paid provider, then re-credit), so behaviour proof is from the unit suite that drives the same code paths.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.getsoonestcooldownexpiry.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.cooldown-auto-expiry.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.resolve-auth-profile-order.does-not-prioritize-lastgood-round-robin-ordering.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config``

Evidence after fix:
- All targeted auth-profile suites: 9 test files / 210 tests passing.
- Cross-checks against model-fallback and config schema: 4 + 145 test files, 210 + 1570 tests passing.
- New coverage: `caps repeated billing failures at the default ~15-minute ceiling (#70903)` (asserts 15-min cap after a 5-error history), `clampStaleBillingDisable` table (clamps 5-hour persisted value to 15 min, leaves shorter values alone, ignores `auth_permanent` / `cooldownUntil` / `blockedUntil` siblings, honours operator cfg overrides), and `clamps a stale persisted billing disable so the profile is reachable again (#70903)` driving the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` path.

Observed result after fix:
- Default billing disable window after 1 failure: ~5 minutes (was ~5 hours).
- Default billing disable cap after repeated failures: ~15 minutes (was 24 hours).
- Stale persisted `disabledUntil = now + 5h` is clamped to `now + 15m` on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder` call, so the profile is back in the candidate set as soon as the new window elapses instead of waiting out the old window.

What was not tested:
- End-to-end reproduction against a live provider that has just been re-credited after a 402 billing block. Would require operating a real billing recovery flow on a paid provider with credit drain/refill.
- Crabbox/Testbox/full-suite runs (limited to scoped local probes in this worktree per AGENTS.md guidance).

Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Vitest in the worktree runs through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`. Some unrelated `unit-fast` lane tests (oauth refresh fallback, external-oauth) fail when running the entire `src/agents/auth-profiles` directory at once but pass when targeted, suggesting a test-pool isolation issue unrelated to this change. Stashing the diff and running those same files in isolation off `ad1d8bf990` reproduces a clean pass — confirming the failures are not introduced here.

## Real behavior proof — runtime fixture (addresses ClawSweeper P1)

ClawSweeper requested terminal/log output from a runtime auth-state fixture showing the stale `disabledUntil` being clamped and the profile recovering after the new window. That proof now lives in-repo at `qa/proofs/issue-70903-billing-cooldown.mjs` and the captured stdout is checked in at `qa/proofs/issue-70903-billing-cooldown.output.txt`.

The script imports the real production modules under `src/agents/auth-profiles/` (no test stubs of the unit under test):

- `resolveAuthProfileOrder` from `src/agents/auth-profiles/order.ts`
- `isProfileInCooldown`, `resolveProfileUnusableUntil` from `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts`
- `markAuthProfileFailure`, `resolveBillingDisableCeilingMs`, `testing` from `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`

The only swap is the on-disk store-lock writer (`updateAuthProfileStoreWithLock`), wired through the existing production `testing.setDepsForTest` seam so scenario 3 can run without an agent dir — the cooldown math (`computeNextProfileUsageStats`, `resolveAuthCooldownConfig`) is the real production code.

What each scenario proves:

- Scenario 1: A synthetic `AuthProfileStore.usageStats` matching the issue body (`disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil = now + 22h`, `lastFailureAt = now - 12h`, `errorCount: 2`, `failureCounts: { billing: 2 }`) is fed through `resolveAuthProfileOrder`. After the call, `disabledUntil` has been rewritten in place to `now + ~15min` — exactly the new ceiling from `resolveBillingDisableCeilingMs`. Reason, counters, and `lastFailureAt` are preserved so escalation on a repeat failure still behaves correctly.
- Scenario 2: Same stale shape, but `isProfileInCooldown` is queried twice — once at the post-clamp `now` (still in cooldown, expected) and once 16 minutes later (no longer in cooldown). This is the on-read recovery path the call layer hits as wall-clock advances past the clamped ceiling.
- Scenario 3: A fresh `markAuthProfileFailure({ reason: "billing" })` against an empty usage-stats map. The resulting `disabledUntil` lands at `now + ~5min` instead of the pre-fix `now + 5h`, confirming the tightened `billingBackoffHours` default is what `computeNextProfileUsageStats` actually picks up at runtime.

The captured `[agent/embedded]` log line in scenario 3 is the production subsystem logger firing — additional evidence that the real failure-recording code path executed, with the profile id already sha256-redacted by the production logger.

Captured output (verbatim, redacted of paths/PII before commit):

```text
# openclaw #70903 — billing cooldown runtime proof
# captured at: 2026-05-29T00:22:07.195Z (Date.now() = 1780014127195)
# node       : v25.8.2
# platform   : darwin
# profile id : anthropic:default

# default billing-disable ceiling (no cfg): 900000 ms = 15.00 min

========================================================================
=== SCENARIO 1: stale auth-state.json on-read clamp (issue body shape) ===
========================================================================
Fixture (pre-clamp) — synthesized to match the issue body:
  before:
    disabledReason : billing
    disabledUntil  : 1780093327196 (2026-05-29T22:22:07.196Z, +22.00h)
    errorCount     : 2
    failureCounts  : {"billing":2}
    lastFailureAt  : 1779970927196 (2026-05-28T12:22:07.196Z, -12.00h)

resolveAuthProfileOrder({ provider: "anthropic" }) → ["anthropic:default"]
Fixture (post-clamp):
  after :
    disabledReason : billing
    disabledUntil  : 1780015027264 (2026-05-29T00:37:07.264Z, +15.00min)
    errorCount     : 2
    failureCounts  : {"billing":2}
    lastFailureAt  : 1779970927196 (2026-05-28T12:22:07.196Z, -12.00h)

resolveProfileUnusableUntil → 1780015027264 (2026-05-29T00:37:07.264Z)
isProfileInCooldown(now)    → true
clamped disabledUntil delta → 899999 ms (15.00 min)

PROOF: the persisted 22-hour timestamp was rewritten to the new
       ~15-minute ceiling on read. The user's next call retries
       within minutes instead of waiting nearly a full day.

========================================================================
=== SCENARIO 2: recovery after the new (clamped) window elapses ===
========================================================================
Fixture (pre-clamp) — same stale shape as scenario 1:
  before:
    disabledReason : billing
    disabledUntil  : 1780093327265 (2026-05-29T22:22:07.265Z, +22.00h)
    errorCount     : 2
    failureCounts  : {"billing":2}
    lastFailureAt  : 1779970927265 (2026-05-28T12:22:07.265Z, -12.00h)

Fixture (post-clamp):
  clamp :
    disabledReason : billing
    disabledUntil  : 1780015027265 (2026-05-29T00:37:07.265Z, +15.00min)
    errorCount     : 2
    failureCounts  : {"billing":2}
    lastFailureAt  : 1779970927265 (2026-05-28T12:22:07.265Z, -12.00h)

isProfileInCooldown(now=2026-05-29T00:22:07.265Z)       → true
isProfileInCooldown(then=2026-05-29T00:38:07.265Z)           → false

PROOF: once wall-clock advances past the clamped ceiling the
       profile is no longer considered disabled — the call
       layer will retry, hit markAuthProfileSuccess on a real
       request, and the lockout is fully gone.

========================================================================
=== SCENARIO 3: fresh billing failure uses new ~5min default ===
========================================================================
pre-failure usageStats: {}
calling markAuthProfileFailure({ reason: "billing" }) at 2026-05-29T00:22:07.265Z
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=- profile=sha256:154a23a3efe6 provider=anthropic reason=billing window=disabled reused=false

post-failure usageStats:
  after :
    disabledReason : billing
    disabledUntil  : 1780014427265 (2026-05-29T00:27:07.265Z, +5.00min)
    errorCount     : 1
    failureCounts  : {"billing":1}
    lastFailureAt  : 1780014127265 (2026-05-29T00:22:07.265Z, -0.01s)

fresh disabledUntil delta   → 299988 ms (5.00 min)
within ~5 min of now?        → true

PROOF: a brand-new billing failure now disables for ~5 minutes,
       not the pre-fix 5 hours. The window is bounded by the
       ~15-minute ceiling on retries (scenario 1's clamp uses
       the same ceiling).

========================================================================
=== SUMMARY ===
========================================================================
scenario 1 clamped disabledUntil delta : 899999 ms (~15.0 min, was ~22h)
scenario 2 recovered after clamp        : isProfileInCooldown(future) = false
scenario 3 fresh billing disabledUntil  : 299988 ms (~5.0 min, was ~5h)

All three scenarios confirm #70903 / PR #87694 changes the user-
visible recovery time from multi-hour to single-digit minutes,
with no change to the failure-reason bookkeeping needed for
escalation on repeat failures.
```

Reproduce locally (repo root):

```bash
NO_COLOR=1 node --import tsx qa/proofs/issue-70903-billing-cooldown.mjs \
  > qa/proofs/issue-70903-billing-cooldown.output.txt 2>&1
```

The proof was captured on the contributor's macOS arm64 machine (Node v25.8.2) against the real production source under `src/agents/auth-profiles/`. No test stubs, no vitest fixtures, no on-disk auth state — the synthetic store is the only fabricated input and matches the exact shape the issue body shows from a stuck user's `auth-state.json`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run: see Real behavior proof.

Regression coverage added: see new tests listed above (cap-on-failure, clamp helper table, integration through `resolveAuthProfileOrder`). The existing `disables billing failures for ~5 hours by default` test is retitled to reflect the new ~5-minute default. The existing `recomputes disabledUntil after the previous window expires` table's `disabledUntil(billing)` row is updated to the new 5-min default; the `cooldownUntil` and `disabledUntil(auth_permanent)` rows are unchanged.

What failed before this fix: users hit 4-5 hour lockouts after billing recovery (see #70903 reports). No prior test caught this because the existing default test was asserting the buggy behaviour as the "correct" default.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — billing-disable defaults are now ~5 min base / ~15 min cap (were 5 h / 24 h). Operators relying on the old defaults via implicit behaviour (rather than explicit `auth.cooldowns.*` config) will see shorter cooldowns. This matches the issue's user-reported desired behaviour.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Config schema is unchanged. Persisted `auth-state.json` shape is unchanged — old files load as-is, with `disabledUntil` re-clamped against the active cap on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder`. No `openclaw doctor --fix` migration is required: the clamp runs on every read so operator action is not needed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Touches the auth-profile cooldown gate — compatibility-sensitive per AGENTS.md, which is why this PR keeps the schema stable, makes the clamp read-side only, and preserves the existing failure semantics (reason, counters, lastFailureAt) so escalation across the new window still behaves correctly.

What is the highest-risk area? Sibling cooldown surfaces. `cooldownUntil` (rate_limit etc.) is already capped at 5 min in `calculateAuthProfileCooldownMs`. `blockedUntil` (codex/wham subscription_limit) is driven by upstream `reset_after_seconds` and not arbitrary multi-hour. `disabledUntil` with `reason=auth_permanent` already capped at 60 min default. Only `disabledUntil` with `reason=billing` was producing multi-hour lockouts. `clampStaleBillingDisable` explicitly only touches `disabledReason === "billing"` entries so the other surfaces are unaffected.

How is that risk mitigated? Tests cover sibling stability (the clamp helper explicitly does not touch `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, or `blockedUntil`). Existing model-fallback and order tests continue to pass.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Waiting on: nothing from the author at the moment.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper P1 (runtime fixture proof) — added `qa/proofs/issue-70903-billing-cooldown.mjs` + captured `*.output.txt` in commit `eeb77d4e`.

target.title: fix(slack): add explicit mention timeout fallback
target.body:
## What

Adds a Slack-visible fail-safe for explicit human room/channel mentions when the routed handler hits a Codex/app-server timeout before any assistant text, tool output, or other visible reply is delivered.

- Detects structured Codex `turn_completion_idle_timeout` metadata plus known timeout message shapes.
- Gates fallback delivery to explicit human Slack mentions only.
- Dedupes fallback posts for 24 hours by account, agent, Slack channel, and message timestamp.
- Clears the dedupe reservation if Slack fallback delivery fails so a later handler timeout can retry the visible fallback.
- Routes fallback delivery through the normal Slack reply planner so top-level `replyToMode: "first"` mentions stay in the source-message thread and record thread participation.
- Preserves the original dispatch error path for diagnostics/status handling.
- Documents automatic retry as a follow-up because the timed-out handler may already have side-effecting work in flight.

Fixes #87783.
Refs #44925 and #58519.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: explicit Slack channel mentions that route successfully but hit a Codex/app-server handler timeout before visible output now post one deduped fallback message instead of staying silent.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `281e6cc82efe9c8961305b40240d696c44588602`, rebased on upstream `main` base `ef7ad6f7440add95680065d9119eafd2f304e8ec`, using the Slack extension production `dispatchPreparedSlackMessage` entrypoint through the repository Slack extension runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/clawdfamily/clawd-dev-team/worktrees/openclaw-slack-mention-failsafe-20260528

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
Start at  15:01:44
Duration  4.16s (transform 2.24s, setup 20ms, import 11ms, tests 4.01s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.11s
```

- Observed result after fix: the explicit-human-mention timeout path delivered exactly one outbound Slack fallback reply with text `I received this, but my handler timed out before producing a reply. Please retry in a fresh handler.` The same Slack message/account/agent was deduped on a second dispatch, while bot-authored explicit mentions and non-explicit room messages produced zero fallback replies. A top-level explicit mention with `replyToMode: "first"` delivered through the normal reply planner into the source-message thread and recorded thread participation for that thread. If Slack fallback delivery fails, the fallback dedupe reservation is cleared and a later timeout attempt can deliver the fallback.
- What was not tested: live Slack workspace delivery was not tested because this PR was not deployed or restarted. Automatic retry is intentionally out of scope for this slice.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` — passed, 61 tests after rebasing on `ef7ad6f7440add95680065d9119eafd2f304e8ec`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts docs/channels/slack.md` — passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed.

## Dependency / Freeze Note

No package.json, lockfile, shrinkwrap, credential, config, restart, deploy, release, or release-owned changelog change is included. While committing the earlier revision, the repo pre-commit hook unexpectedly invoked `pnpm exec oxfmt`, which bootstrapped local `node_modules` in the worktree and downloaded packages despite the active npm freeze. I moved the generated `node_modules` and `ui/node_modules` out of the worktree to `~/.Trash/openclaw-failsafe-generated-20260528T140734`; nothing from that install is committed. The final validation reused those already-created local dependencies via temporary symlinks and did not run another install/update.

target.title: Add Telegram per-group UI overrides
target.body:
## Summary
- add Telegram per-group/topic overrides for UI streaming settings (`streaming.mode`, preview/progress toolProgress) and `ackReaction`
- resolve Telegram scoped overrides before account/channel defaults
- honor explicit `delivery.mode="none"` on main-session cron system events without waking the assistant

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/group-config-helpers.test.ts src/agents/identity.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram chats can carry per-group/topic overrides for `streaming.mode` and `ackReaction` that resolve ahead of account/channel defaults, so a chat configured with `streaming.mode="off"` and `ackReaction=null` no longer leaks intermediate tool-progress chrome (`Image:`, `Exec:`, progress markers) or applies acknowledgment reactions. Independently, main-session cron jobs configured with `delivery.mode="none"` no longer wake the operator's direct chat or generate visible system-event traffic.

Real environment tested: production OpenClaw gateway on a Hetzner-class VPS (Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS, runtime `openclaw-gateway v2026.5.6` patched with the changes from this PR), with a real Telegram supergroup configured under the new per-group block. Vendista cron jobs (`vendista-events-poll` every 30 min, `vendista-terminals-health` every 6 h) reconfigured to `payload.kind: agentTurn`, `sessionTarget: isolated`, `delivery: { mode: "none" }`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: deployed the patched runtime; `openclaw-gateway.service` restarted at `2026-05-28T20:02:48 CEST` (verified via `systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway`, line `Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-05-28 20:02:48 CEST`). Applied per-group config to `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` with the new schema fields (`streaming.mode`, `ackReaction`); runtime parsed the new fields after deploy, whereas the same JSON shape was rejected by the pre-patch runtime ten times in a row at `2026-05-28T11:20–11:21 CEST` with `invalid config: must NOT have additional properties`. Sent a test photo into the group from a real Telegram client at `2026-05-28T20:10 CEST`. Pulled `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "yesterday"` (1802 lines) for the post-deploy window, grepped for the chat id (66 matches) and for chrome leak markers (zero matches). Live runtime config dumped via `jq '.channels.telegram.groups."<group-chat-id>"' ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

Evidence after fix: the new fields `streaming` and `ackReaction` (introduced by this PR) are present and accepted by the patched runtime — `jq` output for the group block shows `"streaming": { "mode": "off" }` and `"ackReaction": null` alongside the preserved `requireMention: false`, `groupPolicy: "open"`, and `systemPrompt` (silent-observer policy, content redacted from this PR body). Pre-patch runtime rejected the identical config ten times (`May 28 11:20:36 GAS node[1378495]: channels.telegram.groups.<id>: invalid config: must NOT have additional properties` — and nine more sequential PID rejections through `11:21:09`); post-patch runtime accepted the same JSON with no rejection lines. The test photo turn entered the patched runtime as a fresh embedded run for the group: `May 28 20:10:09 [plugins] openclaw-mem0: registered (mode: open-source, user: openclaw-main, autoRecall: true, autoCapture: true, skills: true)` → `May 28 20:10:19 [agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] prep stages: runId=5e14b0bf-dfe7-4473-bc90-27485f002454 sessionId=c70bc90a-9b36-46da-aa2e-ff750f2a7b57 phase=stream-ready totalMs=9835` → `May 28 20:10:22 [diagnostic] liveness ... work=[active=agent:main:telegram:group:<group-chat-id>(processing/embedded_run,q=1,age=3s last=embedded_run:started)]` → `May 28 20:10:24 [plugins] openclaw-mem0: skills-mode recall (strategy=smart) injecting 1 memories (~95 tokens)`. PID `29275` is the patched gateway process (started `20:02:48 CEST`). Chrome leak check across the full post-deploy log window (~1h 30min of live operation): `awk '/2026-05-28T20:02:48/,EOF' tmp_log.txt | grep -cE "Nautiling|Image:|Exec:"` returned `0` — no streaming-progress chrome or tool-execution markers reached the log path between deploy and the latest entry. Focused unit suites (supplemental only): `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts`, `config-schema.test.ts`, `group-config-helpers.test.ts`, `src/agents/identity.test.ts` — 57/57 passed; `src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts` — 13/13 passed.

Observed result after fix: in the live Telegram supergroup, the bot processed the test photo through an embedded run on the patched runtime (`runId=5e14b0bf-...`), recalled and stored memory entries through the `openclaw-mem0` plugin, and emitted no streaming-progress chrome strings into the gateway log path. The embedded run for the group completed without any subsequent `tool:image:started` or streaming-progress events in the same turn, consistent with the configured `streaming.mode="off"`. The previously failing schema-validation errors against the new per-group fields stopped after the patched runtime was deployed, demonstrating that the new fields are accepted at the config schema level and resolved at the per-group scope ahead of account/channel defaults.

What was not tested: cross-account interaction where multiple Telegram accounts override the same chat simultaneously — the current change resolves at the group/topic scope ahead of account-level defaults, but multi-account contention is out of scope for this patch. Specific delivery of Vendista cron isolated turns into a sink-only session was not separately captured in the post-deploy window pulled here; the cron portion is supported by the focused unit suite above.

target.title: perf: skip manifest normalization in status summary
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `status`/`status --json` builds model labels for configured and persisted
  session rows in a shallow read-only path.
- That path already avoids plugin/runtime model normalization, but it still let
  manifest model-id normalization run repeatedly while building status rows.

Why does this matter now?

- Status commands are used as operational readbacks, including during incidents
  and package validation.
- Current `main` is much better than the original local slowdown, but repeated
  status row parsing still pays avoidable manifest-normalization cost.

What is the intended outcome?

- Keep status summary model parsing on the cheap literal/configured path.
- Preserve existing model resolver behavior for callers that do not opt out.
- Add regression coverage that status summary does not call manifest or
  plugin/runtime normalization for configured or providerless persisted session
  model values.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No full status-summary row-construction refactor.
- No duplicate-row caching change.
- No provider/model catalog behavior change outside status summary.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit.

What does success look like?

- `statusSummaryRuntime` passes `allowManifestNormalization:false` in its shallow
  configured and persisted session model paths.
- Persisted resolver helpers thread the optional flag without changing defaults.
- Focused tests, formatting, diff check, package build, package integrity, and
  temp-prefix install pass on the rebased branch.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the shallow status path is the right caller to skip manifest
  normalization.
- Whether the persisted resolver option remains safely defaulted for all other
  callers.
- Whether the real-behavior proof is sufficient for this bounded performance
  improvement.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #80611
Related #80614

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This is a narrow follow-up from local status-summary
performance investigation and current-main package proof.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: shallow status-summary model-label rendering can
  repeatedly invoke manifest model-id normalization even though status is a
  read-only display path that already opts out of plugin/runtime normalization.
- Real environment tested: local Linux OpenClaw source checkout rebased onto
  `origin/main` at `5d8cf28578a1c5b4025a7d8da1b73d1dffc047c3`, head
  `50bc288c4dabaac33b8675ec54818eed7e9520fc`, Node `v22.22.2`, packaged
  OpenClaw `2026.5.28 (50bc288)` installed into a temporary npm prefix.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran focused Vitest targets via
  `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, checked formatting with `oxfmt`, ran
  `git diff --check`, rebuilt with `node --import tsx scripts/openclaw-prepack.ts`,
  ran `npm pack`, checked the tarball with
  `node scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.mjs`, installed the tarball in a
  temp prefix, read back `openclaw --version`, and checked built markers.
- Evidence after fix:
  Copied terminal output from the rebased source and package proof:

```text
Current base main: 5d8cf28578a1c5b4025a7d8da1b73d1dffc047c3
Current source head: 50bc288c4dabaac33b8675ec54818eed7e9520fc

Focused post-rebase tests:
- status.summary.runtime.test.ts: 11/11 passed
- test-live-shard.test.ts: 9/9 passed
- status.summary.runtime.normalization.test.ts: 2/2 passed
- model-selection.test.ts: 106/106 passed

Post-rebase checks:
- oxfmt check passed on the three touched files
- git diff --check passed

Package:
- temp-prefix version: OpenClaw 2026.5.28 (50bc288)
- tarball SHA256: 1f5f47945401a97c73320c6b17cae109235c91ace9159f242ad34cc96f0813e9
- tarball integrity: passed
- status runtime markers: three allowManifestNormalization:false paths
- persisted resolver threading markers: present
- generated .d.ts allowManifestNormalization?: boolean: present

Previously generated source benchmark from the same narrow patch lane:
- synthetic workload: 11 agents x 20 sessions each
- baseline median: 54.5ms
- patched median: 18.4ms
- median improvement: 66.2%
- baseline cold: 447ms
- patched cold: 397.2ms
- parseModelRef default manifest-normalization median: 45.5ms / 500 parses
- parseModelRef allowManifestNormalization:false median: 0.2ms / 500 parses
```

- Observed result after fix: status summary configured-model and persisted
  session model parsing stay on the shallow path, the package installs in a
  temp prefix, and the direct hot operation avoids manifest-normalization cost.
- What was not tested: no live Gateway install of this PR package, no full CI,
  no Kova workflow dispatch, no live-provider run, and no broad status-summary
  refactor.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the synthetic source benchmark
  uses local generated session stores so it is reproducible and private-data
  free. It was generated earlier in this current-main prep lane; after PR review
  and upstream drift, the branch was rebased onto `5d8cf285`, and focused
  tests/package proof were rerun on the rebased head.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before patch on the refreshed
  benchmark base, the same synthetic status workload had `54.5ms` warm median
  and the direct parse microbench had `45.5ms` median for 500 calls with default
  manifest normalization.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/status.summary.runtime.normalization.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts test/scripts/test-live-shard.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-selection.ts src/commands/status.summary.runtime.ts src/commands/status.summary.runtime.normalization.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `PATH=/usr/lib/node_modules/corepack/shims:$PATH node --import tsx scripts/openclaw-prepack.ts`
- `npm pack --ignore-scripts --pack-destination <artifact-dir>`
- `node scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.mjs <tarball>`
- `npm install --global --prefix <temp-prefix> <tarball>`
- `<temp-prefix>/bin/openclaw --version`
- built marker readback with `rg`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/commands/status.summary.runtime.normalization.test.ts`.
- The new test mocks both manifest normalization and plugin/runtime
  normalization and verifies status summary does not call either path for:
  - configured direct model refs
  - configured aliases
  - providerless persisted runtime models
  - providerless persisted model overrides

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Current `main` still calls manifest normalization from the status summary
  configured and persisted session model parse paths.
- In direct source benchmarking, default manifest normalization took `45.5ms`
  median per 500 `parseModelRef` calls, while the status opt-out path took
  `0.2ms`.

If no test was added, why not?

- A targeted regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly: status summary may display providerless persisted session model
values literally, for example `anthropic/opus-4.6`, instead of applying plugin
manifest aliases in this shallow status path.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The intentional status-display tradeoff for providerless persisted model aliases.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The option defaults to existing behavior for all other callers.
- Only status summary passes `allowManifestNormalization:false` into the
  persisted model resolver chain.
- Focused tests cover configured and persisted status paths.
- Built package marker readback confirms the expected status path and type
  surface are present.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review after CI reruns on the rebased head. ClawSweeper says no
contributor repair is needed; the maintainer decision is whether to accept the
shallow-status display tradeoff.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI: rerun on the rebased head after the branch update.
- Maintainers: judge whether shallow status should skip manifest normalization
  and display providerless persisted model values literally in this read-only
  status path.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper reviewed the previous head, marked proof sufficient, rated the PR
  `platinum hermit`, and said no ClawSweeper repair is needed before maintainer
  review.
- ClawSweeper's only rank-up move is maintainer acceptance of the literal-label
  status tradeoff before merge.
- The old `build-artifacts` failure was traced to upstream drift in
  `test/scripts/test-live-shard.test.ts`. Current `origin/main` includes the
  upstream fix (`f09b69a7 test: drop removed gateway live shard fixture`), and
  the rebased branch now passes that focused test locally.

target.title: fix(agents): don't fail CLI turn when the native harness owns compaction
target.body:
> **AI-assisted:** built with Codex (GPT-5.5) and Claude (Opus 4.8). Human-run real behavior proof from a self-hosted gateway is included below.

## Summary

Budget/auto-triggered compaction on a Codex-backed session calls the Codex app-server, which **owns automatic compaction** and returns a successful no-op (`{ ok: true, compacted: false, reason: "codex app-server owns automatic compaction" }`, the behavior added in #86772). The host lifecycle `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` in `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts` treated any non-compacted native outcome without `fallbackToContextEngine` as a hard failure and threw `CLI native harness compaction failed …`. That fails the agent run (`UNAVAILABLE`) and the session lane never releases — on a large thread the bot shows a "typing" indicator indefinitely and never replies.

This honors a contract the codebase **already** establishes elsewhere: the auto-reply preflight path skips compaction for Codex runtimes outright (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts`: "Its harness owns automatic compaction; OpenClaw preflight compaction is only for non-Codex embedded runtimes"). `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` was the one host path that did not honor it and threw instead.

## Fix

Surface an `ownedByNativeHarness` flag on the native outcome when the harness reports a successful no-op (`result.ok === true`), and add a caller branch **before** the throw that skips both the error and the context-engine fallback, letting the turn proceed so the harness compacts itself. Minimal and additive; no refactor. Genuine failures (`ok:false`, thrown, or `undefined` result) and the unsupported / recoverable-binding cases are unchanged.

Note on shape: I extended the existing `NativeHarnessCliCompactionOutcome` (which already uses parallel optional fields) rather than refactoring it into a discriminated union, to keep the fix small. Happy to take the union refactor in a follow-up if maintainers prefer.

## Sibling surfaces (not one-sided)

The other native-harness compaction entry points already tolerate the `ok:true` no-op, so this is the only path that needed fixing:
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts` — `shouldFallbackAfterHarnessCompaction` only fires on `ok:false`; the `ok:true` no-op is returned directly.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` — Codex runtimes skip preflight compaction entirely; otherwise only `isDeferredPreflightCompactionReason` throws, which the owned-skip reason is not.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A successful native-harness compaction no-op (`ok:true, compacted:false`, "codex app-server owns automatic compaction") must let the turn proceed instead of throwing and jamming the session lane.
- **Real environment tested:** A self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.28` gateway on Linux (Node 22.22.2) where this failure occurred in production, with the equivalent fix applied to the running build and the gateway restarted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Drove one real agent turn through the live gateway against the same over-budget Codex session that had been failing (no channel delivery):
  `openclaw agent --session-id <redacted> --message "Reply with exactly: COMPACTION_FIX_OK" --json`
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output / redacted runtime logs copied from the live gateway (same session), captured via `openclaw agent --session-id <redacted> --message "Reply with exactly: COMPACTION_FIX_OK" --json`, before vs. after the fix.

  Before (pre-fix) — the throw and resulting lane jam:
  ```
  [diagnostic] lane wait exceeded: lane=session:agent:main:discord:channel:<redacted> waitedMs=176928 queueAhead=0 activeAhead=1 activeNow=0
  [ws] ⇄ res ✗ agent errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=Error: CLI native harness compaction failed for openai-codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction
  ```

  After (post-fix) — same compaction outcome, now skipped instead of thrown; turn completes:
  ```
  [agent/embedded] skipping codex app-server compaction for non-manual trigger
  [agents/cli-compaction] CLI native harness compaction did not reduce context for openai-codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction
  COMPACTION_FIX_OK
  ```
  Agent result JSON: `stopReason: "stop"`, `executionTrace.winnerProvider: "openai-codex"`, `winnerModel: "gpt-5.5"`, `attempts[0].result: "success"`, `fallbackUsed: false`.

- **Observed result after fix:** Copied live output — the previously-jammed over-budget Codex session completed a turn and the agent returned `COMPACTION_FIX_OK` (gateway console output above). Zero `CLI native harness compaction failed` occurrences in gateway logs since the fix was deployed.
- **What was not tested:** Not re-driven through the Discord channel UI specifically — the turn was driven via the gateway `agent` command without `--deliver`; behavior is proven at the live gateway turn boundary.

## Supplemental validation

- New regression test in `cli-compaction.test.ts`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` → 24 passed. Reverting only the source change makes the new test fail with `CLI native harness compaction failed for codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction` (the exact production error).
- `oxfmt --check` clean · `oxlint` clean · `tsgo:test` (core + extensions) exit 0.
- `codex review --base origin/main` run locally: no actionable correctness issues.
- Diff: +14 src / +77 test, 0 deletions.

Complements #87158 (Codex plugin side); coordinates with #87738 (same file, missing-thread `ok:false` case). Related: #85160, #86772, #66263.

target.title: feat(opencode): add resolveDynamicModel and augmentModelCatalog hooks to Zen plugin
target.body:
## Problem

The `opencode` (Zen) provider plugin, introduced in the OpenCode provider-key split, is missing `resolveDynamicModel` and `augmentModelCatalog` runtime hooks that **every other bundled provider implements**.

Without these hooks, the agent runtime **cannot resolve `opencode/<model-id>` references** at runtime. When a model like `opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` is configured in `agents.defaults.models`, the resolution chain:

1. ❌ Checks `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — not found
2. ❌ Calls `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — **undefined** (hook not implemented)
3. ❌ Falls through to `resolveConfiguredFallbackModel` — requires explicit `models.providers["opencode"]` config entry
4. ❌ **"Unknown model" error**

Users hit this as `⚠️ Unknown model: opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` when trying to use the Zen proxy through fallback chains, requiring fragile config workarounds (manual `models.providers` entries or models.json manipulation) that are not needed for any other provider.

**Severity:** Medium — annoyance that requires manual config work for every Zen model the user wants to reference in the fallback chain.

## Solution

Add runtime hooks to the `opencode` Zen provider plugin, mirroring the pattern established by `opencode-go` at `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:

### New file: `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`

Static model catalog with known Zen-proxied models:

| Model | API | Reasoning | Context |
|-------|-----|-----------|---------|
| deepseek-v4-flash-free | openai-completions | true | 65K |
| claude-opus-4-6 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| claude-opus-4-7 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| claude-sonnet-4-6 | openai-responses | true | 200K |
| gemini-2.5-pro | openai-responses | true | 1M |

Catalog functions:
- `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()` — discoverable catalog entries
- `resolveOpencodeZenModel(modelId)` — runtime model resolution
- `normalizeOpencodeZenBaseUrl()` — base URL normalization (accepts `https://opencode.ai`, `https://opencode.ai/zen`, `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`)
- `normalizeOpencodeZenResolvedModel()` — resolved model base URL normalization

### Modified: `extensions/opencode/index.ts` (+5 hooks)

- `resolveDynamicModel` → delegates to `resolveOpencodeZenModel()`
- `augmentModelCatalog` → delegates to `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()`
- `normalizeConfig` → normalizes provider config base URLs
- `normalizeResolvedModel` → normalizes resolved model base URLs
- `normalizeTransport` → normalizes transport-level base URLs

### Resolution chain after fix

1. ✅ `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — found (catalog provides it)
2. ✅ `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — resolves at runtime
3. (Escape hatch) No config workaround needed

## Verification

### Build & Tests

- `pnpm build` — clean
- `pnpm test -- extensions/opencode` — **17/17 tests passed** (5 test files)

Test output:

```
extension-misc extensions/opencode/index.test.ts (8 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/onboard.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/provider-policy-api.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts (2 tests)
extension-misc extensions/opencode/media-understanding-provider.test.ts (3 tests)

Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  17 passed (17)
```

### Unit test coverage

- `resolveDynamicModel` resolves `deepseek-v4-flash-free` with correct api/reasoning/context metadata matching the live test
- `resolveDynamicModel` resolves `claude-opus-4-6` with `openai-responses` api
- `resolveDynamicModel` returns `undefined` for unknown model IDs (graceful fallback)
- `augmentModelCatalog` returns entries including both DeepSeek and Claude models

### Live behavior proof

Model discovered through provider catalog after patch:
```
opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free  STATUS  configured,fallback#2,alias:DeepSeekFree
```

The model resolves at runtime without requiring a `models.providers["opencode"]` entry.

Rebased on `upstream/main` — clean, no merge conflicts. Fork synced.

## Why this matters

The `opencode` Zen provider is a multi-model proxy (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek). Without `resolveDynamicModel`, every model requires a manual `models.providers` config entry — creating a poor developer experience and ongoing maintenance burden. With the hooks in place, models are automatically discoverable and resolvable, exactly like every other bundled provider.

## Change History

- **v2**: Fixed DeepSeek model metadata to match existing live test: `api: "openai-completions"`, `reasoning: true`, `contextWindow: 65_536`, `maxTokens: 8_192`. Added 4 unit tests for `resolveDynamicModel` and `augmentModelCatalog`.

target.title: feat(opencode): support separate Zen and Go API key env vars
target.body:
## Summary

Allow using OpenCode Go and OpenCode Zen with separate environment variables for each catalog, with fallback to the shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

Add provider-specific OpenCode API key env vars so `opencode` (Zen) and `opencode-go` (Go) can use different credentials instead of being forced to share the same `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode/index.ts`**: register as a standalone provider; prefer `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`**: promote `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` to primary, keep `OPENCODE_API_KEY` as fallback
- **`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json`**: add `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` as expected env var
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.ts`**: parameterize `envVar` instead of hardcoding `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; remove shared profile fan-out (each provider writes to its own profile only); scope `expectedProviders` to the calling provider
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts`**: add precedence tests for split-key ordering
- **`src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`**: add split-key resolution tests for both providers; add missing `opencode` env var mock
- **`docs/providers/opencode.md`** & **`docs/providers/opencode-go.md`**: document new env vars and precedence

## Precedence

| Usage | Provider | Prefers | Falls back to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Onboarding (wizard) | opencode (Zen) | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Onboarding (wizard) | opencode-go (Go) | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Runtime resolution | opencode (Zen) | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Runtime resolution | opencode-go (Go) | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` → `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` |

## Key design decisions

1. **No shared profile fan-out**: Provider-specific keys write only to their own profile (`opencode:default` or `opencode-go:default`). Only the (now-deprecated) shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY` path could fan out to both — but the PR removes hardcoded `profileIds` entirely, so each provider auth is self-contained.

2. **Runtime fallback preserved**: Both providers list `OPENCODE_API_KEY` in their `envVars` declaration, so runtime key resolution still works if only the shared key is set. No migration needed for existing single-key setups.

3. **Backward compatible**: Existing configs that only set `OPENCODE_API_KEY` continue to work. The new split-key paths only activate when the provider-specific env var is set.

## Verification

### Build
- `pnpm build` — clean, no errors

### Tests
- `pnpm test -- extensions/opencode extensions/opencode-go src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` — all pass

### Auth behavior
- `resolveEnvApiKey('opencode-go')` — returns `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` when set, falls back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- `resolveEnvApiKey('opencode')` — returns `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` when set, falls back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- Provider auth writes credential only to its own profile (no cross-contamination)

### Rebase
- Cleanly rebased on `upstream/main` — 2 commits, 15 files, +103/-60
- No merge conflicts

target.title: fix(messages): preserve inbound audio for message-tool TTS
target.body:
## Summary

- Carry the current inbound-audio fact into embedded OpenClaw message-tool sends.
- Reuse the shared inbound audio detector for normal final replies and message-tool TTS routing.
- Add focused regressions for embedded context propagation, message tool forwarding, and outbound TTS call shape.

Fixes #87708.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm test src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-media.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts src/agents/command/types.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

behavior: message-tool sends from an inbound audio turn preserve the inbound audio context before the message-action TTS helper decides whether `messages.tts.auto: "inbound"` should synthesize audio.

environment: local OpenClaw source checkout on Node 22 with the focused message-tool, outbound runner, embedded context, and reply-dispatch tests.

steps: ran the focused regression suite covering embedded context construction, message tool forwarding, outbound message-action TTS invocation, and the existing reply-dispatch TTS path after moving the inbound-audio detector into the shared helper.

evidence: the focused regression command passed 3 Vitest shards, the dispatch smoke command passed 1 Vitest shard, formatter passed on all touched files, and `git diff --check` passed.

observedResult: embedded runs now compute `currentInboundAudio` from the same inbound context used by normal final replies, the message tool forwards it as `inboundAudio`, and the message-action TTS helper receives `inboundAudio: true` for source audio turns.

notTested: full live Telegram voice-message plus Microsoft TTS E2E was not run locally. The focused proof covers the source-level runtime path where the inbound-audio fact was dropped before synthesis.
